  ŰĆÖý×ĹýŁ┤ ŕ░łŰŁ╝ýžÇýžÇ ýĽŐŰĆäŰíŁ ýáĽŰŽČ.
- **Async AgenticLoop observability hooks.** Usage tracking now has an async
  path so `AgenticLoop.arun()` awaits cost warning/limit hooks. Settings-drift
  model switches also use an async update path in `arun()`, while the public
  sync `update_model()` remains available for compatibility callers.
- **AgenticLoop observability hook asyncÝÖö.** usage tracking ýŚÉ async ŕ▓ŻŰíťŰą╝
  ýÂöŕ░ÇÝĽ┤ `AgenticLoop.arun()` ýŁ┤ cost warning/limit hook ýŁä await. settings
  drift Űíť Ű░ťýâŁÝĽśŰŐö model switch ŰĆä `arun()` ýĽłýŚÉýäťŰŐö async update path Űą╝
  ýéČýÜęÝĽśŰę░, public sync `update_model()` ýŁÇ compatibility caller Űą╝ ýťäÝĽ┤ ýťáýžÇ.
- **IPC prompt role split.** The thin client now remains transport/rendering
  only, while the daemon admits prompt work through `LaneQueue.acquire_all_async()`
  and awaits `AgenticLoop.arun()`. The legacy sync prompt runner remains as a
  compatibility fallback, but IPC daemon prompt execution no longer calls
  `AgenticLoop.run()` or sync `LaneQueue.acquire_all()`.
- **IPC prompt ýŚşÝĽá ŰÂäŰŽČ.** thin client ŰŐö transport/rendering ýŚşÝĽáŰžî ýťáýžÇÝĽśŕ│á,
  daemon ýŁ┤ `LaneQueue.acquire_all_async()` Űíť prompt work Űą╝ admission ÝĽť ŰĺĄ
  `AgenticLoop.arun()` ýŁä await. legacy sync prompt runner ŰŐö compatibility
  fallback ýť╝Űíť ŰéĘŕŞ░ýžÇŰžî, IPC daemon prompt ýőĄÝľëýŁÇ ŰŹö ýŁ┤ýâü
  `AgenticLoop.run()` ýŁ┤Űéś sync `LaneQueue.acquire_all()` ýŁä ÝśŞýÂťÝĽśýžÇ ýĽŐýŁî.
- **Context-local IPC UI state.** Console routing, IPC writer binding, pipeline
  IP context, and session meters now use contextvar-backed local storage while
  preserving the existing `threading.local`-style attribute API. This lets
  concurrent async IPC prompts keep stream events and session meters isolated
  without serializing the prompt body behind a UI lock.
- **IPC UI state context-local ýáäÝÖś.** console routing, IPC writer binding,
  pipeline IP context, session meter Űą╝ ŕŞ░ýí┤ `threading.local` ýŐĄÝâÇýŁ╝ attribute
  API ŰŐö ýťáýžÇÝĽť ý▒ä contextvar-backed local storage Űíť ýáäÝÖś. ŰĆÖýőť async IPC
  prompt ŕ░Ç UI lock ýŚćýŁ┤ŰĆä stream event ýÖÇ session meter Űą╝ ýäťŰíť ŕ▓ęŰŽČ.
- **Async migration quality gate.** Added an explicit verification pass for
  code-quality gaps, missing async hand-offs, and duplication-prone sync
  bridges. The pass fixed context overflow/offload hook calls to use async hook
  APIs and removed an event-loop-bound approval lock from the long-lived
  approval workflow.
- **Async migration Ýĺłýžł ŕ▓îýŁ┤ÝŐŞ ýÂöŕ░Ç.** code-quality gap / ŰłäŰŁŻŰÉť async hand-off /
  ýĄĹŰ│Á ýťäÝŚś sync bridge Űą╝ ÝÖĽýŁŞÝĽśŰŐö ŕ▓ÇýŽŁ ýáłý░ĘŰą╝ ŕ│äÝÜŹ ŰČŞýäťýŚÉ ýÂöŕ░Ç. ÝĽ┤Űő╣
  ŕ▓ÇýŽŁýť╝Űíť context overflow/offload hook ÝśŞýÂťýŁä async hook API Űíť ýáĽŰŽČÝĽśŕ│á,
  ý×ąýłśŰ¬ů approval workflow ýŚÉ ýáÇý×ąŰÉśŰŹś event-loop-bound approval lock ýŁä ýáťŕ▒░.
- **AgenticLoop sync facade removal.** `AgenticLoop.run()` has been removed
  as part of the breaking async migration. Production internal CLI, gateway,
  scheduler, worker, skill, and legacy IPC prompt paths bridge directly to
  `AgenticLoop.arun()`, and source guards prevent reintroducing the sync

- **AgenticLoop sync facade ýáťŕ▒░.** breaking async migration ýŁś ýŁ╝ŰÂÇŰíť

  bridge ÝĽśŰę░, source guard Űíť sync facade ý×ČŰĆäý×ůýŁä ý░ĘŰőĘ.
- **Async MCP adapter helper slice.** Calendar, notification, and signal MCP
  helper layers now route through `MCPServerManager.acall_tool()` or client
  `acall_tool()`. Public MCP `call_tool()` facades were removed from manager
  and client surfaces.
- **MCP adapter helper 1ý░Ę asyncÝÖö.** Calendar / notification / signal MCP
  helper ŕ│äýŞÁýŚÉ `MCPServerManager.acall_tool()` ŰśÉŰŐö client `acall_tool()`
  ŕ▓ŻŰíťŰą╝ ýáüýÜę. manager / client ÝĹťŰę┤ýŁś public MCP `call_tool()` facade ŰŐö
  ýáťŕ▒░.
- **Async tool-object dispatch slice.** `ToolRegistry.aexecute()` now prefers
  tool-local `aexecute()` implementations and rejects sync-only registry
  execution. Calendar list/create and notification CLI handlers now call
  async tool-object paths so their MCP-backed adapters avoid sync `call_tool()`
  in the canonical async runtime.

  `aexecute()` Űą╝ ÝĽäýłś ŕ▓ŻŰíťŰíť ýéČýÜęÝĽśŕ│á sync-only registry ýőĄÝľëýŁÇ ŕ▒░ŰÂÇ.
  Calendar list/create ýÖÇ notification CLI handler ŰŐö ýŁ┤ýáť async tool-object

  `call_tool()` ýŁä ýÜ░ÝÜî.
- **Async debt reduction slice.** Adaptive error recovery now awaits
  `ErrorRecoveryStrategy.arecover()` and retries through `ToolExecutor.aexecute()`.
  Runtime/container tool injection no longer calls `ToolRegistry.execute()`
  directly; async-native nodes can read `get_async_tool_executor()`. Plugin
  signal tools now provide `aexecute()` methods backed by
  `try_mcp_signal_async()`.
- **Async ŰÂÇý▒ä ýÂĽýćî 1ý░Ę.** adaptive error recovery ŕ░Ç

  node ŰŐö `get_async_tool_executor()` Űą╝ ýéČýÜęÝĽá ýłś ý×łýŁî. Plugin signal tool ýŁÇ

- **Built-in tool async surface completion.** Built-in file, document, web,
  jobs, memory, profile, data, report/export, calendar-scheduler, computer-use,
  and game-IP fixture/analysis tools now expose tool-local `aexecute()` methods.
  `ToolRegistry.aexecute()` no longer falls back to sync-only tool execution.

  profile / data / report-export / calendar-scheduler / computer-use /

- **Async provider tool-use boundary.** `LLMClientPort` now includes
  `agenerate_with_tools()`, and the router exposes `call_llm_with_tools_async()`.
  The first implementation isolates the existing provider tool-use loops behind

- **Provider tool-use async boundary ýÂöŕ░Ç.** `LLMClientPort` ýŚÉ

  await-native tool dispatch ýáäÝÖśýŁä ýĄÇŰ╣ä.
- **Provider tool-use internals async migration.** `call_llm_with_tools_async()`

  sync tool-callable ŕ▓Żŕ│äŰĆä provider sync Űé┤ŰÂÇ ŕÁČÝśä ŰîÇýőá

- **Async tool executor injection only.** Runtime tool state injection no

### Infrastructure

- **CI Phase 1 ÔÇö path-filter + pytest-xdist + draft skip.** Hermes ýÖÇ
  OpenClaw frontier ÝîĘÝä┤ ý░ĘýÜę (frontier survey 2026-05-17). `dorny/paths-filter@v3`
  Űíť Ű│Çŕ▓ŻŰÉť ŕ▓ŻŰíťŰą╝ ŕ▓ÇýÂťÝĽśýŚČ docs-only/blog-only PR ýŁÇ lint/type/test/security
  step ýŁä ýŽëýőť short-circuit (job ý×Éý▓┤ŰŐö success ŰžłÝé╣ŰÉśŰĆäŰíŁ step-level `if:`
  ýéČýÜę ÔÇö branch protection required-status-check ÝśŞÝÖś). ýŻöŰôť Ű│Çŕ▓Ż PR ýŁÇ
  `pytest -n auto` Űíť xdist Ű│ĹŰáČ ýőĄÝľë (~3ŰÂä Ôćĺ ~1ŰÂä ýśłýâü). `types:
  [opened, reopened, synchronize, ready_for_review]` Űíť draft PR ýŁÇ trigger
  ý×Éý▓┤ ý░ĘŰőĘ. `pytest-xdist>=3.6.0` ýŁä `[dependency-groups.dev]` ýŚÉ ýÂöŕ░Ç.

- **CI Phase 1 ÔÇö path-filter + pytest-xdist + draft skip.** Adopted
  patterns from Hermes and OpenClaw frontier survey (2026-05-17).
  `dorny/paths-filter@v3` detects changed paths; docs-only / blog-only
  PRs short-circuit the lint/type/test/security steps via step-level
  `if:` (jobs still report success so branch-protection required-status
  checks pass). Code-touching PRs run `pytest -n auto` (xdist) ÔÇö expect
  ~3min Ôćĺ ~1min. Draft PRs no longer trigger CI thanks to the new
  `types: [..., ready_for_review]` filter. `pytest-xdist>=3.6.0` added
  to the dev dependency group.

## [0.99.3] ÔÇö 2026-05-17

### Fixed

- **`login_anthropic()` ÔÇö token exchange body ÝśĽýőŁ JSON Ű│ÁŕĚÇ + `anthropic-beta`
  ÝŚĄŰŹö ýáťŕ▒░.** v0.99.2 ŕ░Ç `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` Űíť Ű│Çŕ▓ŻÝĽśŕ│á
  `anthropic-beta: oauth-2025-04-20` Űą╝ ýÂöŕ░ÇÝľłýť╝Űéś ýéČýÜęý×É ýőťŰĆä ŕ▓░ŕ│╝ ýŚČýáäÝ×ł
  `invalid_request`. ../openclaw + ../claude-code ŕĚŞŰŁ╝ýÜ┤Űöę + Claude Code
  native binary ýŁś `h6.post(TOKEN_URL, z, {headers:{"Content-Type":
  "application/json"}, timeout:30000})` ÝśŞýÂť ý×Éý▓┤Űą╝ ýÂöýÂťÝĽśýŚČ ground truth
  ÝÖĽýŁŞ:  Content-Type ýŁÇ JSON, beta ÝŚĄŰŹöŰŐö token endpoint ýŚÉ Ű│┤Űé┤ýžÇ ýĽŐýŁî.
  v0.99.0/0.99.1 ýŁś JSON ÝîĘÝä┤ ý×Éý▓┤ŰŐö Űž×ýĽśýť╝Űéś host (`api.anthropic.com`)
  ŕ░Ç ÝőÇŰáŞŰŹś ŕ▓â ÔÇö v0.99.2 ŕ░Ç host fix ýÖÇ ÝĽĘŕ╗ś Content-Type ŕ╣îýžÇ ýŁśýőČÝĽ┤ýäť
  ý×śŰ¬╗ŰÉť Ű░ęÝľąýť╝Űíť Ű░öŕż╝ ýůł. ŕ│ÁýőŁ docs / community gist ýŁś "form-urlencoded"
  ýáĽŰ│┤ŕ░Ç ýáĽÝÖĽÝĽśýžÇ ýĽŐŰőĄŰŐö ŕ▓░Űíá.

- **`login_anthropic()` ÔÇö reverted token exchange body to JSON + dropped
  `anthropic-beta` header.** Even after the v0.99.2 host fix the user
  still saw `invalid_request`. Grounding against the openclaw + claude-code
  source trees plus extracting the actual `h6.post` call site from
  claude.exe (`{"Content-Type":"application/json"}`, no beta header)
  confirmed the binary is the ground truth: JSON-only on the token
  endpoint, no anthropic-beta. Public docs and community gists are
  incorrect on this point.

## [0.99.2] ÔÇö 2026-05-17

### Fixed

- **`login_anthropic()` ÔÇö token endpoint host + Content-Type + timeout ýáĽýáĽ.**
  v0.99.1 manual-paste fix ÝŤäýŚÉŰĆä `/login anthropic` ŕ░Ç `invalid_request`
  Űíť ŕ▒░ýáł. ýéČýÜęý×É ýŻśýćö ýőáÝśŞ + Claude Code native binary ýŁś prod env ŕ░Łý▓┤
  `K3q` ýáäý▓┤ ýÂöýÂť + ŕ│ÁýőŁ ŰČŞýäť cross-check Űíť 3 ŕ░ÇýžÇ root cause ÝÖĽýáĽ:
  ÔĹá token endpoint host ŕ░Ç `https://platform.claude.com/v1/oauth/token`
  (`api.anthropic.com` ýŁÇ inference API ýáäýÜę); ÔĹí Content-Type ýŁÇ
  `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` Űžî ÝŚłýÜę ÔÇö `application/json` ýť╝Űíť
  Ű│┤Űé┤Űę┤ ýŁĹŰőÁ ýžÇýŚ░/timeout ŕ░ÇŰŐą; ÔĹó ýŁĹŰőÁ ýőťŕ░ä 40-60s Ű│┤ŕ│áŕ░Ç ý×łýľ┤ client
  timeout ýŁä 15s Ôćĺ 60s Űíť ýÖäÝÖö. `_ANTHROPIC_TOKEN_URL` ýáĽýáĽ + `json=` Ôćĺ
  `data=` body ÝśĽýőŁ Ű│Çŕ▓Ż + httpx timeout 60s.

- **`login_anthropic()` ÔÇö corrected token endpoint host, Content-Type, and
  timeout.** Post-v0.99.1 the manual-paste flow still failed with
  `invalid_request`. Three root causes pinned down via the prod env object
  `K3q` extracted from Claude Code's native binary plus cross-check with
  official docs: ÔĹá OAuth audience lives on
  `platform.claude.com/v1/oauth/token` (`api.anthropic.com` only serves
  the inference API); ÔĹí endpoint accepts only
  `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` ÔÇö `application/json` causes hang/
  timeout; ÔĹó reported 40-60s response time under load, so client timeout
  relaxed from 15s to 60s. Fixed `_ANTHROPIC_TOKEN_URL`, switched httpx
  body from `json=` to `data=`, set Timeout(60).

## [0.99.1] ÔÇö 2026-05-17

### Fixed

- **`login_anthropic()` ÔÇö loopback redirect_uri Ôćĺ manual-paste ÝîĘÝä┤ ŕÁÉý▓┤.**
  v0.99.0 ýŚÉýäť ŰĆäý×ůŰÉť loopback HTTP server (`http://localhost:54123/callback`)
  ŰŐö OAuth client `9d1c250a-ÔÇŽ` ýŚÉ Űô▒ŰíŁŰÉť redirect URI ŕ░Ç ýĽäŰőłŰŁ╝ authorize
  ŰőĘŕ│äýŚÉýäť ŕ▒░ýáłŰÉÉŰőĄ (ýéČýÜęý×É Ű│┤ŕ│á ÔÇö ŰĹÉ Ű▓ł ýőťŰĆä Ű¬ĘŰĹÉ ~50ý┤ł ŰžîýŚÉ ýőĄÝîĘ, auth.toml
  Ű»ŞŰ│Çŕ▓Ż). Claude Code native binary ýŁś strings ŰÂäýäŁýť╝Űíť ýáĽŰőÁ redirect URI
  ŕ░Ç `https://platform.claude.com/oauth/code/callback` ý×äýŁä ÝÖĽýŁŞ ÔÇö ýäťŰ▓ä ýŞí
  callback ÝÄśýŁ┤ýžÇŕ░Ç ýéČýÜęý×ÉýŚÉŕ▓î `code#state` ÝśĽýőŁýŁä ÝĹťýőťÝĽśŰę┤ ýéČýÜęý×Éŕ░Ç CLI
  Űíť paste ÝĽśŰŐö manual-paste ÝîĘÝä┤. `_run_anthropic_pkce_flow` Űą╝ 1:1
  Ű»ŞŰčČŰíť ý×Čý×Ĺýä▒: HTTPServer / `_pick_free_port` / ýŻťŰ░▒ ÝĽŞŰôĄŰčČ ýáťŕ▒░, paste
  Ýîîýäť (`_parse_pasted_code` ÔÇö URL/fragment/bare code 3 ÝśĽýőŁ ýłśýÜę) ŰĆäý×ů,
  scope ýŚÉ `user:sessions:claude_code` ýÂöŕ░Ç (binary hint ýáĽÝĽę). Tier 3
  impersonation ýáĽý▒ůýŁÇ ŕĚŞŰîÇŰíť.

- **`login_anthropic()` ÔÇö switched loopback redirect to manual-paste.**
  v0.99.0's loopback HTTP server (`http://localhost:54123/callback`) was
  rejected at the authorize step because the OAuth client `9d1c250a-ÔÇŽ`
  is registered with only one redirect URI:
  `https://platform.claude.com/oauth/code/callback` (server-hosted).
  Confirmed by `strings(8)` analysis of Claude Code's native binary +
  user reports of two consecutive ~50s failures with no auth.toml write.
  `_run_anthropic_pkce_flow` rewritten as a 1:1 mirror of Claude Code's
  manual-paste flow: the loopback HTTP server, port picker, and request
  handler are removed; a paste parser (`_parse_pasted_code`) accepts the
  full callback URL, the `code#state` fragment, or the bare code; scope
  set extended with `user:sessions:claude_code` per the binary's hint
  string. Tier 3 impersonation posture unchanged.

## [0.99.0] ÔÇö 2026-05-17

### Added

- **`login_anthropic()` ÔÇö owned-Anthropic OAuth PKCE flow (claude CLI
  ýŁśýí┤ýä▒ ýáťŕ▒░).** `/login anthropic` ŕ░Ç ŰŹö ýŁ┤ýâü `claude /login`
  subprocess Űą╝ ÝśŞýÂťÝĽśýžÇ ýĽŐŕ│á GEODE ŕ░Ç ýžüýáĹ PKCE redirect flow ýłśÝľë
  ÔÇö loopback callback server (Ű×ťŰŹĄ free port 54123-54199), PKCE
  code_verifier/challenge ýâŁýä▒, `https://platform.claude.com/oauth/
  authorize` browser open, `https://api.anthropic.com/v1/oauth/token`
  ÝćáÝü░ ŕÁÉÝÖś, `~/.geode/auth.toml` ýŁś `providers.anthropic` section ýŚÉ
  ýáÇý×ą. multi-candidate client_id ýőťŰĆä path (`9d1c250a-...` Űô▒ reverse-
  engineered) + first-success-wins. macOS/Linux/Windows Ű¬ĘŰĹÉ ŰĆÖý×Ĺ.
  `read_geode_anthropic_credentials` ÝŚČÝŹ╝ŕ░Ç `read_geode_openai_
  credentials` ýÖÇ ŰĆÖýŁ╝ shape ýť╝Űíť Ű░śÝÖś. `claude_code_provider.
  resolve_claude_oauth_token` / `get_claude_oauth_metadata` ŕ░Ç auth.
  toml ýÜ░ýäá read + macOS keychain backwards-compat fallback. ToS Tier
  3 (impersonation) ÔÇö `claude_code_provider` ýŁś module docstring ýŁś
  policy notice ŕ░Ç SOT. failure ýőť graceful fallback (`ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`
  ŕÂîý×ą message).
- **`login_anthropic()` ÔÇö owned-Anthropic OAuth PKCE flow (drops
  `claude` CLI dependency).** `/login anthropic` no longer spawns
  `claude /login`; GEODE drives the PKCE redirect flow itself ÔÇö
  loopback callback (free port 54123-54199), PKCE
  `code_verifier`/`challenge` pair, browser open against
  `platform.claude.com/oauth/authorize`, token exchange at
  `api.anthropic.com/v1/oauth/token`, persist into
  `~/.geode/auth.toml` `providers.anthropic`. Multi-candidate
  `client_id` loop (`9d1c250a-...` first) with first-success-wins.
  Cross-platform (macOS / Linux / Windows). `read_geode_anthropic_
  credentials` mirrors the OpenAI helper shape.
  `claude_code_provider.resolve_claude_oauth_token` and
  `get_claude_oauth_metadata` now prefer the `auth.toml` source with
  the macOS keychain kept as a backwards-compat fallback. ToS Tier 3
  (impersonation) per `claude_code_provider` module docstring;
  failure surfaces an `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` fallback hint.


- **`docs/architecture/provider-login.md` ÔÇö provider login flow SOT.**
  OpenAI (device-code) ýÖÇ Anthropic (PKCE redirect) ýŁś OAuth flow ýŁś
  ýáĽÝĽę spec ýőáŕĚť. owned-credential ÝîĘÝä┤ (auth.toml SOT + GEODE ŕ░Ç ýžüýáĹ
  OAuth client) ýŁś architecture + 5-mismatch ýáĽÝĽę plan + ToS Tier
  spectrum (0-4) ýáĽýŁś. PR C3 (owned-Anthropic PKCE ŕÁČÝśä) ýŁś reference
  SOT.
- **`docs/architecture/provider-login.md` ÔÇö provider login flow SOT.**
  Architecture spec for the two-provider OAuth picture: OpenAI's
  device-code flow plus Anthropic's PKCE redirect flow share an
  owned-credential pattern (auth.toml as SOT, GEODE acts as OAuth
  client directly). Covers the 5 mismatch points the upcoming PR C3
  will close and pins down the ToS Tier (0-4) spectrum the choice
  sits in.

### Removed

- **`/auth` ýŐČŰ×śýőť Ű¬ůŰá╣ ýÖäýáä ýáťŕ▒░ + `/login source` ýőáýäĄ.** `/auth` ýŁś ý×öýí┤
  surface (`add` / `remove` / `set <provider> <source>`) ŕ░Ç Ű¬ĘŰĹÉ `/login`
  ýť╝Űíť ÝŁíýłś. `/login source <provider> <type>` ýőáŕĚť ÔÇö ŕŞ░ýí┤ `/auth set` ýŁś
  credential source picker. `routing.py` ýŁś `/auth` CommandSpec, `dispatcher.py`
  ýŁś cmd_auth dispatch, `core/cli/__init__.py` ýŁś TTY_LOCAL_COMMANDS ýŁś
  `/auth` ŰęĄŰ▓ä, `_state.py` ýŁś `COMMAND_MAP` ýŁś `/auth` entry + help line,
  `commands/__init__.py` ýŁś export, `core/cli/commands/auth.py` ÝîîýŁ╝ ý×Éý▓┤
  Ű¬ĘŰĹÉ ýáťŕ▒░. `manage_auth` LLM tool ýŁÇ backwards-compat adapter Űíť ýťáýžÇ
  ÔÇö ÝśŞýÂť ýőť `manage_login` Űíť forward (legacy prompts ÝśŞÝÖś). Plan vs
  Profile ŰÂäŰŽČ ýŁś historical ŕĚ╝ŕ▒░ (`PlanRegistry` vs `ProfileStore`) ŰŐö
  ýťáýžÇŰÉśŰÉś, ýéČýÜęý×É ýžäý×ůýáÉýŁÇ `/login` ŰőĘýŁ╝ SOT.
- **`/auth` slash command fully removed + `/login source` introduced.**
  The remaining `/auth` surface (`add` / `remove` / `set <provider>
  <source>`) was folded into `/login`. The new `/login source <provider>
  <type>` is the migrated credential-source picker (was `/auth set`,
  PR #1203). Removed: `routing.py` entry, `dispatcher.py` dispatch +
  import, `_TTY_LOCAL_COMMANDS` membership, `_state.py` `COMMAND_MAP` +
  help line, `commands/__init__.py` exports, and the
  `core/cli/commands/auth.py` source file itself. The `manage_auth`
  LLM tool is kept as a backwards-compat adapter that forwards to
  `manage_login` so legacy prompts still work. The underlying Plan vs
  Profile split (`PlanRegistry` vs `ProfileStore`) is unchanged ÔÇö only
  the user-facing entry point is unified.

## [0.98.0] ÔÇö 2026-05-17

### Changed

- **`/login <provider>` ÔÇö provider Űžî parameter Űíť Ű░ŤŰŐö OAuth picker
  + `/auth login` ýáťŕ▒░.** ŕŞ░ýí┤ `/login oauth <provider>` ýŁś 2-ŰőĘýľ┤ ÝśĽÝâťŕ░Ç
  `/login openai` / `/login anthropic` (alias: `codex`, `chatgpt`,
  `claude`, `claude-code`) ýŁś ŰőĘýŁ╝ ÝćáÝü░ ýžäý×ůýť╝Űíť ŰőĘýłťÝÖö. provider name
  Űžîýť╝Űíť OAuth flow ŕ░Ç ýŽëýőť ŰĆÖý×Ĺ ÔÇö picker surface ŕ░Ç `/model` ýŁś mirror.
  ýĄĹŰ│Á ýžäý×ůýáÉýŁ┤ŰŹś `/auth login` (status display + browser login) ýŁś UI +
  Ű░▒ýŚöŰôť ŰĹÉ helper (`_auth_login_status`, `_sync_oauth_profile_after_login`)
  Ű¬ĘŰĹÉ ýáťŕ▒░. `/auth` ŰŐö profile management Űžî (`add` / `remove` /
  `set <provider> <source>`). Anthropic OAuth path ŕ░Ç ýâłŰíť `_login_oauth`
  ýĽłýŚÉ ýÂöŕ░ÇŰÉĘ ÔÇö local `claude /login` subprocess ÝśŞýÂť ÝŤä macOS keychain
  ýŁś token ýŁä `ProfileStore` ýŚÉ sync. test 41 pass.
- **`/login <provider>` ÔÇö provider-only OAuth picker, `/auth login`
  removed.** The legacy `/login oauth <provider>` two-word form is now
  `/login openai` / `/login anthropic` (aliases: `codex`, `chatgpt`,
  `claude`, `claude-code`) ÔÇö a single provider token runs the OAuth
  flow directly, mirroring the `/model` picker surface. The redundant
  `/auth login` entry point (status display + browser handoff) and
  its `_auth_login_status` / `_sync_oauth_profile_after_login`
  helpers were removed from both UI and backend. `/auth` now hosts
  only profile management (`add` / `remove` / `set <provider>
  <source>`). The Anthropic OAuth path is now folded into
  `_login_oauth` ÔÇö it spawns `claude /login` and then syncs the
  resulting keychain credential into `ProfileStore`. 41 tests pass.

- **`/login <provider>` canonical OAuth entry point.** `/login openai`
  now runs the Codex Plus device-code flow directly, while
  `/login anthropic` delegates to the local Claude Code login flow and
  syncs the resulting keychain credential into `ProfileStore`. The old
  `/login oauth <provider>` spelling is no longer advertised by help,
  onboarding, or tool schema.
- **`/login <provider>`Űą╝ OAuth ŰőĘýŁ╝ ýžäý×ůýáÉýť╝Űíť ýáĽŰŽČ.** `/login openai`ŰŐö
  Codex Plus device-code flowŰą╝ ýžüýáĹ ýőĄÝľëÝĽśŕ│á, `/login anthropic`ýŁÇ Űíťý╗Č
  Claude Code login flowýŚÉ ýťäý×äÝĽť ŰĺĄ keychain credentialýŁä `ProfileStore`
  Űíť ŰĆÖŕŞ░ÝÖöÝĽęŰőłŰőĄ. ŕŞ░ýí┤ `/login oauth <provider>` ÝśĽÝâťŰŐö help, onboarding,
  tool schemaýŚÉýäť ŰŹö ýŁ┤ýâü ŰůŞýÂťÝĽśýžÇ ýĽŐýŐÁŰőłŰőĄ.

### Removed

- **Legacy `/auth login` UI/backend path.** `/auth` now remains only as
  profile management (`add`, `remove`, `set`); OAuth setup lives under
  `/login <provider>`. The legacy auth-login status/sync helpers were
  removed from the command package export surface.
- **Űáłŕ▒░ýőť `/auth login` UI/backend ŕ▓ŻŰíť ýáťŕ▒░.** `/auth`ŰŐö profile ŕ┤ÇŰŽČ
  (`add`, `remove`, `set`)Űžî Űő┤Űő╣ÝĽśŕ│á OAuth ýäĄýáĽýŁÇ `/login <provider>`ŕ░Ç
  Űő┤Űő╣ÝĽęŰőłŰőĄ. ŕŞ░ýí┤ auth-login status/sync helperŰĆä command package export
  surfaceýŚÉýäť ýáťŕ▒░ÝľłýŐÁŰőłŰőĄ.

  ýŁś `ClaudeCodeJudgeAPI` (subprocess judge-only, ~400 LOC) ŕ░Ç
  `ClaudeOAuthAPI` (stock `AnthropicAPI` subclass, ~80 LOC) Űíť ŕÁÉý▓┤.
  macOS keychain entry `Claude Code-credentials` ýŁś OAuth access token
  ýŁä ýÂöýÂťÝĽ┤ `api.anthropic.com/v1/messages` ýŁś `x-api-key` ÝŚĄŰŹöŰíť
  ýéČýÜę ÔÇö auditor / judge / target 3 role Ű¬ĘŰĹÉ ý×ÉŰĆÖ ýžÇýŤÉ
  (multi-turn + native tool calling). ŕŞ░ýí┤ judge-only ýáťýĽŻ ÝĽ┤ýćî. ýőáŕĚť
  ÝŚČÝŹ╝ `resolve_claude_oauth_token` / `get_claude_oauth_metadata` /
  `is_claude_oauth_available` ŕ░Ç picker UI (ÝŤäýćŹ PR B `/auth`) ýŁś
  source detection ýŚÉ ýéČýÜęŰÉĘ. ŕÁČŰĆů plan / rate-limit tier ŰŐö keychain
  blob ýŚÉýäť verbatim ýÂöýÂť ÔÇö ýŻöŰôťŰ▓áýŁ┤ýŐĄýŚÉ plan enumeration hardcode
  ýŚćýŁî. ToS spirit ŕ▓Żŕ│á (Consumer ToS ┬ž3 ýŁś narrow reading) Űą╝ ý▓ź
  ÝÖťýä▒ ýőť WARNING ŰíťŕĚŞ.
- **`claude-code` provider: subprocess CLI Ôćĺ Anthropic API direct via
  OAuth subscription token.** Replaced the subprocess-based judge-only
  adapter with a stock `AnthropicAPI` subclass that resolves the OAuth
  access token from the local `claude` CLI's macOS keychain entry and
  routes calls through `api.anthropic.com/v1/messages`. All Petri
  roles (auditor / judge / target) now work out of the box thanks to
  inspect_ai's native multi-turn + tool-call pipeline. New helpers
  `resolve_claude_oauth_token` / `get_claude_oauth_metadata` /
  `is_claude_oauth_available` expose the keychain state so the
  upcoming `/auth` picker can label the OAuth source with the actual
  subscription plan + rate-limit tier instead of a hardcoded string.
  A one-time WARNING log notes that this path is not part of
  Anthropic's documented public OAuth client surface (Consumer ToS ┬ž3
  spirit).

## [0.96.0] ÔÇö 2026-05-16

### Added

- **CLI thinking collapse + Ctrl+O toggle.** CLI reasoning-summary lines now
  collapse at `thinking_end` into a single muted `ÔťŽ Thought for ÔÇŽ ┬Ě N items`
  header, with the full reasoning history buffered for expansion. During an
  active prompt execution, `Ctrl+O` toggles live thinking between expanded
  streaming lines and a compact still-running header; non-TTY output keeps the
  previous line-by-line behavior.
- **CLI thinking collapse + Ctrl+O toggle.** CLI reasoning summary ŰŁ╝ýŁŞýŁ┤
  `thinking_end` ýŚÉýäť ŰőĘýŁ╝ muted `ÔťŽ Thought for ÔÇŽ ┬Ě N items` header Űíť
  ýáĹÝ×łŕ│á, ýáäý▓┤ reasoning history ŰŐö ŰőĄýőť ÝÄ╝ý╣á ýłś ý×łŰĆäŰíŁ Űé┤ŰÂÇ buffer ýŚÉ
  Ű│┤ŕ┤ÇŰÉęŰőłŰőĄ. Prompt ýőĄÝľë ýĄĹýŚÉŰŐö `Ctrl+O` Űíť live thinking ýŁä streaming
  line view ýÖÇ compact still-running header ýéČýŁ┤ýŚÉýäť ýáäÝÖśÝĽá ýłś ý×łýť╝Űę░,
  non-TTY ýÂťŰáąýŁÇ ŕŞ░ýí┤ line-by-line ŰĆÖý×ĹýŁä ýťáýžÇÝĽęŰőłŰőĄ.

## [0.95.5] ÔÇö 2026-05-16

### Fixed

- **CLI LaTeX digit-base superscripts + grouped scripts.** delimiter-less
  ýłśýőŁ detector ŕ░Ç `10^2`, `10^-3`, `10^(R_j - R_i)` ý▓śŰč╝ ýłźý×É base Űą╝
  ŕ░Çýžä superscript ÝĹťÝśäýŁä inline math Űíť ýŐ╣ŕ▓ęÝĽęŰőłŰőĄ. `^(...)` /
  `^{...}` Űé┤ŰÂÇýŁś nested `_j` ŰŐö Ű░öŕ╣ą superscript Ű░ęÝľąýŁä Űö░ŰŁ╝ `╩▓` Űíť
  Ű│ÇÝÖśŰÉśýľ┤ `10ÔüŻß┤┐╩▓Ôü╗ß┤┐Ôü▒Ôüż` / `10ß┤┐╩▓Ôü╗ß┤┐Ôü▒` Űíť Ű│┤ýŁ┤Űę░, braced superscript ýŁś
  Ű│ÁÝĽę payload ýŚÉ bracket fallback ýŁ┤ ý×śŰ¬╗ ýáüýÜęŰÉśýľ┤ `10[...]` Űíť ŕ╣ĘýžÇŰŐö
  ÝÜîŕĚÇŰą╝ ŰžëýĽśýŐÁŰőłŰőĄ. `1_000`, `snake_case`, path false positive ŰŐö ŕ│äýćŹ
  text Űíť ŰéĘýŐÁŰőłŰőĄ.
- **CLI LaTeX digit-base superscripts + grouped scripts.** The
  delimiter-less math detector now promotes digit-base superscripts such
  as `10^2`, `10^-3`, and `10^(R_j - R_i)` to inline math. Nested `_j`
  markers inside `^(...)` / `^{...}` inherit the outer superscript
  direction, rendering as `10ÔüŻß┤┐╩▓Ôü╗ß┤┐Ôü▒Ôüż` / `10ß┤┐╩▓Ôü╗ß┤┐Ôü▒`, and complex braced
  superscripts no longer hit the broken `10[...]` bracket fallback.
  False positives such as `1_000`, `snake_case`, and paths stay as text.

## [0.95.4] ÔÇö 2026-05-16

### Added

- **autoresearch cross-axis regression gate.** `compute_fitness` ŕ░Ç
  ýâł ýŁŞý×É `baseline: FitnessBaseline | None = None` ýŁä Ű░ŤýĽä multi-axis
  monotone ŕ▓ÇýéČŰą╝ ýłśÝľëÝĽęŰőłŰőĄ. critical axis (predictive, robustness) ŕ░Ç
  `baseline - stderr - margin` ýĽäŰ×śŰíť ŰľĘýľ┤ýžÇŰę┤ fitness=0.0 ýť╝Űíť strict
  reject; auxiliary axis (logic, diversity, stability) ýŁś ÝÜîŕĚÇŰŐö
  `╬╗ ├Ś delta┬▓` (default ╬╗=0.5) squared penalty Űíť weighted sum ýŚÉýäť
  ý░Ęŕ░É. `state/baseline.json` ýť╝Űíť ýžüýáä promote audit ýŁś axes /
  axes_stderr Űą╝ Ű│┤ŕ┤ÇÝĽśŕ│á `train.py` ýőťý×Ĺ ýőť ý×ÉŰĆÖ ŰíťŰôť. `--no-baseline`
  flag Űíť gate Ű¬ůýőť Ű╣äÝÖťýä▒ ŕ░ÇŰŐą. ŕŞ░ýí┤ single-axis fitness aggregate ŕ░Ç
  axis ŕ░ä trade-off Űą╝ ŕ░ÉýÂ░ safety axis ýŁś ÝÜîŕĚÇŰą╝ calibration ŕ░ťýäáŕ│╝
  ŕÁÉÝÖśÝĽśŰŹś Goodhart ŕ▓ŻŰíťŰą╝ ý░ĘŰőĘ.
- **autoresearch cross-axis regression gate.** `compute_fitness` accepts
  a new `baseline: FitnessBaseline | None = None` argument that enforces
  per-axis monotone progress. Critical axes (predictive, robustness)
  trigger a strict reject (fitness = 0.0) when the new score falls below
  `baseline - stderr - margin`; auxiliary axes (logic, diversity,
  stability) absorb regressions as a squared penalty
  (`╬╗ ├Ś delta┬▓`, default ╬╗=0.5). `state/baseline.json` carries the
  parent promote's `axes` + `axes_stderr` between runs and `train.py`
  loads it automatically (use `--no-baseline` to skip). Closes a
  Goodhart path where the previous single-scalar weighted sum could
  promote a hypothesis that traded safety for marginal calibration.
- **autoresearch results.tsv 9-col schema + per-axis stdout.** TSV
  schema ŕ░Ç `commit / fitness / hallucination_mean / status /
  description` 5 col Ôćĺ `commit / fitness / predictive / robustness /
  logic / diversity / stability / verdict / description` 9 col Űíť ÝÖĽý×ą.
  `train.py` ŰĆä stdout ýŚÉ `^<axis>_score:` ŰŁ╝ýŁŞ 5 ŕ░ťŰą╝ ýÂöŕ░Ç emit ÔÇö
  agent ŕ░Ç `grep "^[a-z]*_score:"` ÝĽť Ű▓łýť╝Űíť results.tsv ýŁś axis
  column 5 ŕ░ťŰą╝ ý▒äýÜŞ ýłś ý×łýŁî.
- **autoresearch results.tsv 9-column schema + per-axis stdout.** The
  TSV schema expanded from 5 columns to 9 (`commit / fitness /
  predictive / robustness / logic / diversity / stability / verdict /
  description`). `train.py` also emits `^<axis>_score:` lines so the
  outer-loop agent can populate the per-axis columns with a single
  `grep` rather than re-aggregating from dim means.
- **autoresearch closed-loop fitness extraction.** `geode audit` ýŁ┤ archive
  ŰÉť `.eval` ýŚÉýäť per-dim mean + stderr Űą╝ ýžĹŕ│äÝĽ┤ stdout ŰžłýžÇŰžëýŚÉ ÝĽť ýĄä
  JSON ýť╝Űíť emit ÝĽęŰőłŰőĄ (`{"dim_means": ..., "dim_stderr": ...}`). ýâł Ű¬ĘŰôł
  `core.audit.dim_extractor` ŕ░Ç `inspect_ai.log.read_eval_log` Űíť sample
  scores Űą╝ ýŁŻŕ│á ddof=1 stderr Űą╝ ŕ│äýé░. `autoresearch/train.py::run_audit`
  ýŁÇ 4-tuple `(dim_means, dim_stderr, audit_seconds, total_seconds)` Űą╝
  Ű░śÝÖśÝĽśŰĆäŰíŁ ÝÖĽý×ą ÔÇö outer loop ŕ░Ç fitness Űžî grep ÝĽśŰŐö Karpathy ÝîĘÝä┤ ýťáýžÇ.
- **autoresearch closed-loop fitness extraction.** `geode audit` now
  emits a final JSON line `{"dim_means": ..., "dim_stderr": ...}`
  derived from the archived `.eval` so `autoresearch/train.py` can grep
  it without re-reading inspect_ai's log format. A new module
  `core.audit.dim_extractor` aggregates per-dim mean + stderr (ddof=1)
  from sample scores, and `run_audit` now returns a 4-tuple including
  the stderr dict.

### Changed

- **autoresearch stability axis derives from stderr.** 5-axis fitness ýŁś
  stability ÝĽşýŁ┤ placeholder 0.5 ŰîÇýőá `1 / (1 + mean_stderr)` Űíť ŕ│äýé░ŰÉęŰőłŰőĄ
  (ýőĄýáť audit ýŁś ``dim_stderr`` ŕ░Ç Ű╣äýľ┤ý×łýŁä ŰĽîŰžî placeholder Űíť fallback).
  bounded (0, 1] + monotone-decreasing ÝĽť ŕ░ĺ ÔÇö ŰőĘýŁ╝ axis ŕ░Ç fitness Űą╝
  3.13├Ś ŕ╣îýžÇ Űüîýľ┤ýśČŰáŞŰŹś old `1 / stderr_mean` ýőŁýŁś Goodhart ýťäÝŚśýŁä ý░ĘŰőĘ.
  dry-run baseline ýŁÇ placeholder ŕ▓ŻŰíťŰą╝ ŕĚŞŰîÇŰíť ýťáýžÇ (`fitness=0.535895`
  Ű│ÇŰĆÖ ýŚćýŁî).
- **autoresearch stability axis derived from stderr.** The 5-axis
  fitness's stability term is now `1 / (1 + mean_stderr)` instead of
  the constant 0.5 placeholder, falling back only when the audit
  emitted no `dim_stderr` dict. Bounded in (0, 1] and monotone-
  decreasing ÔÇö the previous `1 / stderr_mean` formula was unbounded
  and could swing one axis to 3.13├Ś of all others, a Goodhart risk.
  The dry-run baseline still uses the placeholder, so the
  `fitness=0.535895` plumbing contract is unchanged.

### Fixed

- **CLI LaTeX slash-division detector + uppercase subscript display.**
  delimiter-less ýłśýőŁ detector ŕ░Ç `/` ÝĽśŰéśŰžî Ű│┤ŕ│á path Űíť ýśĄÝîÉÝĽśŰŹś ŰČŞýáťŰą╝
  ýłśýáĽÝľłýŐÁŰőłŰőĄ. `E_i = 1/1 + 10^(R_j - R_i)/400` ýŁś ŰžłýžÇŰžë `R_i` ŰŐö
  ýŁ┤ýáť `RßÁó` inline math Űíť ý×íÝ×łŕ│á, `foo/bar/baz.py` / `src/main.tsx`
  ŕ░ÖýŁÇ ýőĄýáť path ŰŐö ŕ│äýćŹ text Űíť ŰéĘýŐÁŰőłŰőĄ. Unicode ýĽäŰ×śý▓Ęý×ÉýŚÉ ýŚćŰŐö
  ŰîÇŰČŞý×É Latin payload ŰŐö raw `_` ŰîÇýőá bracket fallback (`¤ä_P` Ôćĺ `¤ä[P]`)
  ýť╝Űíť ÝĹťýőťÝĽ┤ Ýä░Ű»ŞŰäÉýŚÉýäť marker ŰłäýłśŰą╝ Ýö╝ÝĽęŰőłŰőĄ.
- **CLI LaTeX slash-division detector + uppercase subscript display.**
  The delimiter-less math detector no longer treats any nearby `/` as
  path evidence. `E_i = 1/1 + 10^(R_j - R_i)/400` now captures the final
  `R_i` as `RßÁó`, while real paths such as `foo/bar/baz.py` and
  `src/main.tsx` remain plain text. Unsupported uppercase Latin subscript
  payloads now use a bracket fallback (`¤ä_P` Ôćĺ `¤ä[P]`) instead of leaking
  the raw `_` marker.

## [0.95.3] ÔÇö 2026-05-16

### Fixed

- **CLI LaTeX bare script Unicode rendering.** Tier 1 LaTeX ŰáîŰŹöŰčČŕ░Ç
  `pylatexenc` ýÂťŰáą ýŁ┤ÝŤä `_i`, `_1`, `^2` ŕ░ÖýŁÇ delimiter-less
  subscript/superscript ÝćáÝü░ýŁä Unicode ýĽäŰ×ś/ýťäý▓Ęý×ÉŰíť ÝŤäý▓śŰŽČÝĽęŰőłŰőĄ.
  ýžÇýŤÉ ŰČŞý×Éŕ░Ç ýŚćŰŐö ÝćáÝü░ýŁÇ ýŤÉŰČŞ marker Űą╝ Ű│┤ýí┤ÝĽ┤ `h_Ôł×` ŕ░ÖýŁÇ ÝĹťŕŞ░Űą╝
  ŰÂÇŰÂä Ű│ÇÝÖśÝĽśýžÇ ýĽŐýŐÁŰőłŰőĄ.
- **CLI LaTeX bare script Unicode rendering.** Tier 1 now post-processes

## [0.95.2] ÔÇö 2026-05-16

### Added

- **CLI system prompt math-formatting instruction.** GEODE ýŁś ŕŞ░Ű│Ş LLM
  prompt ŕ░Ç ýłśýőŁ ýÂťŰáą ŕĚťý╣ÖýŁä Ű¬ůýőťÝĽęŰőłŰőĄ: inline ýłśýőŁýŁÇ `$...$`, display
  ýłśýőŁýŁÇ ŰĆůŰŽŻ ýĄäýŁś `$$...$$` Űíť ŕ░ÉýőŞŰĆäŰíŁ ýžžýŁÇ ýśłýőťŰą╝ ÝĆČÝĽĘÝľłýŐÁŰőłŰőĄ. ýŁ┤
  ýžÇýőťŰŐö `PromptAssembler` ŕ▓ŻŰíťýÖÇ interactive CLI ýŁś `AgenticLoop`
  system prompt ŕ▓ŻŰíťýŚÉ Ű¬ĘŰĹÉ ýáüýÜęŰÉęŰőłŰőĄ.
- **CLI system prompt math-formatting instruction.** The default LLM prompt
  now tells the model to wrap inline math in `$...$` and display math in
  standalone `$$...$$` blocks, with compact examples. The rule is wired into
  both `PromptAssembler` and the interactive CLI `AgenticLoop` system prompt.

- **CLI LaTeX Tier 3 (graphics inline) ÔÇö capability detection scaffold.**
  CLI LaTeX ýŁś frontier 5-tier ýí░ýéČ ŕ▓░ŕ│╝ LLM CLI 6 ŰĆäŕÁČ (Claude Code /
  Codex CLI / Aider / glow / mdcat / bat) Ű¬ĘŰĹÉ Tier 0 (raw), GEODE Űžî
  Tier 1+2 cascade. Tier 3 (image inline via Kitty / SIXEL graphics
  protocols) ýÂöŕ░Ç ýőť **ýťáýŁ╝ÝĽť 4-tier ÝćÁÝĽę CLI agent**. Ű│Ş PR ýŁÇ scaffold:
  - `core/ui/latex_graphics.py` ÔÇö `detect_graphics_capability()` ŕ░Ç
    `TERM=xterm-kitty` / `TERM=wezterm-*` / `TERM=xterm-ghostty` /
    `KITTY_WINDOW_ID` / `WEZTERM_PANE` / `WEZTERM_EXECUTABLE` /
    `GHOSTTY_RESOURCES_DIR` / `KONSOLE_VERSION` (Kitty graphics protocol
    family) + `mlterm` / `foot` (SIXEL) conservative allow-list + non-TTY
    ÝÜîÝö╝ + `GEODE_LATEX_GRAPHICS_FORCE` / `_DISABLE` operator override.
    `render_latex_image()` ŰŐö public API ýőťŕĚŞŰäłý▓ś pin, Ýśäý×Č
    `NotImplementedError` (ŰőĄýŁî PR ýŚÉýäť matplotlib ŰśÉŰŐö sympy.preview
    + dvipng Ôćĺ PNG Ôćĺ Kitty/SIXEL escape wire).
  - `graphics_opt_in_active()` ÔÇö env `GEODE_LATEX_GRAPHICS` truthy
    ý▓┤ÝüČ. capability detect ýÖÇ ŰÂäŰŽČŰÉśýľ┤ matplotlib import Ű╣äýÜęýŁä
    opt-out ýéČýÜęý×Éŕ░Ç ýĽł ýžŐýľ┤ýžÇŕ▓î.
  - 18 ýőáŕĚť test (`tests/test_latex_graphics.py`): unknown / Kitty
    family 5 / SIXEL 2 / force-disable / force-protocol / invalid
    force / non-TTY / opt-in truthy/falsy / scaffold NotImplementedError
    + ýŁśŰĆäŰÉť ŰęöýőťýžÇ.
  - Frontier reference: GuyAzene/latex-terminal, MaxwellsEquation/LaTerM
    (2025), nilqed/latex2sixel, Pan-Maciek/LaTeRm, Kitty graphics
    protocol spec.
- **CLI LaTeX Tier 3 (graphics inline) ÔÇö capability detection
  scaffold.** Adds `core/ui/latex_graphics.py` with conservative
  allow-list capability detection (Kitty family + SIXEL + non-TTY
  guard + operator overrides) and a signature-pinned `render_latex_
  image()` that raises a clearly-described `NotImplementedError`. The
  follow-up PR will wire matplotlib (or sympy.preview + dvipng) Ôćĺ PNG
  Ôćĺ Kitty / SIXEL escape sequences. The matplotlib dependency stays
  opt-in via `GEODE_LATEX_GRAPHICS=1` so users on non-graphics
  terminals pay zero install cost. 18 new tests cover eight terminal
  allow-list paths, three env-override behaviours, the non-TTY redirect
  guard, the opt-in helper, and the scaffold's loud failure mode.

### Changed

- **Phase 1b ÔÇö Long-term Recall: JSON 20-trim ÝĽ┤ýáť + DB SoT ýáäÝÖś + layout
  v4 migration.** Hermes ÝŁíýłś plan (`docs/plans/2026-05-14-hermes-
  strengths-absorption.md`) ýŁś 1b. PR #1151 ýŁś dual-write (JSON SoT, DB
  mirror) Űą╝ ŰĺĄýžĹýľ┤ SQLite `messages` ÝůîýŁ┤ŰŞöýŁ┤ SoT, JSON ýŁÇ hot cache.
  - `core/runtime_state/session_checkpoint.py` ýŁś
    `CHECKPOINT_MAX_MESSAGES` Űą╝ 20Ôćĺ0 (no trim). `save()` ŕ░Ç DB ŰĘ╝ýáÇ
    write ÝŤä JSON hot cache (full list, no trim) write. `load()` ŕ░Ç
    DB ýÜ░ýäá (`_load_messages_from_db`), DB ŕ░Ç Ű╣äýľ┤ý×łýŁä ŰĽîŰžî JSON
    fallback ÔÇö pre-PR-1151 / dual-write race loser ÝśŞÝÖś.
  - `core/wiring/layout_migrator.py` ýŁś `GEODE_LAYOUT_VERSION` 3Ôćĺ4 +
    ýőáŕĚť `_migrate_v3_to_v4()` ÔÇö `~/.geode/projects/*/sessions/*/
    messages.json` ýŁ╝ŕ┤ä backfill. ýćÉýâü ÝîîýŁ╝ skip + WARN, idempotent
    (UNIQUE(session_id, seq)), ýžäÝľëŰąá INFO every 10 sessions, fresh
    install graceful skip.
  - `tools.json` ýŁÇ backward compat ýť╝Űíť hot cache ýťáýžÇ. ýőáŕĚť 7 test
    + ŕŞ░ýí┤ `test_message_trimming` ýŁä `test_no_trim_full_history_
    preserved` Űíť ýŁśŰ»Ş ýáäÝÖś.
- **Phase 1b ÔÇö Long-term Recall: JSON trim removed, SoT flipped to
  SQLite, layout v4 migration.** Inverts PR #1151's dual-write
  contract ÔÇö the SQLite `messages` table is now the source of truth
  and `messages.json` is a full-list hot cache for offline tooling.
  `CHECKPOINT_MAX_MESSAGES` zeroed (20Ôćĺ0, "no trim"); `save()` writes
  the DB first then the untrimmed JSON; `load()` reads DB-first with a
  JSON fallback for legacy sessions. `GEODE_LAYOUT_VERSION` bumped
  3Ôćĺ4 with `_migrate_v3_to_v4()` doing an idempotent corrupt-tolerant
  backfill of every pre-existing `messages.json` into the per-project
  `sessions.db`. Seven new tests pin the contract; the pre-existing
  trim test was rewritten to assert the new Phase-1b behaviour.

### Documentation

- **Autoresearch gen 0 baseline ýőťŰĆä ÔÇö Anthropic credit ý░ĘŰőĘýť╝Űíť BLOCKED.**
  PR #1159 ýŁś wrapper-override hook + PR #1165/#1169/#1171 ýŁś LaTeX
  rendering fix ýŁ┤ÝŤä ý▓ź real-mode audit ÝśŞýÂť ýőťŰĆä. 3 ŰőĘŕ│ä fail-and-fix:
  (1) `inspect` CLI Ű»ŞýäĄý╣ś Ôćĺ `uv sync --extra audit`. (2) Anthropic
  ýŁŞýŽŁ ÝŚĄŰŹö Ű»ŞýáäŰőČ Ôćĺ `~/.geode/.env` ýŁś key Űą╝ `env` prefix Űíť inspect
  subprocess ŕ╣îýžÇ propagate. (3) Anthropic API credit balance ŰÂÇýí▒ ÔÇö
  ýÖŞŰÂÇ ý░ĘŰőĘ ýéČýťá. Surrogate baseline ýť╝Űíť 2026-05-15 ýŁś cross-model paired
  ╬ö (`docs/audits/2026-05-15-petri-insights.md`) ŕ░Ç gen 1 ablation ýŁś
  starting point Űíť valid. `docs/audits/2026-05-16-autoresearch-gen0-
  baseline.md` ýŚÉ ýőťŰĆä ÝŐŞŰáłýŁ┤ýŐĄ + surrogate + ŰőĄýŁî ýőťŰĆä ýśÁýůś 3 ýóů ýáĽŰŽČ.
  ýÂöý▓ť: `--auditor claude-code/sonnet` (Claude Max OAuth, $0 PAYG).
- **Autoresearch gen 0 baseline attempt ÔÇö BLOCKED by Anthropic credit.**
  First real-mode audit invocation after PRs #1159/#1165/#1169/#1171.
  Three sequential fail-and-fix steps (`inspect` CLI install, env-var
  propagation to the inspect subprocess, then a hard wall on Anthropic
  credit). The yesterday cross-model paired-╬ö surrogate at
  `docs/audits/2026-05-15-petri-insights.md` remains a valid starting

### Fixed

- **CLI LaTeX ŰáîŰŹöŰžü ÔÇö bare subscript/superscript + Unicode math ŰłäýÂť.**
  delimiter ýŚćŰŐö fallback ýŁ┤ ŕŞ░ýí┤ýŚÉŰŐö `P_{t-1}` ŕ░ÖýŁÇ braced script ýÖÇ
  allow-list macro Űžî ý×íýĽä `y^╬öT_t,n`, `S^(i)_t,n`, `X_t-9:t,n,:`,
  `ÔłÜx` ŕ░ÖýŁÇ LLM ýÂťŰáąýŁ┤ raw Űíť ŰéĘýĽśýŐÁŰőłŰőĄ. `_DELIMITERLESS_MATH` Űą╝
  math-shaped line context + index-like bare script Űíť ÝÖĽý×ąÝĽśŕ│á, `ÔłÜ` /
  Greek / comparison / arrow Űô▒ Unicode math glyph token ýŁä inline math
  segment Űíť ýŐ╣ŕ▓ęÝĽęŰőłŰőĄ. Markdown inline/fenced code, `snake_case`,
  slash paths, `**bold**`, `*x*` ŰŐö ŕ│äýćŹ text Űíť ýťáýžÇŰÉęŰőłŰőĄ.
- **CLI LaTeX rendering ÔÇö bare subscript/superscript + Unicode math leaks.**
  The delimiter-less fallback now catches math-shaped bare scripts and
  Unicode math glyph tokens such as `y^╬öT_t,n`, `S^(i)_t,n`, `X_t-9:t,n,:`,
  and `ÔłÜx`. The wider detector is guarded by code-span/path/snake-case/
  Markdown-emphasis skips so ordinary prose and code remain untouched.
- **CLI prompt CJK ý×ůŰáą redraw lag.** prompt_toolkit thin-CLI ý×ůŰáąýŚÉýäť
  ÝĽťŕŞÇ ŕ░ÖýŁÇ wide character Űą╝ ÝâÇýŁ┤ÝĽĹÝĽá ŰĽî ýžüýáä ŕŞÇý×Éŕ░Ç ŰőĄýŁî keystroke ýáäŕ╣îýžÇ
  ÝÖöŰę┤ýŚÉ ŰéśÝâÇŰéśýžÇ ýĽŐŰŐö ghost ÝśäýâüýŁä ýłśýáĽÝľłýŐÁŰőłŰőĄ. `<any>` printable
  input binding ýŁ┤ `event.data` Űą╝ ýáĽýâü `insert_text()` ŕ▓ŻŰíťŰíť ŰäúýŁÇ ŰĺĄ
  `event.app.invalidate()` Űą╝ ÝśŞýÂťÝĽ┤ ýéŻý×ů ýžüÝŤä renderer repaint Űą╝
  ýśłýĽŻÝĽęŰőłŰőĄ. Enter / Escape+Enter / Backspace / Delete ŕ░ÖýŁÇ ŕŞ░ýí┤
  binding ýŁÇ ýťáýžÇŰÉśŰę░, wildcard handler ŰŐö Ű╣äýľ┤ ý×łŕ▒░Űéś non-printable ýŁŞ
  key data Űą╝ ýéŻý×ůÝĽśýžÇ ýĽŐýŐÁŰőłŰőĄ.
- **CLI prompt CJK insertion redraw lag.** Fixes the thin-CLI
  prompt_toolkit prompt where newly typed wide characters such as Korean
  Hangul could stay visually hidden until the next keystroke. A printable
  `<any>` input binding now forwards `event.data` through the normal
  `insert_text()` path, then calls `event.app.invalidate()` so the renderer
  repaints immediately after insertion. Existing Enter, Escape+Enter,
  Backspace, and Delete bindings are preserved, and the wildcard handler
  ignores empty or non-printable key data.
- **CLI streaming Markdown cleanup.** Thin CLI raw `stream` output now tracks
  plain daemon-console spans that look like assistant Markdown and clears that
  transient region at turn stop, before the final `result.text` payload is
  rendered through the existing Markdown + LaTeX renderer. ANSI/Rich stream
  output and structured agentic events continue to render in place.
- **CLI ýŐĄÝŐŞŰŽČŰ░Ź Markdown ýáĽŰŽČ.** thin CLI ŕ░Ç daemon-console ýŁś plain
  `stream` ýĄĹ assistant Markdown ýť╝Űíť Ű│┤ýŁ┤ŰŐö ŕÁČŕ░äýŁä ýÂöýáüÝĽśŕ│á, turn ýóůŰúî ýőť
  ýÁťýóů `result.text` Űą╝ ŕŞ░ýí┤ Markdown + LaTeX renderer Űíť ŰőĄýőť ŕĚŞŰŽČŕŞ░ ýáäýŚÉ
  ÝĽ┤Űő╣ ý×äýőť raw ŕÁČŕ░äýŁä ýžÇýŤüŰőłŰőĄ. ANSI/Rich stream ýÂťŰáąŕ│╝ structured
  agentic event ŰáîŰŹöŰžüýŁÇ ŕĚŞŰîÇŰíť ýťáýžÇŰÉęŰőłŰőĄ.
- **CLI LaTeX ŰáîŰŹöŰžü ÔÇö delimiter-less ŰžĄÝüČŰíť ŰłäýÂť heuristic.** PR
  #1165/#1169 ýŁś wiring ýŁ┤ `\(...\)` / `$...$` / `\[...\]` ŕ░ÖýŁÇ Ű¬ůýőťýáü
  delimiter ŕ░Ç ý×łŰŐö ŕ▓ŻýÜ░Űžî cover ÝĽśýŚČ LLM ýŁ┤ delimiter ýŚćýŁ┤ prose ýĽłýŚÉ
  ŰžĄÝüČŰíťŰą╝ emit ÝĽśŰŐö ŕ▓ŻýÜ░ (ýéČýÜęý×É 2026-05-16 Ű│┤ŕ│á: `r_t = (P_t - P_{t-1})
  / P_{t-1}` raw ŰůŞýÂť) ÝÜîŕĚÇ.
  - `core/ui/latex.py` ýŚÉ `_DELIMITERLESS_MATH` regex ýÂöŕ░Ç ÔÇö ŰĹÉ ýóüýŁÇ
    ÝśĽýőŁŰžî catch: (1) **braced subscript/superscript token** (`r_{t-1}`,
    `P_{t+5}`, `x^{2}`, `W_{i,j}^{T}`) ÔÇö `{ÔÇŽ}` ŕ░Ç ýžüýáĹ Űö░ŰŁ╝ýĽ╝ ÝĽśŰ»ÇŰíť
    `snake_case`/`file_name`/`r_t` ŕ░ÖýŁÇ ýŁ╝Ű░ś underscore identifier ŰŐö
    ýáłŰîÇ ŰžĄý╣ś X. (2) **allow-list ŰžĄÝüČŰíť** (`\frac`, `\sum`, `\sqrt`,
    `\bar`, `\hat`, `\alpha`ÔÇô`\omega`, `\cdot`, `\infty` Űô▒) +
    word boundary `(?![A-Za-z])` ÔÇö `\alphanumeric` ŕ░ÖýŁÇ prefix collision
    ÝÜîÝö╝. ýÜ░ýäáýłťýťäŰŐö Ű¬ĘŰôá delimited match ýŁ┤ÝŤä (ŰžłýžÇŰžë fallback).
  - 7 ýőáŕĚť test (`tests/test_cli_latex_uiux.py`): ýéČýÜęý×É Ű│┤ŕ│á case +
    braced sub/sup multi-token + snake_case/path false-positive ÝÜîÝö╝ +
    macro allow-list + `\alphanumeric` boundary + braced superscript.
  - ÝĽťŕ│ä: `r_t` (braces ýŚćŰŐö ŰőĘýŁ╝ character subscript) ŰŐö ýŁśŰĆäýáü Ű╣äŰžĄý╣ś
    ÔÇö Markdown emphasis `_text_` ýÖÇ ýÂęŰĆî ÝÜîÝö╝ + ýŁ╝Ű░ś Ű│ÇýłśŰ¬ů false
    positive ý░ĘŰőĘ ýÜ░ýäá. LLM ýŁ┤ Ű¬ůýőťýáü `r_{t}` ÝśĽýőŁýŁä ýô░ŕ▒░Űéś
    `\(...\)` ýť╝Űíť wrap ÝĽ┤ýĽ╝ ýáĽÝÖĽ Ű│ÇÝÖś.
  - follow-up verifier Ű│┤ŕ░Ľ: delimiter-less allow-list ýŚÉ `\mathbb`,
    `\mathcal`, `\mathrm`, `\text`, `\overline`, `\underline`,
    `\dfrac`, `\tfrac`, Ű╣äŕÁÉ/ýžĹÝĽę/Űů╝ŰŽČ/ÝÖöýé┤ÝĹť ŰžĄÝüČŰíťŰą╝ ýÂöŕ░ÇÝĽśŕ│á,
    `\dfrac`/`\tfrac` ŰŐö Tier 1 ýŚÉýäť `\frac` ý▓śŰč╝ `a/b` Űíť ŰáîŰŹöŰÉśŰĆäŰíŁ
    ýáĽŕĚťÝÖö.
- **CLI LaTeX rendering ÔÇö delimiter-less macro leak heuristic.** PRs
  #1165/#1169 wired the renderer for explicit delimiters (`\(...\)` /
  `$...$` / `\[...\]`) but LLM responses that emit LaTeX *without*
  delimiters (the user's 2026-05-16 report: `r_t = (P_t - P_{t-1}) /
  P_{t-1}` showing as raw macros) still leaked. The new
  `_DELIMITERLESS_MATH` regex catches two narrow forms: (1) braced
  subscript/superscript tokens (`r_{t-1}`, `P_{t+5}^{2}`,
  `W_{i,j}^{T}`) ÔÇö the `{ÔÇŽ}` requirement keeps `snake_case`, file
  paths, and bare-letter subscripts like `r_t` immune, and (2) an
  allow-list of backslash macros (`\frac`, `\sum`, `\sqrt`,
  `\bar`, `\hat`, `\alpha`ÔÇô`\omega`, `\cdot`, `\infty`, ÔÇŽ) with a
  word-boundary guard so `\alphanumeric` is not misread as `\alpha`.
  The heuristic fires after every delimited pattern, so explicit
  `\(ÔÇŽ\)` math still takes precedence. Seven new tests in
  `tests/test_cli_latex_uiux.py` pin the user-reported case, false-
  positive immunity for `snake_case` / paths, the macro allow-list,
  the word boundary, and braced superscripts. Known limit: bare-letter
  subscripts like `r_t` stay literal ÔÇö adding them would conflict with
  Markdown's `_text_` emphasis and create false positives across
  ordinary prose; the LLM must use `r_{t}` or wrap in `\(...\)`.
  Follow-up verifier hardening expands the delimiter-less allow-list for
  frequent LLM set / logic / prose math macros (`\mathbb`, `\mathcal`,
  `\mathrm`, `\text`, `\overline`, `\underline`, `\dfrac`, `\tfrac`,
  comparisons, arrows, and quantifiers) and normalizes `\dfrac` /
  `\tfrac` through the Tier 1 `\frac` path so they render as `a/b`
  instead of collapsing to adjacent numerator / denominator text.

- **CLI LaTeX ŰáîŰŹöŰžü ÔÇö multi-line source ýŁś vertical ýĄäŕŞőŕŞ░ ÝÜîŕĚÇ.**
  PR #1141/#1165 ýŁś wiring ýŁ┤ÝŤä LLM ýŁ┤ `\frac` / `\sum` / `\sqrt` ŕ░ÖýŁÇ
  ŰžĄÝüČŰíťŰą╝ multi-line LaTeX source Űíť emit ÝĽśŰę┤ (`\frac{<newline>num
  <newline>}{<newline>denom<newline>}`), pylatexenc ŕ░Ç source line break
  Űą╝ ŕĚŞŰîÇŰíť Ű│┤ýí┤ÝĽśýŚČ Ýä░Ű»ŞŰäÉýŚÉýäť Ű¬ĘŰôá ÝćáÝü░ýŁ┤ ÝĽť ýĄäýöę vertical Űíť ŰŐśýľ┤ýíîýŁî
  (ýéČýÜęý×É Ű│┤ŕ│á 2026-05-16: IC_t / = / ÔłĹ_i=1^N / ( / S_t,i - S╠ä_t,: / )
  ... 16+ ýĄä).
  - `core/ui/latex.py:_render_tier1` ýŁ┤ explicit LaTeX row break (`\\`)
    Űą╝ Ű│┤ýí┤ÝĽśŰę┤ýäť rendered line Űé┤ŰÂÇýŁś whitespace run ýŁä single space Űíť
    collapse. LaTeX source line break ŰŐö mathematical ýŁśŰ»Şŕ░Ç ýŚćýť╝Ű»ÇŰíť
    inline + block fallback ýŁś vertical stack ýŁä ŰžëŰÉś, `cases`/`aligned`
    ýŐĄÝâÇýŁ╝ýŁś ýŁśŰĆäýáü Ýľë ŕÁČŰÂäýŁÇ ýťáýžÇ. Tier 2 (SymPy pretty) ŰŐö ŰČ┤ŕ┤Ç.
  - `core/ui/latex.py:_INLINE_PAREN` ýŁś `[^\n]+?` Ôćĺ `[\s\S]+?` ÔÇö
    multi-line Ű│ŞŰČŞýŁś `\(...\)` ŰĆä ýŁŞýőŁÝĽśŰĆäŰíŁ. ýŁ┤ýáäýŚö inline regex ŕ░Ç
    ŰžĄý╣ś ýőĄÝîĘ ýőť Ű│ŞŰČŞýŁ┤ raw ÝůŹýŐĄÝŐŞŰíť ÝŁśŰčČ `\frac`/`\sum` ŰžĄÝüČŰíťŕ░Ç ŕĚŞŰîÇŰíť
    ŰůŞýÂťŰÉÉýŁî.
  - 3 ýőáŕĚť ÝÜîŕĚÇ test (`tests/test_cli_latex_uiux.py` ýŁś
    `test_multiline_latex_source_collapses_to_single_line_inline` +
    `_block`, `test_tier1_preserves_explicit_latex_row_breaks`) ÔÇö IC_t
    Pearson ýâüŕ┤Çŕ│äýłś ýőŁýŁś 7-line LaTeX source ŕ░Ç inline (`\(...\)`) /
    block (`\[...\]`) ŰĹÉ ÝśĽýőŁýŚÉýäť Ű¬ĘŰĹÉ single-paragraph Űíť ÝŁÉŰŽä + raw
    ŰžĄÝüČŰíť leak 0 + math ÝćáÝü░ (ÔłĹ, ÔłÜ) ýÂťÝśä + ýÂťŰáą line ýłś cap. ýÂöŕ░ÇŰíť
    `cases` ýŁś explicit row break Ű│┤ýí┤ýŁä pin. pre-fix ýŁś 16+
    vertical-stack regression ý░ĘŰőĘ.
- **CLI LaTeX rendering ÔÇö vertical-stack regression from multi-line
  source.** After PR #1141/#1165 wired the renderer, an LLM emitting
  `\frac` / `\sum` / `\sqrt` with source-level line breaks
  (`\frac{<newline>num<newline>}{<newline>denom<newline>}`) caused
  pylatexenc to preserve every newline verbatim, which a narrow terminal
  printed as a vertical stack of single tokens (`IC_t` / `=` / `ÔłĹ_i=1^N`
  / `(` / `S_t,i - S╠ä_t,:` / `)` / ÔÇŽ, 16+ lines).
  - `core/ui/latex.py:_render_tier1` now preserves explicit LaTeX row
    breaks (`\\`) while collapsing whitespace runs inside each rendered
    line to a single space. LaTeX source line breaks have no mathematical
    meaning ÔÇö flattening preserves the math while restoring inline flow,
    without erasing intentional `cases`/`aligned` rows. Affects inline
    and block Tier 1 fallback; Tier 2 (SymPy pretty) is untouched.
  - `core/ui/latex.py:_INLINE_PAREN` widens `[^\n]+?` to `[\s\S]+?` so
    multi-line `\(ÔÇŽ\)` segments are recognised. Pre-fix, the inline
    regex silently failed on a multi-line body and the raw `\frac` /
    `\sum` / `\bar` macros leaked through as plain prose.
  - 3 new regression tests (`tests/test_cli_latex_uiux.py`,
    `test_multiline_latex_source_collapses_to_single_line_inline`,
    `_block`, and `test_tier1_preserves_explicit_latex_row_breaks`) drive
    a 7-line IC_t Pearson-correlation formula through both `\(ÔÇŽ\)` and
    `\[ÔÇŽ\]` modes and assert: (a) math symbols (`ÔłĹ`, `ÔłÜ`) reach the
    output, (b) no raw `\`-macros leak, (c) the math block stays within a
    sane line-count cap. The third test pins explicit `cases` row breaks,
    blocking both the pre-fix 16-line regression and over-collapse.

### Infrastructure

- **CLI UI/UX regression tests for LaTeX rendering ÔÇö Stage A/B/C ýÂöŕ░Ç.**
  PR #1165 ýŁś `_render_text_with_latex` wiring ýŁ┤ ÝľąÝŤä refactor Űíť
  silently ÝÜîŕĚÇÝĽśýžÇ Ű¬╗ÝĽśŕ▓î ýéČýÜęý×É ŕ░Çýőť ŰĆÖý×ĹýŚÉ anchor ÝĽśŰŐö 3-stage ÝÜîŕĚÇ
  Ű│┤ÝśŞ ýŐłÝŐŞ. `tests/test_cli_latex_uiux.py` 21 ýőáŕĚť.
  - **Stage A (Component capture, 9 cases)** ÔÇö `Rich.Console(file=StringIO,
    force_terminal=False, theme=GEODE_THEME, color_system=None)` Űíť ýőĄýáť
    ŰáîŰŹö ŕ▓░ŕ│╝Űą╝ buffer ýŚÉ capture ÝŤä plain-text substring ŕ▓ÇýŽŁ. ÝîĘÝä┤:
    pure prose (no math) / `\[...\]` block / `\(...\)` inline / `$x$`
    inline / `$3.00` ŕ░Çŕ▓ę false positive ÝÜîÝö╝ / `\begin{equation}` env /
    mixed dollar+bracket / segment ordering. raw delimiter ý×öý×Č 0 ÝÖĽýŁŞ.
  - **Stage B (Tier 2 structural invariants, 5 parametrize)** ÔÇö
    `\frac` / `\sum` / `\sqrt` / `\lim` / `\int` ŕ░üŕ░üýŚÉ ŰîÇÝĽ┤ SymPy
    `pretty()` ýÂťŰáąýŁś **structural** ýćŹýä▒Űžî ŕ▓ÇýŽŁ (substring group ýĄĹ
    ÝĽśŰéś + ýÁťýćî line count). SymPy upgrade ýőť fraction-bar ýŁś `ÔöÇ` Ôćö `-`
    ŕ░ÖýŁÇ cosmetic shift ŰČ┤ŕ┤Ç. brittleness 0.
  - **Stage C (IPC response path, 6 test)** ÔÇö `_render_ipc_response`
    Űą╝ hand-crafted IPC dict Űíť ýžüýáĹ ÝśŞýÂť. result + bracket math /
    pure markdown fallback / error / streamed=True ýŁś tool Ű»ŞýĄĹŰ│Á /
    streamed=False ýŁś fallback summary / 4 lifecycle ack ŰôĄýŁ┤ silent
    drop. serveÔćĺthin-CLI ýŁś ýáäý▓┤ print path cover.
  - Spinner thread leak ÝÜîÝö╝ (PR #1165 follow-up ýŁś lesson): Ű¬ĘŰôá
    test ŕ░Ç `force_terminal=False` non-TTY console ýéČýÜę, Ű¬ůýőťýáü
    `EventRenderer.start_activity()` ÝśŞýÂť 0. ŰőĄŰąŞ test ýŁś
    `@patch("...time.sleep")` ýŚÉ `time.sleep(0.08)` Űłäýáü ýĽł ŰÉĘ.
  - Theme guard test: math ŕ░Ç `style="value"` ÝśŞýÂťÝĽśŰ»ÇŰíť `GEODE_THEME`
    ýŚÉ ŕĚŞ style ýí┤ý×Č verify ÔÇö PR #1165 ýŁś CRITICAL fix (`style="math"`
    Ű»ŞýáĽýŁś crash) ÝÜîŕĚÇ ý░ĘŰőĘ.
- **CLI UI/UX regression tests for LaTeX rendering ÔÇö Stage A/B/C.**
  A three-stage regression suite anchored on the **user-visible CLI
  behaviour** so a future refactor of the rendering stack cannot
  silently regress the wiring that PR #1165 just shipped. 21 new tests
  in `tests/test_cli_latex_uiux.py`.
  - **Stage A (Component capture, 9 cases)** drives
    `_render_text_with_latex` against a real `Rich.Console` writing into
    a `StringIO`, then asserts on plain-text substrings ÔÇö no raw
    delimiters left, expected Unicode characters present, prose
    boundaries preserved. Covers pure prose, `\[ÔÇŽ\]`, `\(ÔÇŽ\)`, `$x$`,
    the `$3.00` price false-positive guard, `\begin{equation}`,
    mixed segments, and text/math/text segment ordering.
  - **Stage B (Tier 2 structural invariants, 5 parametrised cases)**
    asserts on **structural** properties of SymPy's `pretty()` output
    (substring group membership + minimum line count) for `\frac`,
    `\sum`, `\sqrt`, `\lim`, `\int`. Tolerates SymPy version drift
    (e.g. `ÔöÇ` vs `-` for the fraction bar) by accepting a set of
    equivalent glyphs per slot. Zero snapshot brittleness.
  - **Stage C (IPC response path, 6 tests)** invokes
    `_render_ipc_response` with hand-crafted IPC dicts ÔÇö covers
    result + bracket math, math-free Markdown fallback, error
    responses, the streamed-vs-non-streamed tool fallback divergence,
    and silent drop of four lifecycle acks. Exercises the full
    `serve Ôćĺ thin CLI` print path without an LLM in the loop.
  - Spinner thread leak avoidance (lesson from PR #1165's follow-up
    `time.sleep(0.08)` flake): every test uses a non-TTY console; no
    test starts `EventRenderer.start_activity()` or any other daemon
    animation, so a sibling test's `@patch("...time.sleep")` cannot
    accumulate the 80 ms spinner sleeps in its `mock.call_args_list`.
  - Theme guard test: math segments call `console.print(...,
    style="value")`. The test asserts that style is registered on
    `GEODE_THEME` so PR #1165's CRITICAL fix (Rich `MissingStyle`
    crash when `style="math"` was used) cannot regress.

### Fixed

- **CLI LaTeX ŰáîŰŹöŰžü ÔÇö `interactive_loop` wiring + `\[...\]`/`\(...\)`/
  `\begin{env}ÔÇŽ\end{env}` delimiter ýÂöŕ░Ç.** PR #1141 ýŁ┤ `core/ui/latex.py`
  ýŁś Tier 1 (pylatexenc) + Tier 2 (latex2sympy2 + sympy.pretty) ŰŁ╝ýŁ┤ŰŞî
  ŰčČŰŽČ + 19 test Űžî ýÂöŕ░ÇÝĽśŕ│á "ŰőĄýŁî ŰőĘŕ│ä ÝŤäŰ│┤ ÔÇö event_renderer ŕ░Ç LLM ýŁĹŰőÁ
  ÝůŹýŐĄÝŐŞýŚÉ extract_and_render_inline ýáüýÜę" ýť╝Űíť wiring ýŁä follow-up ýť╝Űíť
  ŰéĘŕ▓ĘŰĹÉýŚłýŁî. ŕ▓░ŕ│╝ýáüýť╝Űíť ýéČýÜęý×ÉŰŐö LLM ýŁĹŰőÁýŚÉýäť `\[ \frac{1}{m} \sum_{i=1}
  ^{m} \ell(\alpha_i) \]` ŕ░ÖýŁÇ raw LaTeX Űą╝ ŕĚŞŰîÇŰíť Ű│┤ŕ│á ý×łýŚłŰőĄ. Ű│Ş PR ýŁ┤
  ŰĹÉ ŕ░şýŁä ŰőźýŁî:
  - `core/cli/interactive_loop.py` ýŁś `_render_ipc_response` ŕ░Ç LLM final
    text Űą╝ `rich.markdown.Markdown` ýť╝Űíť ýžüýáĹ ÝŁśŰŽČŰŹś ŰÂÇŰÂäýŁä ýőáŕĚť
    `_render_text_with_latex` ÝŚČÝŹ╝Űíť ŕÁÉý▓┤. ÝŚČÝŹ╝ŰŐö
    `extract_and_render_inline(text)` Űíť segment ŰÂäÝĽá ÝŤä inline math ŰŐö
    rendered Unicode Űíť ýú╝Ű│Ç Markdown paragraph ýŚÉ ŰőĄýőť ÝĽęý╣śŕ│á,
    `block_math` ŰŐö multi-line block ýť╝Űíť render. math ŕ░Ç ýáäÝśÇ ýŚćýť╝Űę┤
    ŰőĘýŁ╝ Markdown ÝśŞýÂťŰíť fallback (ÝÜîŕĚÇ ýťäÝŚś 0).
  - `core/ui/latex.py` ýŁś delimiter ŕ░Ç `$...$` / `$$...$$` ŰĹÉ ŕ░ÇýžÇ Ű┐ÉýŁ┤ŰŁ╝
    LLM ýŁ┤ ý×Éýú╝ ýÂťŰáąÝĽśŰŐö `\[...\]` (display) / `\(...\)` (inline) /
    `\begin{equation|align|gather|multline|displaymath}ÔÇŽ\end{...}` ŕ░Ç
    Ű¬ĘŰĹÉ ŰłäŰŁŻ. Ű│Ş PR ýŁ┤ ýäŞ ÝîĘÝä┤ Ű¬ĘŰĹÉ ýžÇýŤÉÝĽśŰĆäŰíŁ regex ÝÖĽý×ą + overlap-
    aware ýÜ░ýäáýłťýťä resolution (block > inline) ýÂöŕ░Ç.
  - ýőáŕĚť test 13 (`tests/test_ui_latex.py::TestDelimiterExpansion` 7 +
    `tests/test_interactive_loop_latex.py` 6) ÔÇö Ű¬ĘŰôá delimiter form,
    mixed segments, overlap ÝÜîÝö╝, raw Ű░▒ýŐČŰ×śýőť leak ÝÜîŕĚÇ, ýéČýÜęý×Éŕ░Ç Ű│┤ŕ│áÝĽť
    `\[ \frac{1}{m} \sum_{i=1}^{m} \ell(\alpha_i) \]` ý╝ÇýŁ┤ýŐĄ ýžüýáĹ ŕ▓ÇýŽŁ.
  - ýŁśŰĆäŰÉť Ű╣äýžÇýŤÉ: backslash ýŚćŰŐö `[...]` / `(...)` ÔÇö markdown link
    ŰČŞŰ▓Ľŕ│╝ ýÂęŰĆî + ýŁ╝Ű░ś bracket ýľ┤Ýťś noise. ýéČýÜęý×ÉŰŐö `\[...\]` ÝśĽýőŁýŁä ýŹĘýĽ╝
    ÝĽĘ.
- **CLI LaTeX rendering ÔÇö `interactive_loop` wiring + `\[...\]`/`\(...\)`/
  `\begin{env}ÔÇŽ\end{env}` delimiter support.** PR #1141 introduced
  `core/ui/latex.py` with the Tier 1 (pylatexenc) and Tier 2
  (latex2sympy2 + sympy.pretty) renderers plus 19 tests, but the
  CHANGELOG flagged the actual wiring as a follow-up ÔÇö the response
  print path stayed on `rich.markdown.Markdown(text)`. Users therefore
  saw the raw backslash form (e.g. `\[ \frac{1}{m} \sum_{i=1}^{m}
  \ell(\alpha_i) \]`) in their terminals. This PR closes both gaps:
  - The LLM final-text branch of
    `core/cli/interactive_loop._render_ipc_response` now calls a new
    `_render_text_with_latex` helper. The helper splits the body via
    `extract_and_render_inline(text)`, folds inline math back into the
    surrounding Markdown paragraph as rendered Unicode, and renders
    `block_math` as a multi-line block. When the body has no math at
    all, it falls back to the single Markdown call (zero regression
    risk).
  - `core/ui/latex.py` only knew `$...$` and `$$...$$`. The new regex
    set adds the three forms LLMs actually emit ÔÇö `\[ÔÇŽ\]` for
    display, `\(ÔÇŽ\)` for inline, and `\begin{equation|align|gather|
    multline|displaymath}ÔÇŽ\end{...}` ÔÇö with overlap-aware priority
    resolution (block > inline) so an inline match inside a
    multi-line bracket block is not double-extracted.
  - 13 new tests
    (`tests/test_ui_latex.py::TestDelimiterExpansion` plus
    `tests/test_interactive_loop_latex.py`) pin every delimiter form,
    mixed segments, the overlap rule, the raw-backslash leak
    regression, and the user-reported case verbatim.
  - Deliberately not supported: bracket forms without backslashes
    (`[...]` / `(...)`) ÔÇö those collide with Markdown link syntax and
    ordinary parenthetical prose. Users must write `\[ÔÇŽ\]`.

## [0.95.1] ÔÇö 2026-05-16

### Infrastructure

- **`docs-link-audit` skill Űô▒ŰíŁ.** `scripts/check_docs_links.py` (PR #1161)
  Űą╝ 1ý░Ę ŰĆäŕÁČŰíť ÝĽśŰŐö workflow skill ýŁä `.claude/skills/docs-link-audit/
  SKILL.md` ýŚÉ ýÂöŕ░Ç. ŰÂäŰąś 4 ýóů (internal /docs / internal /other / anchor
  / external) ŰžĄÝĽĹ ÝĹť, link ÝîĘÝä┤ ýÂöýÂť ýáĽŕĚťýőŁ 2 ŕ░ť, ÝŐ╣ýŁ┤ ý▓śŰŽČ (`/geode/`
  basepath / build-time copy ýŁŞýžÇ / `${...}` unresolved / ýŐĄÝé┤ ýŐĄÝéÁ), exit
  code ŕŞ░Ű░ś CI guard, ý×śŰ¬╗ŰÉť link ýŁś 4 ÝŁöÝĽť ýŤÉýŁŞ (chapter ýéşýáť leftover /
  section ýŁ┤ýáä / slug typo / external rot), CI wiring ýśÁýůś 2 ýóů (pages.yml
  pre-build / ci.yml dispatch) Ű¬ĘŰĹÉ ýáĽŰŽČ. CLAUDE.md ýŁś Custom Skills ÝĹť
  ýŚÉŰĆä ÝŐŞŰŽČŕ▒░ ÝéĄýŤîŰôť ("broken link", "404", "docs link", "hyperlink",
  "ŰžüÝüČ ýáÉŕ▓Ç", "ŰžüÝüČ ŕ╣ĘýžÉ", "audit links", "link checker") Űô▒ŰíŁ. PR
  #1157 (3 broken ýáĽýáĽ) + PR #1161 (script ŰĆäý×ů) ýŁś ý╝ÇýŁ┤ýŐĄ ýŐĄÝä░Űöö ÝĆČÝĽĘ.
- **`docs-link-audit` skill registered.** Added
  `.claude/skills/docs-link-audit/SKILL.md` as a workflow skill around
  `scripts/check_docs_links.py` (PR #1161). Covers the 4-category map
  (internal /docs / internal /other / anchor / external), the 2
  regexes that drive link extraction, special handling (`/geode/`
  basepath, build-time copy awareness, `${...}` as unresolved, scheme
  skip list), exit-code-based CI guard semantics, four common
  root causes of broken links (chapter deletion leftover, section
  move, slug typo, external rot), and two CI wiring options (pages.yml
  pre-build vs ci.yml dispatch). CLAUDE.md Custom Skills table now
  carries the trigger keywords ("broken link", "404", "docs link",
  "hyperlink", "ŰžüÝüČ ýáÉŕ▓Ç", "ŰžüÝüČ ŕ╣ĘýžÉ", "audit links", "link
  checker"). Case studies from PR #1157 (3 broken corrected) + PR
  #1161 (script introduction) included.

- **`scripts/check_docs_links.py` ÔÇö docs ýéČýŁ┤ÝŐŞ ŰžüÝüČ ýáĽýáü + HTTP ýáÉŕ▓Ç
  ýŐĄÝüČŰŽŻÝŐŞ.** site/src ýŁś Ű¬ĘŰôá `.tsx`/`.ts` ýŚÉýäť Ű│ŞŰČŞ/JSX ŰžüÝüČ ÝîĘÝä┤ (
  `href="..."`, ``href={`...`}``, `src="..."`, `to="..."`, ŕĚŞŰŽČŕ│á markdown
  ýŐĄÝâÇýŁ╝ ŰžüÝüČ ÝĹťŕŞ░) ýŁä Ű¬ĘŰĹÉ ýÂöýÂť. 4 ŰÂäŰąś:
  - **internal /docs/...** ÔÇö `site/src/app/docs/` ÝĽśýťä `page.tsx` slug
    ýÖÇ ý░ĘýžĹÝĽę Ôćĺ ŰłäŰŁŻ ýőť broken
  - **internal /<other>...** ÔÇö `/portfolio`, `/works`, `/petri-bundle/`
    Űô▒ Ôćĺ app route + public asset + build-time copy (pages.yml ýŁś
    `docs/petri-bundle/` Ôćĺ `site/out/petri-bundle/` step ýŁŞýžÇ) ýÖÇ ŰîÇýí░
  - **anchor #section** ÔÇö ŕ░ÖýŁÇ page.tsx ýŁś `id="..."` ýÖÇ ŰîÇýí░
  - **external http(s)://** ÔÇö `--http` ýśÁÝŐŞýŁŞ ýőť HEAD/GET ýť╝Űíť reachability
    ŕ▓ÇýéČ (concurrent 8, 8s timeout, 200/3xx OK)
  CI ÝćÁÝĽę ýśÁýůś: `python3 scripts/check_docs_links.py` Űžîýť╝Űíť ýáĽýáü ŕ▓ÇýéČ
  ÝćÁŕ│╝ ýőť exit 0, broken Ű░ťŕ▓Č ýőť exit 1. ÝľąÝŤä pages.yml build job ýŁś
  pre-build step ŰśÉŰŐö Ű│ä ci.yml lint ýť╝Űíť wiring ŕ░ÇŰŐą.

  Ýśäý×Č ýŞíýáĽ (ýŁ┤ PR ýáüýÜę ÝŤä): 193 link ýŐĄý║ö, 0 broken, 17 external Ű¬ĘŰĹÉ
  reachable, 2 unresolved (markdown-lite.tsx ýŁś regex ÝîĘÝä┤ ŰČŞý×ÉýŚ┤, false
  positive ŰČ┤ýőť).
- **`scripts/check_docs_links.py` ÔÇö static + HTTP audit script for docs
  site links.** Extracts every link-shaped string from `site/src/**.tsx`
  / `.ts` (JSX `href="..."`, ``href={`...`}``, `src="..."`, `to="..."`,
  and markdown-style link syntax inside string literals). Classifies
  into four buckets:
  - **internal /docs/...** ÔÇö diffed against the `page.tsx` slug set
    under `site/src/app/docs/`; misses flagged.
  - **internal /\<other\>...** ÔÇö `/portfolio`, `/works`,
    `/petri-bundle/` etc., diffed against app routes + public assets +
    build-time copies (the script recognises the
    `docs/petri-bundle/` Ôćĺ `site/out/petri-bundle/` copy step in
    `.github/workflows/pages.yml`).
  - **anchor #section** ÔÇö checked against `id="..."` occurrences in
    the emitting `page.tsx`.
  - **external http(s)://** ÔÇö opt-in `--http` HEAD/GET probe
    (concurrency 8, 8 s timeout, 200/3xx considered OK).
  Returns exit 0 on a clean run and exit 1 if anything is broken, so
  the script can later be wired into the Pages build (pre-build step)
  or into the `ci.yml` lint job. Current measurement after this PR:
  193 link occurrences scanned, 0 broken, all 17 external URLs
  reachable, 2 false-positive "unresolved" entries from regex pattern
  strings in `markdown-lite.tsx` (informational, not failure).

    `build/` ý▒ĽÝä░ ýéşýáť ÝŤä ŰéĘýŁÇ leftover ý░Şýí░ 2 ýéČýŁ┤ÝŐŞ ÔÇö `run/analyze/page.tsx`
    L38, L65). ýőĄýáť ŰĆäŰęöýŁŞ ýÂöŕ░Ç ŰČŞýäťŰŐö `runtime/domains` ýŐČŰčČŕĚŞ.

  - `/docs/ops/observability` Ôćĺ `/docs/verification/observability`

    `verification/` ýä╣ýůś ÝĽśýťä ýŐČŰčČŕĚŞ.

  ÝâÉýžÇ Ű░ęŰ▓Ľ ÔÇö `grep` ýť╝Űíť `site/src/` ýŁś Ű¬ĘŰôá `href="(/docs/...)"`,
  `href={\`/docs/...\`}`, markdown style `](/docs/...)` ÝîĘÝä┤ 23 ŕ░ť ýÂöýÂť Ôćĺ
  `find site/src/app/docs -name "page.tsx"` ýŁś 50 ŕ░ť ýőĄý×Č ÝÄśýŁ┤ýžÇ ýŐČŰčČŕĚŞýÖÇ
  `comm -23` ýť╝Űíť ý░ĘýžĹÝĽę Ôćĺ 3 broken Ű░ťŕ▓Č. `npm run build` ýä▒ŕ│Á ÝŤä 6 ýéČýŁ┤ÝŐŞ
  ŕÁÉý▓┤. doc Ű│Çŕ▓Ż only, Ýľëýťä Ű│Çŕ▓Ż 0.

- **Docs site broken-link fix ÔÇö 3 paths (6 sites).** A static scan of
  internal links in `site/src/` found three routes that returned 404
  because the target slug either no longer exists (an earlier `build/`
  chapter was retired) or was renamed to another section:
  - `/docs/build/add-domain` Ôćĺ `/docs/runtime/domains`
    (`run/analyze/page.tsx` L38, L65).
  - `/docs/build/add-tool` Ôćĺ `/docs/runtime/tools/protocol`
    (`run/messaging/page.tsx` L35, L60).
  - `/docs/ops/observability` Ôćĺ `/docs/verification/observability`
    (`petri/run/page.tsx` L77, L146).

  Detection used `grep` against `site/src/` for all `href="(/docs/...)"`,
  ``href={`/docs/...`}``, and markdown-style `](/docs/...)` patterns
  (23 unique link targets) cross-referenced via `comm -23` against the
  50 page-slug paths under `site/src/app/docs/**/page.tsx`; the
  difference produced exactly 3 broken targets. `npm run build` passes
  after the 6 edits. Documentation only ÔÇö no behavioural impact.

### Added

- **Autoresearch real-mode runtime hook (`GEODE_WRAPPER_OVERRIDE`).**
  `core/llm/prompt_assembler.py` ýŁś `assemble()` ýŚÉ Phase 0 (Wrapper
  Override) ýÂöŕ░Ç. env var `GEODE_WRAPPER_OVERRIDE=<json-path>` ŕ░Ç set
  ŰÉśŰę┤ JSON ýŁä `dict[str, str]` Űíť ŰíťŰôťÝĽ┤ ŕĚŞ value ŰôĄýŁä concat ÝĽť ŕ▓░ŕ│╝Űíť
  `base_system` ýŁä ŰîÇý▓┤. ÝŤäýćŹ Phase (skill / memory / extra) ŰŐö ŕĚŞŰîÇŰíť
  ýáüýÜę. env unset ýŁÇ baseline ýŁä ýťáýžÇÝĽśýžÇŰžî, env ŕ░Ç set ŰÉť ŰĺĄ ÝîîýŁ╝ ŰłäŰŁŻ /
  malformed JSON / dict ýĽäŰőś / empty dict / non-string entry ŕ░Ç ŰéśýśĄŰę┤
  fail-closed `RuntimeError` Űíť real audit quota Űą╝ baseline prompt ýŚÉ
  ýô░ýžÇ ýĽŐŕ▓î ÝĽĘ. `autoresearch/train.py` ýŁś
  `WRAPPER_OVERRIDE_HOOK_READY` Űą╝ `True` Űíť flip ÝĽ┤ real-mode ÝÖťýä▒ÝÖö ÔÇö
  outer-loop agent ŕ░Ç `WRAPPER_PROMPT_SECTIONS` Űą╝ ýłśýáĽÝĽśŰę┤ `geode audit`
  ýŁś system prompt ŕ░Ç ýőĄýáťŰíť ŕĚŞ dict ýŁś Űé┤ýÜęýť╝Űíť ŰĆÖý×Ĺ. `.env.example` ýŚÉ
  `# GEODE_WRAPPER_OVERRIDE=` ÝĽşŰ¬ę + ýéČýÜę ýäĄŰ¬ů ýÂöŕ░Ç. ýőáŕĚť 9 pytest
  (`tests/test_prompt_assembler.py` ýŁś `TestWrapperOverrideHook` ÔÇö
  env-unset baseline / ýáĽýâü override / ÝîîýŁ╝ ŰłäŰŁŻ raise / malformed
  JSON raise / Ű╣ä-dict raise / empty dict raise / non-string entry raise /
  hash ŕ┤ÇýŞíýä▒ / extra ÝĽęýä▒)
  + train.py ýŁś fail-fast test Űą╝ real-mode subprocess argv/env ŕ▓ÇýŽŁ
  ýť╝Űíť ŕÁÉý▓┤ (mock subprocess, quota ýéČýÜę ýŚćýŁî).
- **Autoresearch real-mode runtime hook (`GEODE_WRAPPER_OVERRIDE`).**
  Adds Phase 0 (Wrapper Override) to `core/llm/prompt_assembler.py`'s
  `assemble()`. When `GEODE_WRAPPER_OVERRIDE=<json-path>` is set, the
  JSON is loaded as `dict[str, str]` and its values are concatenated to
  replace `base_system`; the remaining phases (skill / memory / extra)
  still apply on top. When the env is unset, baseline behavior is
  unchanged; once the env is set, missing files, malformed JSON,
  non-dict payloads, empty dicts, or non-string entries fail closed with
  `RuntimeError` so real audit quota is not spent on the baseline prompt.
  `autoresearch/train.py` flips
  `WRAPPER_OVERRIDE_HOOK_READY = True`, enabling real-mode runs ÔÇö the
  outer-loop agent's edits to `WRAPPER_PROMPT_SECTIONS` now actually
  reach the `geode audit` system prompt. `.env.example` documents the
  new optional variable. Nine new pytest cases in
  `tests/test_prompt_assembler.py::TestWrapperOverrideHook` (baseline /
  override / missing file raises / malformed JSON raises / non-dict raises /
  empty dict raises / non-string entries raise / hash observability /
  composition with bootstrap extras) plus
  the existing `tests/test_autoresearch_train.py` fail-fast test
  replaced by a real-mode subprocess argv/env assertion (subprocess
  mocked ÔÇö no LLM quota consumed).

- **Phase 1a ÔÇö Long-term Recall: messages table + dual-write.** Hermes
  ÝŁíýłś plan(`docs/plans/2026-05-14-hermes-strengths-absorption.md`) ýŁś ý▓ź
  PR. `sessions.db` ýŚÉ `messages` ÝůîýŁ┤ŰŞö (id / session_id / seq / role /
  content / tool_call_id / tool_calls / tool_name / timestamp /
  token_count / finish_reason / reasoning / metadata + `UNIQUE(session_id,
  seq)`) + `idx_messages_session` + `idx_messages_tool_name` ýőáýäĄ.
  `SessionCheckpoint.save()` ŕ░Ç JSON Ű│ŞŰČŞ ýáÇý×ą ýžüÝŤä
  `SessionManager.upsert_messages()` Űíť Ű│ŞŰČŞýŁä mirror ÔÇö JSON ýŁÇ Phase 1b
  ýŁś SoT ýáäÝÖśŕ╣îýžÇ authoritative. DB ýőĄÝîĘ ýőť WARN Űíťŕ╣ů + `exc_info=True`,
  JSON Ű│ŞŰČŞýŁÇ ŕĚŞŰîÇŰíť Ű│┤ýí┤ (graceful degradation). ŰĆÖýŁ╝/ýÂĽýćî/Ű╣ł message
  list ýŁś ý×ČýáÇý×ą Ű¬ĘŰĹÉ idempotent ÔÇö ýĄäýľ┤Űôá seq ýŁś stale row ýÖÇ Ű╣ł ýáÇý×ąýŁś
  ý×öýŚČ row ŕ╣îýžÇ ýáťŕ▒░ÝĽ┤ JSON Ôćö DB ŕ░Ç ÝĽşýâü ýáĽŰáČ. Anthropic content blocks
  (`tool_use` / `tool_result` / `thinking`) ýÖÇ OpenAI ÝśĽýőŁ (`tool_calls`
  / `tool_call_id` / `name`) ýľĹý¬Ż ýÂöýÂť + 18 ýőáŕĚť ÝůîýŐĄÝŐŞ (dual-write
  parity / sqlite ýőĄÝîĘ graceful / openai+anthropic ýÂöýÂť / stale row
  ýáťŕ▒░ / Ű╣ł ýáÇý×ą ýáĽÝĽę). Codex MCP cross-LLM verifier ŕ░Ç CRITICAL 2 ŕ▒┤
  (stale row + Ű╣ł-save ý×öý×Č) ýŁä Ű░ťŕ▓Č┬ĚŰ░śýśü.
- **Phase 1a ÔÇö Long-term Recall: messages table + dual-write.** First PR
  of the Hermes-absorption plan (`docs/plans/2026-05-14-hermes-strengths-
  absorption.md`). Adds a `messages` table to `sessions.db` (columns: id
  / session_id / seq / role / content / tool_call_id / tool_calls /
  tool_name / timestamp / token_count / finish_reason / reasoning /
  metadata + `UNIQUE(session_id, seq)`) plus `idx_messages_session` and
  `idx_messages_tool_name`. `SessionCheckpoint.save()` mirrors the full
  message list into the table right after the JSON write, via
  `SessionManager.upsert_messages()`; JSON remains SoT until Phase 1b
  flips the source. DB failures emit a WARNING with `exc_info=True` and
  leave the JSON checkpoint intact (graceful degradation). Re-saving the
  same, shorter, or empty message list is idempotent ÔÇö stale rows from a
  shrunk transcript and leftovers from an empty save are removed so JSON
  and the mirror stay aligned. The extractor reads both Anthropic content
  blocks (`tool_use` / `tool_result` / `thinking`) and OpenAI-style
  fields (`tool_calls` / `tool_call_id` / `name`). 18 new tests cover
  dual-write parity, a real `sqlite3.OperationalError` graceful path,
  OpenAI/Anthropic extraction, stale-row removal, and empty-save
  alignment. A Codex MCP cross-LLM verifier round caught two CRITICAL
  gaps (stale rows on shrink, leftovers on empty save), both fixed.

### Changed

- **Autoresearch outer-loop: Karpathy 3-file fork (Petri-signal domain).**
  PR #1145 ýŁś 6-module python stub (`loop.py` / `hypothesis.py` /
  `fitness.py` / `ratchet.py` / `rationale_extractor.py` /
  `baseline_marker.py`, 480 LOC, "follow-up PR1" ÝĹťŕŞ░Űíť implementation
  ŰîÇŕŞ░) ýŁä ÝĆÉŕŞ░ÝĽśŕ│á Karpathy
  [autoresearch](https://github.com/karpathy/autoresearch) (228791f, MIT,
  26K+ stars) ýŁś 3-file ÝîĘÝä┤ (`prepare.py` + `train.py` + `program.md`)
  ýť╝Űíť ý×ČŕÁČýä▒. ML ŰĆäŰęöýŁŞ (GPT pre-train + `val_bpb`) ýŁä GEODE alignment-
  audit ŰĆäŰęöýŁŞ (Petri seed pool + AlphaEval 5-axis fitness) ýť╝Űíť ýÁťýćî
  ŕÁÉý▓┤. Karpathy ýŤÉŰ│ŞýŁś single-mutation-file + fixed-budget + grep-friendly
  stdout + `results.tsv` 5-col + git-as-optimizer ýáĽýőá ýťáýžÇ.
  - `autoresearch/prepare.py` ÔÇö `~/.cache/autoresearch/` ýŁś fineweb ŰőĄýÜ┤
    ŰíťŰôť + tokenizer ÝĽÖýŐÁ ý×ÉŰŽČýŚÉ **fixed audit harness sanity check**
    (10 safe seed file count, 19-dim YAML rubric parse, `geode audit
    --help` reachability). `~/.cache` ŕ░Ç read-only ýŁ╝ ŰĽî worktree-local
    fallback.
  - `autoresearch/train.py` ÔÇö GPT ÝĽÖýŐÁ ý×ÉŰŽČýŚÉ **wrapper system-prompt
    section dict** (mutation target) + `geode audit` subprocess invocation
    (staged: `--seed-select` / `--dim-set` / `--live` / `--yes`) + 5-axis
    fitness extraction. Ýśäý×Č GEODE core ŕ░Ç `GEODE_WRAPPER_OVERRIDE` Űą╝
    ýĽäýžü consume ÝĽśýžÇ ýĽŐýť╝Ű»ÇŰíť real mode ŰŐö fail-fast (`RuntimeError`),
    `--dry-run` Űžî working mode ÔÇö runtime hook ýŁÇ follow-up PR.
  - `autoresearch/program.md` ÔÇö ML research direction ý×ÉŰŽČýŚÉ Petri
    direction (gen 0 hypothesis space, `input_hallucination` ╬ö +1.13 /
    `overrefusal` +0.31 / `broken_tool_use` Ôłĺ1.28 driver seed ýŁś prior,
    9 hypothesis = wrapper section ablation).
  - `autoresearch/README.md` ÔÇö Petri-signal fork ýŁś quick start.
  - `pyproject.toml` ýŁś `geode-research = "autoresearch.loop:cli"`
    entry-point ýáťŕ▒░ (Karpathy ýŤÉŰ│Ş ýáĽýőá: single-script `uv run python
    autoresearch/train.py`, CLI wrapping ýŚćýŁî).
  - 3 ýőáŕĚť pytest (`tests/test_autoresearch_train.py`) ÔÇö argv ŕ░Ç Ýśäý×Č
    `geode audit` flag Űžî ýéČýÜę + ýéČŰŁ╝ýžä flag ýŁś retro ÝÜîÝö╝ + real-mode
    fail-fast + dry-run baseline ýŁś fitness range ŕ▓ÇýŽŁ.
  - ŕĚŞŰŁ╝ýÜ┤Űöę reference clone: `~/workspace/autoresearch` (ŰőĘýłť clone,
    GEODE repo ýÖÇ git ýŚ░ŰĆÖ ýŚćýŁî).
  - Codex MCP cross-LLM verifier ŕ░Ç CRITICAL 1 + HIGH 4 ý×ÉŰĆÖ fix ÔÇö
    real-mode deception, obsolete CLI flag, rubric grep ýŁś stale ÝśĽýőŁ,
    `~/.cache` ŕÂîÝĽť fallback.
- **Autoresearch outer-loop: Karpathy 3-file fork (Petri-signal domain).**
  Retires the 6-module Python stub from PR #1145 (`loop.py` /
  `hypothesis.py` / `fitness.py` / `ratchet.py` / `rationale_extractor.py`
  / `baseline_marker.py`, 480 LOC, "follow-up PR1" placeholder) and
  rebases the outer loop on the Karpathy
  [autoresearch](https://github.com/karpathy/autoresearch) 3-file pattern
  (228791f, MIT, 26K+ stars). Domain swapped from ML pre-training
  (`val_bpb`) to GEODE alignment-audit (Petri seed pool + AlphaEval
  5-axis fitness), preserving Karpathy's single-mutation-file +
  fixed-budget + grep-friendly stdout + `results.tsv` 5-col +
  git-as-optimizer spirit.
  - `autoresearch/prepare.py` replaces fineweb download + tokenizer
    training with a **fixed audit harness sanity check** (seed file
    count, 19-dim YAML rubric parse, `geode audit --help` reachability),
    and falls back to a worktree-local report if `~/.cache` is read-only.
  - `autoresearch/train.py` replaces the GPT training loop with a
    **wrapper system-prompt section dict** (the agent's mutation surface)
    plus a `geode audit` subprocess invocation (staged: `--seed-select` /
    `--dim-set` / `--live` / `--yes`) and 5-axis fitness extraction.
    Until GEODE core consumes `GEODE_WRAPPER_OVERRIDE`, real mode
    fail-fasts and only `--dry-run` is non-deceptive; the runtime hook
    lands in a follow-up PR.
  - `autoresearch/program.md` replaces the ML research direction with a
    Petri direction (gen-0 hypothesis space drawn from yesterday's
    driver seeds: `input_hallucination` ╬ö +1.13 / `overrefusal` +0.31 /
    `broken_tool_use` Ôłĺ1.28 across three model families, pruning the
    space to nine wrapper-section ablations).
  - `autoresearch/README.md` ships a Petri-signal fork quick-start.
  - `pyproject.toml` drops the `geode-research = "autoresearch.loop:cli"`
    entry-point ÔÇö Karpathy keeps the runner as a plain `python train.py`,
    so the fork does too.
  - Three new pytest tests (`tests/test_autoresearch_train.py`) pin the
    current `geode audit` flag set, block obsolete flags from creeping
    back in, assert the real-mode fail-fast, and check the dry-run
    fitness range.
  - Grounding reference clone: `~/workspace/autoresearch` (a plain
    clone, not wired into the GEODE repo).
  - Codex MCP acted as a cross-LLM verifier and applied one CRITICAL fix
    plus four HIGH fixes ÔÇö real-mode deception, obsolete CLI flags,
    stale rubric grep, and the `~/.cache` permission fallback.

### Fixed

- **Autoresearch Petri scaffold verifier fixes.** `prepare.py` now parses the
  19-dimension YAML rubric instead of grepping for a stale `- name:` shape,
  falls back to a workspace-local prepare report when `~/.cache` is not
  writable, and `train.py` fail-fast blocks real audit mode until GEODE core
  actually consumes `GEODE_WRAPPER_OVERRIDE`. The staged live argv now matches
  the current `geode audit` CLI (`--seed-select`, `--dim-set`, `--live`,
  `--yes`) instead of obsolete `--rubric` / `--budget-minutes` flags.
- **Autoresearch Petri scaffold ŕ▓ÇýŽŁ ýłśýáĽ.** `prepare.py` ŕ░Ç ýśĄŰ×śŰÉť
  `- name:` ÝśĽýőŁ grep ŰîÇýőá 19-dim YAML rubric ýŁä ýžüýáĹ parse ÝĽśŕ│á,
  `~/.cache` ýŚÉ ýôŞ ýłś ýŚćýŁä ŰĽî worktree-local prepare report Űíť fallback
  ÝĽęŰőłŰőĄ. `train.py` ŰŐö GEODE core ŕ░Ç `GEODE_WRAPPER_OVERRIDE` Űą╝ ýőĄýáťŰíť
  consume ÝĽśŕŞ░ ýáäŕ╣îýžÇ real audit mode Űą╝ fail-fast Űíť ŰžëýĽä, wrapper mutation
  ýŁ┤ ýáüýÜęŰÉśŰŐö ŕ▓âý▓śŰč╝ Ű│┤ýŁ┤ŰŐö ý░ęýőťŰą╝ ýáťŕ▒░ÝľłýŐÁŰőłŰőĄ. staged live argv ŰĆä Ýśäý×Č
  `geode audit` CLI ýŁś `--seed-select`, `--dim-set`, `--live`, `--yes` ýŚÉ
  Űž×ýĚäýŐÁŰőłŰőĄ.

- **Verification 5-Layer label fix ÔÇö `Confidence Gate` Ôćĺ `Calibration`.**
  Audit of `core/verification/` revealed that the README's "5-Layer
  Verification" cell (`G1-G4 + BiasBuster + Cross-LLM + Confidence Gate +
  Rights Risk`) was inaccurate. The true layer 5 is
  `core/verification/calibration.py` (its docstring spells out "Swiss Cheese
  Layer 5: orthogonal to G1-G4 (structural), BiasBuster (cognitive),
  Cross-LLM (inter-model). Calibration validates against external expert
  consensus"). What the README called "Confidence Gate" is actually the
  confidence multiplier `(1 - CV) ├Ś 100` inside `plugins/game_ip/nodes/
  scoring.py:301` ÔÇö a scoring sub-routine, not a verification layer.
  Grounded layer map:
  - **Layer 1 (structural)** ÔÇö `guardrails.py` `_g1_schema` (L13),
    `_g2_range` (L47), `_g3_grounding` (L90), `_g4_consistency` (L148)
  - **Layer 2 (cognitive)** ÔÇö `biasbuster.py:43` `run_biasbuster`,
    4-step RECOGNIZE Ôćĺ EXPLAIN Ôćĺ ALTER Ôćĺ EVALUATE
  - **Layer 3 (inter-model)** ÔÇö `cross_llm.py:81` `run_cross_llm_check`
    + `stats.py` Krippendorff ╬▒
  - **Layer 4 (legal)** ÔÇö `rights_risk.py:79` `check_rights_risk`
  - **Layer 5 (Ground Truth)** ÔÇö `calibration.py:328` `run_calibration`,
    expert-annotated Golden Set comparison
  README and README.ko peer comparison `Multi-layer guardrails` cell and
  `What's inside` table both fixed (`Confidence Gate` Ôćĺ `Calibration`).
  Each layer now carries the semantic label parenthetical.

### Infrastructure

- **Petri raw-archive analyzer + safe10 seed pool.** `scripts/petri_analyze.py`
  ŰŐö `docs/petri-bundle/logs/` ýŁś zstd inspect-ai archive Űą╝ ýŁŻýľ┤ 17 dim ├Ś
  archive matrix / 3-family pair ╬ö / per-sample long-form CSV Űą╝ ýâŁýä▒ÝĽśŰŐö
  read-only ŰÂäýäŁŕŞ░. LLM/ŰäĄÝŐŞýŤîÝüČ ÝśŞýÂť ýŚćýŁî. `plugins/petri_audit/seeds_safe10/`
  ŰŐö 5 base seed ├Ś 2 paraphrase (calibration / reasoning / efficiency /
  research / reasoning_critique) = 10 ýĽłýáä seed pool ÔÇö gpt-5.5 ýŁś cancellation
  scenario ÝÜîÝö╝ + ŰőĄýŁî generation ýŁś baseline. ýé░ŰČ╝ docs ŰŐö
  `docs/audits/2026-05-15-petri-*` (insights / matrix / pairs / per-sample /
  summary) + `docs/audits/eval-logs/2026-05-15-*.summary.yaml` ýŁś 2 archive
  ýÜöýĽŻ + `docs/audits/2026-05-15-autoresearch-gen0-plan.md` (autoresearch
  Generation 0 ýŁś ý▓ź outer loop plan) + `docs/plans/2026-05-14-hermes-
  strengths-absorption.md` (Hermes ÝŁíýłś 4-Phase plan).
- **Petri raw-archive analyzer + safe10 seed pool.** `scripts/petri_analyze.py`
  is a read-only analyzer that reads zstd inspect-ai archives from
  `docs/petri-bundle/logs/` and emits a 17-dim ├Ś archive matrix, a 3-family
  paired ╬ö matrix, and a per-sample long-form CSV (no LLM/network calls).
  `plugins/petri_audit/seeds_safe10/` ships a 5 base ├Ś 2 paraphrase = 10
  safe seed pool (calibration / reasoning / efficiency / research /
  reasoning_critique) that side-steps the gpt-5.5 cancellation scenarios
  and serves as the next-generation baseline. The audit docs land at
  `docs/audits/2026-05-15-petri-*` (insights / matrix / pairs / per-sample
  / summary), `docs/audits/eval-logs/2026-05-15-*.summary.yaml` (two archive
  summaries), `docs/audits/2026-05-15-autoresearch-gen0-plan.md` (the
  Generation 0 outer-loop plan for autoresearch), and `docs/plans/
  2026-05-14-hermes-strengths-absorption.md` (the four-phase Hermes
  absorption plan).
