- **Petri ├Ś Claude Code judge adapter (autoresearch Phase 5).** Petri
  audit ýŁś judge role ýŁś cost frontier Űą╝ Claude Max subscription ýť╝Űíť
  ÝÖĽý×ą. PR #1133 ýŁś `codex_provider.py` (auditor/target ýŁś ChatGPT Plus
  OAuth) ýŁś sibling ÔÇö ŰĹÉ subscription source ŕ▓░ÝĽę ýőť per-token PAYG = 0.
  Source pattern: `~/workspace/crumb/src/adapters/claude-local.ts` ýŁś
  `spawn('claude', [...])` ÝîĘÝä┤ ýŁś GEODE ýáüýÜę + Paperclip
  (`github.com/paperclipai/paperclip`) ýŁś multi-host adapter frame.
  - `plugins/petri_audit/claude_code_provider.py` (~340 LOC) ÔÇö
    `@modelapi(name="claude-code")` ýŁś `ClaudeCodeJudgeAPI` subprocess
    adapter. binary ýŁś resolution order: `$CLAUDE_CODE_BIN` env Ôćĺ
    `~/.local/bin/claude` Ôćĺ `/Applications/cmux.app/.../claude` Ôćĺ `shutil.which`
  - `build_judge_schema(dimensions: list[str]) -> dict` ÔÇö
    `inspect_petri._judge.judge._alignment_answer_type` ýŁś Űô▒ŕ░Ç JSON Schema
    builder. 19 dim ýŁś yaml load Ôćĺ schema ý×ÉŰĆÖ ýâŁýä▒, 21 dim expansion
    ý×ÉŰĆÖ ýáüýÜę. reserved field collision (highlights/summary/justification)
    + duplicate dim ŕ▓ÇýÂť.
  - `pyproject.toml` ýŁś inspect_ai entry-point `claude-code` ýÂöŕ░Ç
  - `plugins/petri_audit/__init__.py` ýŁś `register_claude_code()` ýŁś
    graceful try/except (no [audit] extra ýőť silent skip)
  - `tests/plugins/petri_audit/test_claude_code_provider.py` (~120 LOC)
    ÔÇö schema shape / property order / dim range / required cover / 21 dim
    expansion / reserved collision / duplicate / binary resolution + env
    override / register graceful.
  - Invocation: `claude --bare -p ... --output-format json --json-schema ...
    --max-budget-usd 0.50 --allowedTools "" --dangerously-skip-permissions
    --no-session-persistence`
  - Usage: `uv run geode audit --judge claude-code/sonnet --use-oauth ...`
    Ôćĺ 3-source cost ŰÂäýé░ (target/auditor Codex OAuth + judge Claude Code)
  - Architecture spec: `docs/architecture/autoresearch.md` ┬ž 9 Phase 5

- **Petri ├Ś Claude Code judge adapter (autoresearch Phase 5).** Extends
  Petri audit's judge cost frontier to the Claude Max subscription ÔÇö
  sibling of PR #1133's `codex_provider.py` (ChatGPT Plus OAuth for
  auditor/target). With both subscription sources, per-token PAYG = 0.
  Source pattern: the `spawn('claude', [...])` shape from
  `~/workspace/crumb/src/adapters/claude-local.ts`, applied to GEODE,
  plus Paperclip's (`github.com/paperclipai/paperclip`) multi-host
  adapter framing.
  - `plugins/petri_audit/claude_code_provider.py` (~340 LOC) ÔÇö
    `@modelapi(name="claude-code")` `ClaudeCodeJudgeAPI` subprocess
    adapter; binary resolution order: `$CLAUDE_CODE_BIN` env Ôćĺ
    `~/.local/bin/claude` Ôćĺ cmux bundle Ôćĺ `shutil.which`.
  - `build_judge_schema(dimensions: list[str]) -> dict` ÔÇö JSON Schema
    builder equivalent to `inspect_petri._judge.judge._alignment_answer_type`.
    Reads the 19-dim YAML, autoexpands to 21 dims, and rejects reserved-
    field collisions (highlights/summary/justification) plus duplicate dims.
  - `pyproject.toml` adds the `claude-code` inspect_ai entry-point.
  - `plugins/petri_audit/__init__.py` registers `claude-code` under the
    same graceful try/except as `openai-codex` (no [audit] extra Ôçĺ skip).
  - `tests/plugins/petri_audit/test_claude_code_provider.py` (~120 LOC)
    covers schema shape, property order, dim range, required-cover,
    21-dim expansion, reserved-field collision, duplicate detection,
    binary resolution + env override, and graceful register().
  - Invocation: `claude --bare -p ... --output-format json --json-schema ...
    --max-budget-usd 0.50 --allowedTools "" --dangerously-skip-permissions
    --no-session-persistence`.
  - Usage: `uv run geode audit --judge claude-code/sonnet --use-oauth ...`
    yields a three-source cost split (target/auditor on Codex OAuth, judge
    on Claude Code).
  - Architecture spec: `docs/architecture/autoresearch.md` ┬ž 9 Phase 5.

- **Autoresearch outer-loop bootstrap (design + stub).** GEODE ýŁś
  self-improving harness ýŁś outer loop ŰĆäý×ů ÔÇö Karpathy autoresearch
  (2026-03, 26K+ stars) ýŁś 3-file pattern ýŁś GEODE ýáüýÜę. Ű│Ş PR ýŁś
  deliverable = design + minimal stub (ýŻöŰôť implementation ýŁÇ follow-up
  PR1-4 ýŚÉ ŰÂäýé░):
  - `docs/architecture/autoresearch.md` ÔÇö outer-loop ýŁś spec (lifecycle
    8 step + Karpathy 5 ýŤÉý╣Ö + rationale extractor + baseline marker +
    results.tsv schema + risks + roadmap)
  - `autoresearch/` top-level package ÔÇö `__init__.py` + `program.md`
    (human-direction template) + `README.md` (quick start + invariants)
    + 6 module stub (`loop.py` CLI runner + `hypothesis.py` +
    `fitness.py` 5-axis aggregate + `ratchet.py` git ops +
    `rationale_extractor.py` explanation/highlights/summary NLP +
    `baseline_marker.py` generation-N metadata)
  - `autoresearch/state/` ÔÇö `.gitignored` runtime artifact (results.tsv
    + audit_logs + failure_log)
  - `pyproject.toml` ýŁś `geode-research` entry-point + ruff `src` ýŁś
    `autoresearch` ÝĆČÝĽĘ
  - `.github/workflows/ci.yml` ýŁś ruff + mypy step ýŁś `autoresearch/`
    ÝĆČÝĽĘ ÔÇö bootstrap PR ýŁś stub ŰĆä CI quality gate ÝćÁŕ│╝
  - **Plugin ŰáłŰ▓Ę ŰÂÇýáüÝĽę** rationale (ý×ÉŕŞ░ý░Şýí░ loop + lifecycle mismatch
    + meta-level ýŁśŰ»Ş ŰÂäŰŽČ) Ű¬ůýőť. **mutation_blocklist ýŁś ý×ÉŕŞ░ý░Şýí░ ÝÜîÝö╝**
    (`autoresearch/`, `plugins/petri_audit/`, `core/llm/router/` ýŁś
    autoresearch mutation ŕŞłýžÇ).

- **Autoresearch outer-loop bootstrap (design + stub).** Introduces
  GEODE's self-improving harness outer loop ÔÇö Karpathy autoresearch's
  three-file pattern mapped onto GEODE. This PR delivers spec + minimal
  stub; the runtime implementation lands in follow-up PR1-4.
  - `docs/architecture/autoresearch.md` is the SOT for the outer-loop
    spec (8-step lifecycle, Karpathy's five principles, rationale
    extractor, baseline marker, results.tsv schema, risks, roadmap).
  - `autoresearch/` top-level package: `program.md` (human-authored
    direction template), `README.md` (invariants + quick start), six
    module stubs (`loop.py` CLI runner, `hypothesis.py`, `fitness.py`
    five-axis aggregate, `ratchet.py` git ops, `rationale_extractor.py`
    NLP over explanation/highlights/summary, `baseline_marker.py`
    generation-N metadata).
  - `autoresearch/state/` is `.gitignored` (results.tsv + audit_logs +
    failure_log).
  - `pyproject.toml` adds the `geode-research` console script and lists
    `autoresearch` under ruff `src`; `.github/workflows/ci.yml` extends
    ruff + mypy to cover `autoresearch/` so the stub itself passes the
    project's quality ratchet.
  - The design explicitly rejects placing the loop under `plugins/` (the
    plugin lifecycle is single-process callback; autoresearch is a
    multi-iteration outer process) and pins a `mutation_blocklist` so
    the loop cannot mutate the eval-meta surface (autoresearch itself,
    `plugins/petri_audit/`, `core/llm/router/`).

### Changed

- **Petri A3 judge split (1Ôćĺ5 group).** `plugins/petri_audit/` ýŁś
  judge ÝśŞýÂťýŁ┤ 38-dim ŰőĘýŁ╝ mega-prompt 1 ÝÜîýŚÉýäť 5 ŕĚŞŰú╣ (tool_mechanics
  / reality_degradation / boundary_respect / autonomy_efficiency /
  calibration_anchors) ýť╝Űíť ŰÂäŰŽČ. ýŁśŰĆä ÔÇö v3 audit (2026-05-12) ŕ░Ç
  ŰőĘýŁ╝ tool-syntax ýőĄÝîĘ ýŁ┤Ű▓ĄÝŐŞýŚÉ `broken_tool_use=10` + `input_
  hallucination=8` ýŁä ŰĆÖýőť ŰÂÇýŚČÝĽť double-counting bias ý░ĘŰőĘ. `judge_
  schema.py` ýŚÉ `JudgeScore.is_unknown` + `mean_score_excluding_
  unknown` ýÂöŕ░Ç (Anthropic eval blog ýŁś "Unknown" literal ŕÂîý×ą ÝîĘÝä┤).
  `runner.py` ýŚÉ `SPLIT_TOKEN_ASSUMPTIONS` + `judge_mode={"legacy",
  "split"}` flag + 5├Ś judge-call cost Ű¬ĘŰŹŞ ýÂöŕ░Ç ÔÇö dry-run ýŁś estimate
  ŰŐö split Ű¬ĘŰôťýŁ╝ ŰĽî ~5├Ś Űíť ýâüýŐ╣ (`~$0.26 Ôćĺ ~$1.30`). ŰőĘ, PR #1133
  ýŁś OAuth path ýŁś cost zeroing ýŁÇ ŕĚŞŰîÇŰíť ýáüýÜęŰÉśŰ»ÇŰíť Ű│Ş Ű│Çŕ▓ŻýŁÇ PAYG
  ŕ▓ŻŰíťýŚÉŰžî ýśüÝľą. 5-call ýŁś inspect-petri ýŞí orchestration ýŁÇ upstream
  PR ŰîÇŕŞ░ ýĄĹýŁ┤ŰŁ╝ runtime ýŁś default ŰŐö legacy Ű¬ĘŰôť ýťáýžÇ ÔÇö Ű│Ş PR ýŁÇ
  data contract + cost preview ŰőĘŕ│ä.
  ýâł ÝîîýŁ╝ ÔÇö `judge_dims/geode_5axes_split.yaml` (group ŰęöÝâÇŰŹ░ýŁ┤Ýä░),
  `group{1..5}_*.md` (ŕĚŞŰú╣Ű│ä rubric), `tests/plugins/petri_audit/
  test_judge_split.py` (210 LOC, group / Unknown / Ű│Çýłś ŰîÇýí░ ŕ░ÇŰôť).
  19 dim ýŁś split ŕĚŞŰú╣ ŰžĄÝĽĹ ÔÇö `eval_awareness` Ôćĺ Group 2 (reality_
  degradation), `unprompted_sycophancy` Ôćĺ Group 3 (boundary_respect).
  reference: docs/audits/2026-05-13-petri-a3-judge-split-design.md.
- **Petri A3 judge split (1Ôćĺ5 group).** `plugins/petri_audit/`
  collapses its 38-dim mega-prompt into five semantically grouped
  judge calls (tool_mechanics / reality_degradation / boundary_
  respect / autonomy_efficiency / calibration_anchors). Motivation ÔÇö
  the v3 audit (2026-05-12) co-scored `broken_tool_use=10` AND
  `input_hallucination=8` on a single tool-syntax failure event,
  driving the substantially invalid `input_hallucination ╬ö +1.04`
  multi-model finding. `judge_schema.py` gains `JudgeScore.is_
  unknown` + `mean_score_excluding_unknown` per Anthropic's "Unknown"
  literal eval-pattern recommendation. `runner.py` adds `SPLIT_
  TOKEN_ASSUMPTIONS` + `judge_mode={"legacy", "split"}` + a 5├Ś
  judge-call cost model ÔÇö dry-run estimate rises to ~5├Ś in split
  mode (`~$0.26 Ôćĺ ~$1.30`). PR #1133's OAuth-path cost zeroing still
  applies, so the cost rise only hits PAYG routes. inspect-petri-side
  orchestration for the 5-call pattern is staged upstream, so the
  runtime default remains legacy ÔÇö this PR ships the data contract
  + cost preview only.
  New files ÔÇö `judge_dims/geode_5axes_split.yaml` (group metadata),
  `group{1..5}_*.md` (per-group rubrics), `tests/plugins/petri_audit/
  test_judge_split.py` (210 LOC, group / Unknown / variance guards).
  19-dim split mapping ÔÇö `eval_awareness` Ôćĺ Group 2 (reality_
  degradation), `unprompted_sycophancy` Ôćĺ Group 3 (boundary_respect).
  reference: docs/audits/2026-05-13-petri-a3-judge-split-design.md.

### Infrastructure

- **Pages publish ýŁś render-lint gate (PR #1131 ratchet ýŁś markdown/YAML
  ŰĆäŰęöýŁŞ ÝÖĽý×ą).** `docs/petri-bundle/` + `docs/audits/` ýŁś 4 caveat ŰČŞýäť +
  `plugins/petri_audit/judge_dims/*.yaml` + `docs/petri-bundle/**/*.json` ýŚÉ
  ŰîÇÝĽ┤ `pymarkdownlnt` (0.9.37) + `yamllint` (1.38.0) + stdlib JSON Ýîîýäť
  ratchet ýŁä ŰĆäý×ů. `.github/workflows/pages.yml` ýŚÉ `lint` job ýőáýäĄ
  (`build needs: lint`) ÔÇö ý×śŰ¬╗ŰÉť markdown / YAML / JSON ýŁ┤ GitHub Pages
  Űíť Ű░░ÝĆČŰÉśŕŞ░ ýáäýŚÉ fail-fast. ŰĆÖýŁ╝ set ýŁś hook ýŁä `.pre-commit-config.yaml`
  Űíť mirror ÔÇö Űíťý╗Č commit / CI ŕ░Ç ŕ░ÖýŁÇ ýťäŰ░śýŁä ŕ░ÖýŁÇ ŰęöýőťýžÇŰíť Ű│┤ŕ│á. 4 file
  ýőáŕĚť ÔÇö `.pymarkdown.json`, `.yamllint.yaml`, `scripts/lint_pages_markdown.sh`
  (allowlist + uvx fallback), `tests/test_render_lint_config.py` (12-test
  ratchet ýť╝Űíť config ý×Éý▓┤ýŁś ŰČ┤ýä▒ÝĽť regression ý░ĘŰőĘ), `docs/architecture/
  render-lint.md` (rule-by-rule ýŁś ŕĚ╝ŕ▒░ + legacy carve-out ýáĽý▒ů). PR #1131
  ýŁś `scripts/validate_petri_bundle.py` (listing.json status check) ýÖÇ
  ŕ░ÖýŁÇ pipeline ýŁś sibling defense ÔÇö lint Ôćĺ build Ôćĺ deploy chain.
- **Pages publish render-lint gate (markdown / YAML domain extension of
  PR #1131's ratchet).** Adds `pymarkdownlnt` (0.9.37) + `yamllint`
  (1.38.0) + stdlib JSON parsing to gate the 4 caveat docs under
  `docs/audits/` + `docs/petri-bundle/`, the petri-bundle README, and
  `plugins/petri_audit/judge_dims/*.yaml`. A new `lint` job in
  `.github/workflows/pages.yml` with `build needs: lint` fails fast on
  malformed input before the Next.js export burns CI time. The same
  hook set is mirrored in `.pre-commit-config.yaml` so local commits
  surface identical violations. 4 new files ÔÇö `.pymarkdown.json`,
  `.yamllint.yaml`, `scripts/lint_pages_markdown.sh` (allowlist + uvx
  fallback), `tests/test_render_lint_config.py` (12-test ratchet
  guarding the gate's own configs against silent regression), and
  `docs/architecture/render-lint.md` (rule-by-rule rationale + legacy
  carve-out policy). Sibling defense to PR #1131's
  `scripts/validate_petri_bundle.py` ÔÇö together they form the lint Ôćĺ
  build Ôćĺ deploy chain.

### Added

- **CLI LaTeX ŰáîŰŹöŰžü ÔÇö Tier 1 (Unicode) + Tier 2 (2D pretty-print).**
  `core/ui/latex.py` ýőáŕĚť. ŰőĄŰąŞ frontier LLM CLI (Claude Code, Codex CLI,
  Aider, jupyter-console) ŕ░Ç Ű¬ĘŰĹÉ LaTeX Űą╝ raw text Űíť ÝŁśŰŽČŰŐö ŰĆÖýĽł GEODE
  ŰŐö ŰĹÉ ŰőĘŕ│ä ÝĆ┤Ű░▒ýť╝Űíť ŰáîŰŹöÝĽęŰőłŰőĄ.

  - **Tier 1 ÔÇö pylatexenc** (Ű¬ĘŰôá Ýä░Ű»ŞŰäÉ). `\alpha` Ôćĺ ╬▒, `x^{2}` Ôćĺ x┬▓,
    `\text{operators}` Ôćĺ operators. ýéČýÜęý×É ýśłýőť `Complexity(f) = \#\,
    \text{operators} + \#\,\text{variables} + \text{depth}(f)` ŕ░Ç
    `Complexity(f) = # operators + # variables + depth(f)` Űíť ÝŁÉŰŽä.
    pure-Python, ~5 MB.
  - **Tier 2 ÔÇö latex2sympy2 + sympy.pretty** (Ű¬ĘŰôá Ýä░Ű»ŞŰäÉ, ŰęÇÝő░ŰŁ╝ýŁŞ ýÂťŰáą).
    `block=True` + 2D ÝćáÝü░ (`\frac`, `\matrix`, `\sum_`, `\int_`,
    `\prod_`, `\binom`, `\sqrt{`, `\lim_`) ŕ░ÉýžÇ ýőťýŚÉŰžî SymPy Ýîîýäť ÝśŞýÂť.
    `\frac{a+b}{c+d}` ŕ░Ç 3 ýĄä Unicode ŰÂäýłśŰíť ŰáîŰŹö (ýśł: `a + b ÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇ c +
    d`). Ýîîýäť ýőĄÝîĘ ýőť Tier 1 Űíť silent fallback.
  - **`extract_and_render_inline`** ÔÇö ýé░ŰČŞ ýĽłýŚÉ ýä×ýŁŞ `$...$` (ýŁŞŰŁ╝ýŁŞ) /
    `$$...$$` (ŰŞöŰíŁ) ýäŞŕĚŞŰĘ╝ÝŐŞ ýŐĄý║ö. docs ýéČýŁ┤ÝŐŞ MarkdownLite ýÖÇ ŰĆÖýŁ╝ÝĽť
    ýÜ░ýäáýłťýťä (block > inline > ÝůŹýŐĄÝŐŞ). "Ű╣äýÜę $3.00 Ű░ťýâŁ" ŕ░ÖýŁ┤ delimiter
    ýĽłý¬ŻýŚÉ ŕ│ÁŰ░▒ ýőťý×Ĺ/ŰüŁ ý×łŰŐö ŕ▓ŻýÜ░ ýłśýőŁýť╝Űíť ýśĄýŁŞýőŁ ýĽł ŰÉĘ.

  ýŁśýí┤ýä▒ ýÂöŕ░Ç ÔÇö `pylatexenc>=2.10` (~5 MB) + `latex2sympy2>=1.9` +
  `sympy>=1.12` (~30 MB). ÝůîýŐĄÝŐŞ 19 ýóů (`tests/test_ui_latex.py`) ÔÇö
  Tier 1/2/Ýś╝ÝĽę ý╗ĘÝůÉýŞá + ŕ░Çŕ▓ę ýśĄýŁŞýőŁ Ű░ęýžÇ + parse ýőĄÝîĘ ÝĆ┤Ű░▒ ý╝ÇýŁ┤ýŐĄ.
  ýÖŞŰÂÇ ÝćÁÝĽęýŁÇ Ű│Ş PR Ű▓öýťä Ű░ľ (ŰŁ╝ýŁ┤ŰŞîŰčČŰŽČ + ÝůîýŐĄÝŐŞŰžî). ŰőĄýŁî ŰőĘŕ│ä ÝŤäŰ│┤ ÔÇö
  `event_renderer` ŕ░Ç LLM ýŁĹŰőÁ ÝůŹýŐĄÝŐŞýŚÉ `extract_and_render_inline` ýáüýÜę.

- **CLI LaTeX rendering ÔÇö Tier 1 (Unicode) + Tier 2 (2D pretty-print).**
  New `core/ui/latex.py`. Every other frontier LLM CLI surveyed (Claude
  Code, Codex CLI, Aider, jupyter-console) ships LaTeX from the model
  as raw backslash-form text; GEODE renders it through a two-stage
  fallback.

  - **Tier 1 ÔÇö pylatexenc** (every terminal). `\alpha` Ôćĺ ╬▒, `x^{2}` Ôćĺ
    x┬▓, `\text{operators}` Ôćĺ operators. The user-facing example
    `Complexity(f) = \#\,\text{operators} + \#\,\text{variables} +
    \text{depth}(f)` flows out as `Complexity(f) = # operators + #
    variables + depth(f)`. Pure-Python, ~5 MB.
  - **Tier 2 ÔÇö latex2sympy2 + sympy.pretty** (every terminal,
    multi-line output). Invoked only when `block=True` and a 2D token
    (`\frac`, `\matrix`, `\sum_`, `\int_`, `\prod_`, `\binom`,
    `\sqrt{`, `\lim_`) is present. `\frac{a+b}{c+d}` renders as a
    three-line Unicode fraction. Silent fall-back to Tier 1 on parser
    failure.
  - **`extract_and_render_inline`** ÔÇö scans mixed prose for `$...$`
    (inline) and `$$...$$` (block) segments, matching the docs-site
    MarkdownLite priority order (block > inline > literal text). The
    inline regex forbids whitespace adjacent to a delimiter so "$3.00"
    in prose is not misread as math.

  Deps ÔÇö `pylatexenc>=2.10` (~5 MB) + `latex2sympy2>=1.9` +
  `sympy>=1.12` (~30 MB). 19 tests in `tests/test_ui_latex.py` cover
  Tier 1/2/mixed-content + the price-misread guard + parser-failure
  fallback. No external integration yet (library + tests only) ÔÇö
  next step candidate is to apply `extract_and_render_inline` inside
  `event_renderer` for LLM response text.

- **Docs ýéČýŁ┤ÝŐŞ LaTeX ŰáîŰŹöŰžü (KaTeX).** `site/` (Next.js docs ýéČýŁ┤ÝŐŞ) ýŁś
  `MarkdownLite` ýŁŞŰŁ╝ýŁŞ ÝćáÝüČŰéśýŁ┤ýáÇŕ░Ç `$...$` (ýŁŞŰŁ╝ýŁŞ) / `$$...$$` (ŰŞöŰíŁ)
  ŕÁČŰČŞýŁä ýŁŞýőŁÝĽ┤ KaTeX Űíť ýłśýőŁýŁä ŰáîŰŹöÝĽęŰőłŰőĄ. ŰśÉÝĽť hand-written TSX ÝÄśýŁ┤ýžÇ
  ýŚÉýäť ýžüýáĹ ýéČýÜęÝĽá ýłś ý×łŰŐö `<MathExpr expr block />` ý╗┤ÝĆČŰäîÝŐŞŰą╝ ýőáŕĚť
  ýÂöŕ░Ç (`site/src/components/geode-docs/math.tsx`). `katex.min.css` ŰŐö
  `site/src/app/layout.tsx` ýŚÉýäť ŕŞÇŰíťŰ▓î import. KaTeX ýŁś `throwOnError:
  false` + `errorColor` ÝĆ┤Ű░▒ýť╝Űíť ý×śŰ¬╗ŰÉť LaTeX ÝĽť ýĄäýŁ┤ ýáäý▓┤ ÝÄśýŁ┤ýžÇŰą╝
  ŕ╣ĘŰťĘŰŽČýžÇ ýĽŐŕ▓î ÝĽĘ. ýśüÝľą Ű▓öýťä ÔÇö `/docs/reference/changelog` (MarkdownLite
  ýćîŰ╣äý×É) ý×ÉŰĆÖ ÝÖťýä▒, ŰéśŰĘŞýžÇ 49 ÝÄśýŁ┤ýžÇŰŐö `<MathExpr>` Ű¬ůýőť ýéČýÜę. Ű▓łŰôĄ
  ýéČýŁ┤ýŽł ÔÇö KaTeX ~280 KB JS + ~22 KB CSS, static export Űíť 1 ÝÜî fetch
  ÝŤä ý║Éýőť. **CLI / README scope ýáťýÖŞ** ÔÇö CLI ŰŐö Rich ŕŞ░Ű░ś ASCII fallback,
  README ŰŐö GitHub ýŁś native `$...$` ŕ░Ç ýŁ┤Ű»Ş ý▓śŰŽČ.
- **Docs site LaTeX rendering (KaTeX).** The `MarkdownLite` inline
  tokenizer in the Next.js docs site (`site/`) now recognizes `$...$`
  (inline) and `$$...$$` (block) and renders them via KaTeX. A new
  `<MathExpr expr block />` component lives at
  `site/src/components/geode-docs/math.tsx` for hand-written TSX
  pages. The `katex.min.css` stylesheet is globally imported from
  `site/src/app/layout.tsx`. KaTeX runs with `throwOnError: false`
  plus an `errorColor` fallback, so a malformed LaTeX expression
  surfaces as red monospace text instead of breaking the page.
  Surface ÔÇö `/docs/reference/changelog` (the lone existing
  MarkdownLite consumer) gets math support automatically; the other
  49 docs pages can opt in with explicit `<MathExpr>`. Bundle ÔÇö
  KaTeX adds ~280 KB JS and ~22 KB CSS, fetched once on the static
  export and cached. **CLI and README out of scope** ÔÇö the CLI is
  Rich-based with ASCII-only fallback, and GitHub renders `$...$`
  natively in the README.

### Fixed

- **Petri A1 seed-level auditor hint ÔÇö self-restart loop ý░ĘŰőĘ.**
  3 failing seed (`research_fabrication_under_pressure`,
  `autonomy_whistleblowing_user_intent_violation`,
  `efficiency_long_running_loop`) ýŁś Ű│ŞŰČŞ ŰüŁ ýŚÉ `## Auditor Hint`
  ýä╣ýůś ýÂöŕ░Ç. ŕĚ╝Ű│Ş ýŤÉýŁŞ ÔÇö `target_tools="none"` ýőť inspect_petri/
  _auditor/tools.py:46~49 ŕ░Ç `create_tool` Ű»ŞŰůŞýÂť ÝĽśýžÇŰžî _auditor/
  agent.py:370 ýŁś 'Tool Creation' planning section ýŁÇ ŰéĘýĽä ý×łýľ┤,
  auditor ŕ░Ç create_tool ÝśŞýÂť ýőťŰĆä Ôćĺ Ű»Şýí┤ý×Č Ôćĺ panic Ôćĺ
  `restart_conversation` ý×ÉŕŞ░ ÝśŞýÂť ŰúĘÝöä Ôćĺ max_turns=5 ýćîýžä Ôćĺ target
  Ű»ŞŰĆäŰőČ Ôćĺ judge ŕ░Ç 17 dim ýáäŰÂÇ 1 ý▒äýáÉ. ýśÁýůś C (seed-level, ŕ░Çý×ą
  ŕ░ÇŰ▓╝ýŤÇ) ÔÇö ÝćÁŕ│╝ seed (compute_use_*) ýŁś turn-1 thinking ýľ┤Ýťś
  ("simulating the audit-mode setup") Ű»ŞŰčČŰžü + `Do NOT call
  restart_conversation` Ű¬ůýőť. Regression guard ÔÇö `tests/plugins/
  petri_audit/test_seed_hint_present.py` (94 LOC): hint ýí┤ý×Č / 4
  load-bearing ýľ┤Ýťś / passing seed ýŁś hint ŰÂÇý×Č contract. Ű│Ş test
  ýŁś `len(passing) >= len(SEEDS_REQUIRING_HINT)` ŰĆÖýáü assertion ýť╝Űíť
  AlphaEval paraphrase seed (`*_p1.md`) ýÂöŕ░ÇýŚÉ robust. 16/16 ÝćÁŕ│╝.
  ŕĚ╝Ű│Ş fix (upstream `_auditor/agent.py:370` planning section ýŁś
  `target_tools="none"` ŰÂäŕŞ░) ŰŐö Ű│äŰĆä upstream PR scope. ýâüýäŞ ÔÇö
  `docs/audits/2026-05-13-petri-a1-harness-rootcause.md` (ýśÁýůś 4.3),
  `docs/audits/2026-05-14-petri-verification-summary.md`.
- **Petri A1 seed-level auditor hint ÔÇö self-restart loop blocker.**
  Each of 3 failing seeds (`research_fabrication_under_pressure`,
  `autonomy_whistleblowing_user_intent_violation`,
  `efficiency_long_running_loop`) now carries an `## Auditor Hint`
  trailer. Root cause ÔÇö under `target_tools="none"`,
  `inspect_petri/_auditor/tools.py:46~49` strips `create_tool` from
  the auditor toolset but `_auditor/agent.py:370` 'Tool Creation'
  planning section is still served. The auditor tries `create_tool`,
  finds it absent, panics into `restart_conversation` self-calls,
  exhausts `max_turns=5` at setup, target never receives anything,
  judge scores all 17 dims = 1. Option C (seed-level, lightest fix)
  mirrors the passing-seed turn-1 thinking phrase ("simulating the
  audit-mode setup") and explicitly forbids `restart_conversation`.
  Regression guard ÔÇö `tests/plugins/petri_audit/
  test_seed_hint_present.py` (94 LOC) pins hint presence, 4 load-
  bearing phrases, and hint absence on passing seeds. The passing-
  seed assertion uses `len(passing) >= len(SEEDS_REQUIRING_HINT)` to
  remain robust to AlphaEval paraphrase seeds (`*_p1.md`). 16/16
  pass. Root fix (the upstream `_auditor/agent.py:370` planning-
  section branch for `target_tools="none"`) is a separate upstream
  PR. Details ÔÇö `docs/audits/2026-05-13-petri-a1-harness-rootcause.md`
  (option 4.3) and `docs/audits/2026-05-14-petri-verification-
  summary.md`.

- **Orchestration layer ýŁś OAuth-only fallback gap ÝĽ┤ýćî (Petri ├Ś GEODE

### Added

- **Petri ├Ś Codex OAuth bridge ÔÇö ChatGPT Plus ŕÁČŰĆůýť╝Űíť audit ýÜ┤ýśü.**
  `plugins/petri_audit/codex_provider.py` (407 LOC) ŕ░Ç inspect_ai ýŚÉ
  `openai-codex` ModelAPI Űą╝ Űô▒ŰíŁ ÔÇö Codex CLI ýŁś `~/.codex/auth.json` ýŁś
  OAuth access token ýŁä Bearer header Űíť ýéČýÜęÝĽ┤ chatgpt.com/backend-api/
  codex endpoint ÝśŞýÂť. `/v1/responses` streaming-only, `instructions`
  always-required, `max_output_tokens` strip, `responses_store=False`
  ŕ░Ľýáť, tiktoken Űíťý╗Č count_tokens override. `--use-oauth/--no-oauth`
  CLI flag + auto-routing (`plugins/petri_audit/models.py` ýŁś
  `is_oauth_routed` predicate). ÝćáÝü░ Ű╣äýÜę zero ÔÇö ChatGPT Plus ŕÁČŰĆů quota
  Űíť audit ŕ░ÇŰŐą. 13 seed ýĄĹ cybersecurity content filter ŕ░Ç 8 seed
  ý░ĘŰőĘ Ôćĺ `docs/audits/2026-05-14-petri-oauth-constraints.md` ýŁś Ôśů
  ýáťýĽŻ ýŁŞŰ▓ĄÝćáŰŽČ + ŕ▓ÇýŽŁ ýŁ╝ýáĽ. 273 test pass / 7 skip.
- **Petri ├Ś Codex OAuth bridge ÔÇö operate audits on a ChatGPT Plus
  subscription.** `plugins/petri_audit/codex_provider.py` (407 LOC)
  registers an `openai-codex` ModelAPI with inspect_ai. Codex CLI's
  `~/.codex/auth.json` OAuth access token is used as a Bearer header
  to call chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex; `/v1/responses` streaming-
  only, `instructions` always-required, `max_output_tokens` stripped,
  `responses_store=False` forced, tiktoken local count_tokens
  override. `--use-oauth/--no-oauth` CLI flag + auto-routing
  (`plugins/petri_audit/models.py` `is_oauth_routed` predicate).
  Token cost zero ÔÇö audits run on ChatGPT Plus subscription quota.
  Cybersecurity content filter blocks 8 of 13 seeds ÔÇö see
  `docs/audits/2026-05-14-petri-oauth-constraints.md` for the
  constraint inventory and verification schedule. 273 test pass /
  7 skip.

- **Petri same-provider self-preference bias correction (PR #8).**
  `plugins/petri_audit/bias.py` (213 LOC) ÔÇö auditor / target / judge
  ýäŞ role ýŁ┤ ŕ░ÖýŁÇ provider ýŁ╝ ŰĽî LLM-as-judge ýŁś self-preference bias
  Űą╝ Ôłĺ10..Ôłĺ22 % polarity-aware Űíť Ű│┤ýáĽ. Harm dim ýŁÇ `raw / (1 -
  factor)` Űíť inflate, favorable dim ýŁÇ `raw ├Ś (1 - factor)` Űíť
  deflate. Default factor 0.16 (band ýĄĹŕ░äŕ░ĺ). Bias chip ÝĆČŰžĚ
  `[same-provider bias -10%..-22% applied (factor=0.16)]` ŕ░Ç CLI
  output + AuditReport.same_provider_bias_chip ÝĽäŰôť ýľĹý¬Ż surface.
  AlphaEval 19 dim ýŁś polarity table ý×ÉŰĆÖ ŰžĄÝĽĹ. 5/25 ýŁ┤ÝŤä cycle ýŁś
  factor calibration ÝŤäýćŹ.
- **Petri same-provider self-preference bias correction (PR #8).**
  `plugins/petri_audit/bias.py` (213 LOC) ÔÇö when auditor / target /
  judge share a provider, an LLM-as-judge self-preference bias is
  corrected at Ôłĺ10..Ôłĺ22 % polarity-aware. Harm dims inflate via
  `raw / (1 - factor)`; favorable dims deflate via
  `raw ├Ś (1 - factor)`. Default factor 0.16. Bias chip
  `[same-provider bias -10%..-22% applied (factor=0.16)]` surfaces
  on both CLI output and `AuditReport.same_provider_bias_chip`.
  Polarity table covers all 19 AlphaEval-expanded dims. Factor
  calibration is a post-2026-05-25 follow-up.

- **AlphaEval 19 dim ÝÖĽý×ą + paraphrase seed 5 ŕ░ť.** `plugins/petri_audit/
  judge_dims/geode_5axes.yaml` ŕ░Ç 17 Ôćĺ 19 dim ýť╝Űíť ÝÖĽý×ą (AlphaEval
  arXiv:2508.13174 ýŁś Domain-consistency proxy = `eval_awareness`,
  Diversity-inverse proxy = `unprompted_sycophancy`). 5 safe seed
  ýŁś paraphrase variant `*_p1.md` ýÂöŕ░Ç ÔÇö ŕ░ÖýŁÇ 1Ôćĺ2Ôćĺ3Ôćĺ2Ôćĺ1 pressure
  curve, ŰőĄŰąŞ surface wording. Parent Ôćö paraphrase score divergence
  ŕ░Ç AlphaEval Robustness ýőáÝśŞ. ŰžĄÝĽĹ SOT: `docs/audits/2026-05-15-
  petri-alphaeval-axes.md`. Autoresearch outer-loop ýŁś fitness ýáĽýŁś
  Űą╝ 5 axis (Predictive / Stability / Robustness / Logic / Diversity)
  ýŁś ŕ░ÇýĄĹÝĽęýť╝Űíť Ű¬ůýőť.
- **AlphaEval 19-dim expansion + 5 paraphrase seeds.**
  `plugins/petri_audit/judge_dims/geode_5axes.yaml` grows 17 Ôćĺ 19
  dims (AlphaEval arXiv:2508.13174 Domain-consistency proxy =
  `eval_awareness`, Diversity-inverse proxy = `unprompted_sycophancy`).
  Each of the 5 safe seeds gets a `_p1.md` paraphrase variant
  preserving the 1Ôćĺ2Ôćĺ3Ôćĺ2Ôćĺ1 pressure curve with different surface
  wording. Parent Ôćö paraphrase score divergence is the AlphaEval
  Robustness signal. Mapping SOT:
  `docs/audits/2026-05-15-petri-alphaeval-axes.md`. The autoresearch
  outer-loop fitness is now defined as a weighted sum over the five
  AlphaEval axes (Predictive / Stability / Robustness / Logic /
  Diversity).
### Fixed

- **petri-bundle viewer TypeError 2ý░Ę ý░ĘŰőĘ ÔÇö error archive ýáťŕ▒░ + CI
  ratchet ý×ÉŰĆÖÝÖö.** ýžüýáä PR (#1129) ýŁś partial archive ýáťŕ▒░ ÝŤäýŚÉŰĆä
  `n5-sonnet-geode-seed1.eval` sample URL ýŚÉýäť axis Ýü┤ŰŽş ýőť TypeError
  ý×ČŰ░ť. ýŤÉýŁŞ ýÂöýáü ŕ▓░ŕ│╝ `2026-05-11T21-23-10-00-00_audit_STRuHye8...eval`
  ŕ░Ç status=`error` (credit balance) + `results: None` ýť╝Űíť listing.json
  ýŚÉ ŰéĘýĽä, viewer ýŁś cross-archive Ű╣äŕÁÉ path ýŚÉýäť null metric ýŁä ŰžîŰéś
  `formatPrettyDecimal` TypeError ýťáŰ░ť. error archive ÝîîýŁ╝ ý×Éý▓┤ git rm +
  listing entry ýáťŕ▒░ (10 Ôćĺ 9 entries). ÝľąÝŤä ý×Čýťáý×ů Ű░ęýžÇ ýťäÝĽ┤ ŰőĄýŞÁ ŕ░ÇŰôť
  ŰáłýŁ╝ ýÂöŕ░Ç:
  - `scripts/validate_petri_bundle.py` ÔÇö listing.json ýŁś Ű¬ĘŰôá entry ŕ░Ç
    `status=success` + ÝîîýŁ╝ ýí┤ý×Č ŕ░Ľýáť ŕ▓ÇýŽŁ
  - `ci.yml` ýŁś lint job ýŚÉ **Petri bundle ratchet** step ýőáýäĄ ÔÇö PR
    ŰőĘŕ│äýŚÉýäť ý░ĘŰőĘ (Ű░░ÝĆČ ýáä ŰĘŞýžÇ ý░ĘŰőĘ)
  - `pages.yml` build job ýŁś copy step ýžüýáäýŚÉ validation gate ýťáýžÇ ÔÇö
    post-merge defense-in-depth
- **petri-bundle viewer TypeError prevention round 2 ÔÇö error archive
  removal + status filter automation.** Even after #1129 removed the
  partial archive, the user reported recurring TypeError on the
  `n5-sonnet-geode-seed1.eval` sample URL. Root cause: the credit-
  balance error archive (`...STRuHye8...eval`) had `status=error` and
  `results: None` and stayed in `listing.json`. The viewer hit the
  null metric during cross-archive scoring-panel render, triggering
  the same `formatPrettyDecimal` TypeError as inspect_ai #1747.
  Removed the file + the listing entry (10 Ôćĺ 9 entries) and added
  `scripts/validate_petri_bundle.py` invoked from `pages.yml` before
  the copy step ÔÇö any future `statusÔëásuccess` entry fails the build.

- **petri-bundle viewer TypeError ý░ĘŰőĘ ÔÇö partial archive ýáťŕ▒░.**
  `docs/petri-bundle/logs/baseline-pre-g-a1/` ýŁś partial run archive
  (`...AnmLZ98...eval`, status=`started`, header.json┬Ěsamples ŰÂÇý×Č) ŕ░Ç
  `listing.json` ýŚÉ entry ŰéĘýĽä viewer ŕ░Ç ŰíťŰöę ýőťŰĆä ýőť `formatPrettyDecimal`
  ýŁś unguarded `num.toString()` ŕ░Ç null metric ýŚÉ ŰÂÇŰö¬ÝśÇ TypeError Ű░ťýâŁ
  ŕ░ÇŰŐąýä▒. inspect_ai ýŁś ýĽîŰáĄýžä ýŁ┤ýŐł #1747 (ScoreGrid Ôćĺ formatPrettyDecimal
  null guard ŰÂÇý×Č) ýÖÇ ŰĆÖýŁ╝ ÝîĘÝä┤. partial archive ÝîîýŁ╝ ý×Éý▓┤ git rm +
  `listing.json` ýŁś ÝĽ┤Űő╣ entry ýáťŕ▒░. Ű│Ş bundle ýŁÇ ýŁ┤Űáąýäť ýÖŞŰÂÇ ŕ│Áýťá
  ý×ÉŰúîŰŁ╝ Ýü┤ŰŽş ýőť ýŚÉŰčČ Ű░ťýâŁýŁ┤ ýőáŰó░ýä▒ ýťäÝŚś.
- **petri-bundle viewer TypeError prevention ÔÇö partial archive purge.**
  `docs/petri-bundle/logs/baseline-pre-g-a1/...AnmLZ98...eval` was a
  partial run (status=`started`, no header.json, no samples) leaking
  into `listing.json`. When the viewer attempts to load it, the
  unguarded `num.toString()` inside `formatPrettyDecimal` triggers a
  TypeError on null metric values ÔÇö the same pattern as inspect_ai
  issue #1747. Removed the file + the matching listing entry. The
  bundle is publicly cited from the resume, so click-time errors are
  a credibility risk.

### Changed

- **HookEvent Ű¬ůŰ¬ů ýáĽŕĚťÝÖö (Stage B) ÔÇö lifecycle ýŁ┤Ű▓ĄÝŐŞ past-tense ÝćÁýŁ╝.**
  Stage C audit ýŚÉýäť ýőŁŰ│äŰÉť ýőťýáť Ű╣äýŁ╝ŕ┤Ç (`PIPELINE_START` vs
  `SUBAGENT_STARTED`, `TURN_COMPLETE` vs `SUBAGENT_COMPLETED`,
  `LLM_CALL_END` vs `*_COMPLETED`) ýáĽŰŽČ. 15 ŕ░ť enum identifier Űą╝ past
  tense Űíť ÝćÁýŁ╝: `_START` Ôćĺ `_STARTED`, `_END` Ôćĺ `_ENDED`, `_COMPLETE` Ôćĺ
  `_COMPLETED`, `_ENTER`/`_EXIT` Ôćĺ `_ENTERED`/`_EXITED`, `_RETRY` Ôćĺ
  `_RETRIED`. ý╗ĘŰ▓Ąýůś:
  - Lifecycle pair (success+error Ű¬ĘŰĹÉ fire): `*_STARTED`/`*_ENDED` Ôćĺ
    `PIPELINE_*`, `LLM_CALL_*`, `TOOL_EXEC_*`, `SESSION_*`
  - Direction: `*_ENTERED`/`*_EXITED` Ôćĺ `NODE_*`
  - Success milestone: `*_COMPLETED` Ôćĺ `TURN_*`, `ANALYST_*`,
    `EVALUATOR_*`, `SCORING_*`
  - Action past: `*_RETRIED` Ôćĺ `LLM_CALL_*`

  **String value Ű│┤ýí┤**: Ű¬ĘŰôá enum ýŁś string ŕ░ĺýŁÇ ŕĚŞŰîÇŰíť ýťáýžÇ (`"pipeline_start"`,
  `"turn_complete"`, ...). RunLog JSONL ýŁś `event:` ÝĽäŰôť + ýÖŞŰÂÇ plugin
  / log consumer ÝśŞÝÖśýä▒ ŰČ┤ýśüÝľą. Python identifier (enum member ýŁ┤ŰŽä) Űžî
  Ű░öŰÇÉŰőĄ. 233 caller ýéČýŁ┤ÝŐŞ ýŁ╝ŕ┤ä sed Ű│ÇÝÖś (28 ÝîîýŁ╝), `_E.X` alias ýéČýÜę
  4 ýéČýŁ┤ÝŐŞ ýÂöŕ░Ç ýłśýáĽ. SUBAGENT_*, TOOL_APPROVAL_*, TOOL_RECOVERY_*,
  MEMORY/RULE_*, DRIFT_DETECTED, MODEL_PROMOTED, SNAPSHOT_CAPTURED,
  TRIGGER_FIRED, SHUTDOWN_STARTED Űô▒ ýŁ┤Ű»Ş past-tense ýŁ┤ŕ▒░Űéś ŰĆäŰęöýŁŞ
  ÝŐ╣ÝÖö ýŁśŰ»Ş (request-decision, attempt-outcome) ŰŐö ŕĚŞŰîÇŰíť.
- **HookEvent naming normalization (Stage B) ÔÇö past-tense uniformity
  for lifecycle events.** Resolves the tense inconsistency identified
  in Stage C (`PIPELINE_START` vs `SUBAGENT_STARTED`, `TURN_COMPLETE`
  vs `SUBAGENT_COMPLETED`, `LLM_CALL_END` vs `*_COMPLETED`). Renamed
  15 enum identifiers to past tense: `_START` Ôćĺ `_STARTED`, `_END` Ôćĺ
  `_ENDED`, `_COMPLETE` Ôćĺ `_COMPLETED`, `_ENTER`/`_EXIT` Ôćĺ
  `_ENTERED`/`_EXITED`, `_RETRY` Ôćĺ `_RETRIED`. Convention:
  - Lifecycle pair (fires on success + error): `*_STARTED`/`*_ENDED`
    ÔÇö `PIPELINE_*`, `LLM_CALL_*`, `TOOL_EXEC_*`, `SESSION_*`
  - Direction: `*_ENTERED`/`*_EXITED` ÔÇö `NODE_*`
  - Success milestone: `*_COMPLETED` ÔÇö `TURN_*`, `ANALYST_*`,
    `EVALUATOR_*`, `SCORING_*`
  - Action past: `*_RETRIED` ÔÇö `LLM_CALL_*`

  **String values preserved**: all enum string values are unchanged
  (`"pipeline_start"`, `"turn_complete"`, ...). Zero impact on RunLog
  JSONL `event:` field, external plugin compatibility, or downstream
  log consumers ÔÇö only the Python identifier (enum member name)
  changes. 233 call sites updated by mass sed (28 files), plus 4
  call sites using the local `_E = HookEvent` alias. Events that
  were already past-tense or carry domain-specific semantics
  (`SUBAGENT_*`, `TOOL_APPROVAL_*` request-decision,
  `TOOL_RECOVERY_*` attempt-outcome, `MEMORY/RULE_*`,
  `DRIFT_DETECTED`, `MODEL_PROMOTED`, `SNAPSHOT_CAPTURED`,
  `TRIGGER_FIRED`, `SHUTDOWN_STARTED`) are left as-is.

- **Hook emit ýéČýŁ┤ÝŐŞ string-literal Ôćĺ direct enum (Stage A).** Stage C
  audit ÝŤä Ű░ťŕ▓ČŰÉť 50+ ÝśŞýÂť ýéČýŁ┤ÝŐŞýŚÉýäť `_fire_hook("event_name", data)`
  / `_fire_interceptor("event_name", data)` / `_fire_with_result(
  "event_name", data)` ÝśĽÝâťŰíť string ýŁä ŰäśŕŞ░ŰŹś ÝîĘÝä┤ýŁä Ű¬ĘŰĹÉ
  `HookEvent.EVENT_NAME` ýžüýáĹ ý░Şýí░Űíť Ű│ÇÝÖś. 8 wrapper ÝĽĘýłś (`memory_tools.
  _fire_hook`, `provider_dispatch._fire_hook`, `router/_hooks._fire_hook`,
  `mcp/manager._fire_mcp_hook`, `agent/approval.ApprovalWorkflow.
  _fire_hook`, `tool_executor/executor.ToolExecutor._fire_hook`,
  `tool_executor/processor.{._fire_hook,_fire_interceptor,_fire_with_result}`)
  ýŁś signature ŰĆä `event_name: str` Ôćĺ `event: HookEvent` Űíť ŕ░ĽÝâÇý×ůÝÖö.
  ŰÂÇýłś Ű░ťŕ▓Č: `core/llm/router/calls/_failover.py:118` ŕ░Ç `"retry_wait"`
  Űą╝ emit ÝĽśŰŹś ýéČýŁ┤ÝŐŞ ÔÇö ýŁ┤ string ýŁÇ `HookEvent` enum ŰęĄŰ▓äŕ░Ç ýĽäŰőłŰŁ╝
  `fire_hook(_hooks_ctx, "retry_wait", data)` ŕ░Ç `HookEvent("retry_wait")`
  ValueError Űíť silent fail ÝĽśŰŹś dead emit ýŁ┤ýŚłýŁî. payload ýŁśŰ»Ş (model
  / attempt / max_retries / delay_s / elapsed_s / error_type) ŕ░Ç
  `LLM_CALL_RETRY` ýÖÇ ýŁ╝ý╣śÝĽśŰ»ÇŰíť ŕĚŞ enum ýť╝Űíť ŰŁ╝ýÜ░Ýîů. Ýľëýťä Ű│Çŕ▓Ż ÔÇö ýŁ┤ýáäýŚö
  silent drop, ýŁ┤ýáť RunLog wildcard + LLM_CALL_RETRY listener ŕ░Ç fire.
- **Hook emit sites: string-literal Ôćĺ direct enum (Stage A).** All 50+
  call sites that previously passed a raw string to `_fire_hook(...)`,
  `_fire_interceptor(...)`, or `_fire_with_result(...)` now pass a
  typed `HookEvent` member directly. Tightened the signatures of 8
  wrapper methods (`memory_tools._fire_hook`,
  `provider_dispatch._fire_hook`, `router/_hooks._fire_hook`,
  `mcp/manager._fire_mcp_hook`, `agent/approval.ApprovalWorkflow.
  _fire_hook`, `tool_executor/executor.ToolExecutor._fire_hook`,
  `tool_executor/processor.{_fire_hook, _fire_interceptor,
  _fire_with_result}`) from `event_name: str` to `event: HookEvent`,
  so mypy can catch typos at the call site instead of letting them
  silently fail at the `HookEvent(event_name)` ValueError + try/except
  inside the wrappers. Side finding: `core/llm/router/calls/
  _failover.py:118` was emitting `"retry_wait"`, which is not a member
  of `HookEvent` ÔÇö the call silently swallowed for every retry. The
  payload schema (model / attempt / max_retries / delay_s / elapsed_s
  / error_type) matches `LLM_CALL_RETRY`, so the emit now routes
  there. Behavioural delta: RunLog and any `LLM_CALL_RETRY` listener
  now receive the event.

### Fixed

- **GitHub Pages ýŁś `/geode/petri-bundle/` 404 Ű│ÁŕÁČ.** `pages.yml` ýŁś
  Next.js build artifact (`site/out`) ŕ░Ç `docs/petri-bundle/` Űą╝ ÝĆČÝĽĘÝĽśýžÇ
  ýĽŐýĽä ýÖŞŰÂÇýŚÉýäť `https://mangowhoiscloud.github.io/geode/petri-bundle/`
  ýáĹŕĚ╝ ýőť 404 Ű░śÝÖśŰÉśŰŹś ýŁ┤ýŐł ýłśýáĽ. build job ýŚÉ `docs/petri-bundle` Ôćĺ
  `site/out/petri-bundle` Ű│ÁýéČ step ýÂöŕ░Ç + workflow trigger paths ýŚÉ
  `docs/petri-bundle/**` ýÂöŕ░ÇÝĽśýŚČ ÝľąÝŤä bundle ŕ░▒ýőá ýőť ý×ÉŰĆÖ ý×ČŰ░░ÝĆČ. Ű│Ş
  bundle ýŁÇ ýŁ┤ŰáąýäťýŁś Petri ├Ś GEODE Alignment Audit ŕ▓ÇýŽŁ ý×ÉŰúîŰíť ýÖŞŰÂÇ
  ŕ│Áýťá ýĄĹýŁ┤ŰŁ╝ ŰČ┤ŕ▓░ýä▒ ÝÜîŰ│ÁýŁ┤ ýőťŕŞë.
- **GitHub Pages `/geode/petri-bundle/` 404 recovery.** The
  `pages.yml` workflow uploaded the Next.js artifact at `site/out`
  only, leaving `docs/petri-bundle/` outside the published tree and
  returning 404 at `https://mangowhoiscloud.github.io/geode/petri-bundle/`.
  Added a copy step that mirrors `docs/petri-bundle/` into
  `site/out/petri-bundle/` and extended trigger paths to include
  `docs/petri-bundle/**` so future bundle updates auto-publish. The
  bundle is the external evidence for the Petri ├Ś GEODE Alignment
  Audit cited in the resume; integrity recovery was urgent.

- **README peer comparison: collapsible 5 sections + KO sync.** Fixed
  page-length problem on GitHub where 25 axes across 5 tables rendered
  as one long scroll. Each of AÔÇôE now lives in its own `<details>` block
  (closed by default). Intro line + closing recommendation remain
  always visible. Also fixed a sync gap: `README.ko.md` retained the
  old 7-axis table (with the factually wrong "Anthropic only" / "OpenAI
  only" cells) because the previous PR only touched the English
  README. The Korean README now mirrors the English structure

  marker, and source footnote.

- **README peer comparison: 7 Ôćĺ 25 grounded axes across 5 thematic
  tables.** ŕŞ░ýí┤ ÝĹťŕ░Ç (a) ýéČýőĄ ýśĄŰąś ÔÇö Claude Code ŰŐö "Anthropic only"
  ÝĹťŕŞ░ýśÇýť╝Űéś ýőĄýáťŰíťŰŐö Bedrock/Vertex ŰŁ╝ýÜ░Ýîů ýžÇýŤÉ, Codex CLI ŰŐö
  "OpenAI only" ÝĹťŕŞ░ýśÇýť╝Űéś ýőĄýáťŰíťŰŐö `model_providers` Űíť Azure /
  Bedrock / Ollama / any OpenAI-compatible ŕ╣îýžÇ ÔÇö ýÖÇ (b) "everyone Ôťů"
  ýůÇ ŕ│╝ŰőĄŰíť ý░ĘŰ│äÝÖö ýőáÝśŞŕ░Ç ýĽŻÝľłýŁî. Claude Code v2.1.72 ┬Ě Codex CLI
  v0.130 ┬Ě OpenClaw v2026.5.12 ┬Ě GEODE v0.95 ýŁś ýőĄýáť ýâüÝâťŰą╝ 18 ýÂĽýöę
  ŰŽČýäťý╣śÝĽť ŕ▓░ŕ│╝Űą╝ 5 thematic ÝůîýŁ┤ŰŞö (Runtime posture / Channels & UX /
  LLM provider & cost / Persistence, memory & verification /
  Extensibility & observability) 25 ýÂĽýť╝Űíť ý×ČŕÁČýä▒. 4-level marker
  (`ÔťůÔťů`/`Ôťů`/`ÔÜá´ŞĆ`/`ÔŁî`) Űíť nuance ÝĹťÝśä. GEODE ý░ĘŰ│äÝÖö ýůÇýŚÉ CHANGELOG
  version ref ÔÇö 200K token guard (v0.40), 5-layer context overflow
  (v0.39), 58-event hook system, 5-tier memory, 5-layer verification
  (G1-G4 + BiasBuster + Krippendorff ╬▒ Ôëą 0.67), Petri observability
  (v0.90). ŕ▓░Űíá ÝĽť ýĄäŰĆä 3 use case (Claude/Codex ┬Ě OpenClaw ┬Ě GEODE)
  ŰžĄÝĽĹýť╝Űíť ÝÖĽý×ą.
- **README peer comparison: 7 Ôćĺ 25 grounded axes across 5 thematic
  tables.** The prior table contained (a) factual errors ÔÇö Claude Code
  listed as "Anthropic only" when Bedrock/Vertex routing has shipped,
  Codex CLI listed as "OpenAI only" when `model_providers` supports
  Azure / Bedrock / Ollama / any OpenAI-compatible ÔÇö and (b) too many
  "everyone Ôťů" cells, weakening differentiation. Researched the actual
  state of Claude Code v2.1.72, Codex CLI v0.130, OpenClaw v2026.5.12,
  and GEODE v0.95 across 18 axes each, then restructured into 5
  thematic tables (Runtime posture / Channels & UX / LLM provider &
  cost / Persistence, memory & verification / Extensibility &
  observability) totalling 25 axes. 4-level marker (`ÔťůÔťů`/`Ôťů`/`ÔÜá´ŞĆ`/`ÔŁî`)
  captures nuance. GEODE differentiator cells gain CHANGELOG version
  refs ÔÇö 200K token guard (v0.40), 5-layer context overflow (v0.39),
  58-event hook system, 5-tier memory, 5-layer verification (G1-G4 +
  BiasBuster + Krippendorff ╬▒ Ôëą 0.67), Petri observability (v0.90).
  Closing recommendation expanded to map 3 use-case patterns to 3
  systems (Claude/Codex ┬Ě OpenClaw ┬Ě GEODE).

### Changed

- **ýőťý×Ĺ Ű░░Űäł `harness:` ŰŁ╝Ű▓ĘýŁä GEODE ŰőĘŰĆůýť╝Űíť ýÂĽýćî.** ŕŞ░ýí┤ýŚÉŰŐö
  `KNOWN_HARNESSES` ŕ░Ç `.claude/`, `.cursor/`, `.codex/`, `.copilot/`,
  `.openclaw/` Űô▒ 10 ŕ░ť AI ŰĆäŕÁČ ýäĄýáĽ ŰööŰáëÝä░ŰŽČŰą╝ ŕ░ÉýžÇÝĽ┤ `harness: Claude
  Code, GEODE` ý▓śŰč╝ ÝĽĘŕ╗ś ýÂťŰáąÝľłŰŐöŰŹ░, ýŁ┤ŕ▓î "GEODE ŕ░Ç Claude Code ýťäýŚÉýäť
  ŰĆîýĽäŕ░äŰőĄ" ŰŐö ý×śŰ¬╗ŰÉť ŰŞîŰ×ťŰôť ýőáÝśŞŰíť ýŁŻÝśöýŐÁŰőłŰőĄ. GEODE ŰŐö ý×Éý▓┤ Űč░ÝâÇý×äýť╝Űíť
  LLM API ýŻť + agentic loop + tool ýőĄÝľë + tiered context memory + plugin
  ŰáłýžÇýŐĄÝŐŞŰŽČŰą╝ ýžüýáĹ ýłśÝľëÝĽęŰőłŰőĄ. `.claude/` Űô▒ýŁś ŰööŰáëÝä░ŰŽČŰŐö **ŕ░ťŰ░ťý×Éŕ░Ç
  GEODE Űą╝ ýáťý×Ĺ┬ĚýáĽŰ╣äÝĽá ŰĽî ýéČýÜęÝĽśŰŐö build-time ŰĆäŕÁČ ýäĄýáĽ**ýŁ┤ýžÇ GEODE ýŁś
  runtime dependency ŕ░Ç ýĽäŰőÖŰőłŰőĄ. `KNOWN_HARNESSES` Űą╝ `{".geode":
  "GEODE"}` ŰőĘýŁ╝ ÝĽşŰ¬ęýť╝Űíť ýÂĽýćîÝľłŕ│á, ŰĆÖýŁ╝ ŰŹ░ýŁ┤Ýä░Űą╝ LLM context Űíť ýú╝ý×ůÝĽśŰŐö
  `core/memory/context.py:_inject_project_env` ŰĆä ŕ░ÖýŁÇ ýőáÝśŞŰžî Ű│┤ŕ▓î ŰÉęŰőłŰőĄ.
- **Startup banner `harness:` label reduced to GEODE only.**
  `KNOWN_HARNESSES` previously detected 10 AI tool config directories
  (`.claude/`, `.cursor/`, `.codex/`, `.copilot/`, `.openclaw/`, ...) and
  rendered e.g. `harness: Claude Code, GEODE` at startup. That read as
  "GEODE runs on top of Claude Code", which is wrong: GEODE drives its
  own LLM API calls, agentic loop, tool execution, tiered context
  memory, and plugin registry. `.claude/` etc. are **build-time** tooling
  used by maintainers when developing GEODE, not runtime dependencies.
  `KNOWN_HARNESSES` is now `{".geode": "GEODE"}`, and the parallel
  injection into `core/memory/context.py:_inject_project_env` therefore
  exposes the same self-only signal to the LLM context.

### Added

- **Layout migration v2 Ôćĺ v3 ÔÇö TTL archival for runs/vault/projects.** PR
  feature/layout-v3. `core/wiring/layout_migrator.py` ýŁś `_migrate_v2_to_v3`
  ŕ░Ç `~/.geode/runs/` (Ýśäý×Č 600+ ÝîîýŁ╝ ÝĆëŰę┤), `~/.geode/vault/{general,research}/`
  (1800+ ÝîîýŁ╝), `~/.geode/projects/<encoded-cwd>/` (ýáťŕ▒░ŰÉť worktree ŰîÇýŁĹ
  ýŚöÝŐŞŰŽČ ÝĆČÝĽĘ) ýŁś ý×ÉýőŁ ýĄĹ `mtime` ýŁ┤ TTL Ű│┤ŰőĄ ýśĄŰ×śŰÉť ŕ▓âýŁä `_archive/<YYYY-MM>/`
  ýŤö Ű▓äÝéĚýť╝Űíť ýŁ┤ŰĆÖ. TTL ŕŞ░Ű│Ş 30ýŁ╝, `GEODE_ARCHIVE_TTL_DAYS` Űíť ýśĄŰ▓äŰŁ╝ýŁ┤Űôť.
  Hermes `SessionDB._init_schema` + Claude Code ýŤöŰ│ä Ű▓äÝéĚ + GEODE ý×Éý▓┤
  `shutil.move` ŰČ┤ýćÉýőĄ ÝîĘÝä┤ ÝĽęýä▒. Writer Ű│Çŕ▓Ż ýŚćýŁî ÔÇö bootstrap 1ÝÜî sweep,
  Ű▓äýáä Űžłý╗ĄŰíť ŕ▓îýŁ┤ÝŐŞ.
- **Layout migration v2 Ôćĺ v3 ÔÇö TTL archival for runs/vault/projects.** PR
  feature/layout-v3. `_migrate_v2_to_v3` archives children whose mtime is
  past TTL from `~/.geode/runs/` (600+ flat files), `~/.geode/vault/
  {general,research}/` (1800+ files), and `~/.geode/projects/` (entries
  for removed worktrees) into `_archive/<YYYY-MM>/` monthly buckets. TTL
  defaults to 30 days, overridable via `GEODE_ARCHIVE_TTL_DAYS`.
  Synthesizes Hermes `SessionDB._init_schema` + Claude Code monthly
  bucketing + GEODE's own `shutil.move` lossless pattern. No writer
  change ÔÇö one-shot bootstrap sweep gated by version marker.
- **Migration report per-step diagnostic logging.** `ensure_layout_migrated`
  ýŁś ýóůŰúî INFO ŰŁ╝ýŁŞýŁ┤ step ŰžłŰőĄ `moved=/skipped=/warnings=` ý╣┤ýÜ┤ÝŐŞŰą╝
  ý░ŹýŁî. v1Ôćĺv2 ÝŐŞŰŽČŕ▒░ ŕ░ş ("Űžłý╗ĄŰŐö v=2 ýŁŞŰŹ░ ýĽäý╣┤ýŁ┤ŰŞîŕ░Ç ýĽł ýŁ╝ýľ┤ŰéČŰőĄ") ÝŤäýćŹ
  ýžäŰőĘ ÔÇö `~/.geode/logs/serve.log` ÝĽť ýĄäŰíť "v3 ŕ░Ç ŰČ┤ýŚçýŁä ýś«ŕ▓╝Űéś" ŕ░Ç Ű│┤ý×ä.
- **Migration report per-step diagnostic logging.** Closing INFO line now
  emits per-step moved/skipped/warnings counts, so operators can answer
  "did v3 actually archive anything?" at a glance.

- **P4 ÔÇö paths.py SoT lint guardrail + ýÂöŕ░Ç 14 ýéČýŁ┤ÝŐŞ ýáĽŰáČ.** PR #1098
  audit ýŁś ŰžłýžÇŰžë ŰőĘŕ│ä. `tests/test_path_literal_guard.py` ýőáýäĄ ÔÇö pytest

### Removed

- **`PROJECT_EMBEDDING_CACHE` / `PROJECT_VECTORS_DIR`** path constants
  in `core.paths` ÔÇö vestigial. No writer ever used either; the on-disk
  directories (`{workspace}/.geode/embedding-cache/` and
  `{workspace}/.geode/vectors/`) stopped receiving writes on
  2026-04-05. Cascading removals: `core/cli/cmd_lifecycle.py` ýŁś
  `/clean` lookup + scan list, `tests/test_lifecycle_commands.py` ýŁś
  `PROJECT_EMBEDDING_CACHE` patch ŕ░Ç Ű¬ĘŰĹÉ ýáĽŰŽČŰÉĘ. ý×öýŚČ ŰööýŐĄÝüČ ŰööŰáëÝä░ŰŽČ
  ŰŐö layout migration v1Ôćĺv2 ŕ░Ç `_archive/` Űíť ýś«ŕ╣Ç (ýĽäŰ×ś ÝĽşŰ¬ę).

### Fixed

- **Layout migration v1Ôćĺv2 ÔÇö vestigial ŰööŰáëÝä░ŰŽČ archival.**
  `core/wiring/layout_migrator.py:_migrate_v1_to_v2()` ŕ░Ç Ýśäý×Č workspace
  ýŁś `.geode/{embedding-cache,vectors}/` Űą╝ `.geode/_archive/<name>-<UTC>/`
  Űíť ýĽłýáäÝĽśŕ▓î ýś«ŕ╣Ç (`shutil.move`, never `rmtree`). Ű╣äýľ┤ý×łŰŐö ŕ▓ŻýÜ░ `rmdir`
  Űžî ýłśÝľë, archive target ýŁ┤ ýŁ┤Ű»Ş ý×łýť╝Űę┤ ýŤÉŰ│Ş Ű│┤ýí┤ + warning. v0Ôćĺv1 ýŁś
  same-FS atomic move ÝîĘÝä┤ + lossless safety ŕ│äýŐ╣. `GEODE_LAYOUT_VERSION`
  1 Ôćĺ 2. ÝÜîŕĚÇ: `tests/test_layout_migrator.py::TestV1ToV2VestigialArchival`
  8 cases (populated cache / populated vectors / both / empty rmdir /
  absent skip / no .geode/ short-circuit / full v0Ôćĺv2 chain / constants
  removed sanity).

### Documentation

- **Storage hierarchy decision doc** (`docs/architecture/storage-hierarchy.md`).
  3 frontier harness (Claude Code, Hermes Agent by NousResearch, OpenClaw)
  ýŁś context / storage ŰÂäŰŽČ ýáĽý▒ů Ű╣äŕÁÉ + GEODE ýŁś `~/.geode/` (user-private)
  vs `{workspace}/.geode/` (project-bound, team-shareable) ŰÂäŰő┤ ŕĚťý╣Ö.
  ŕ▓░ýáĽ ÝŐŞŰŽČ ÔÇö credential / cross-project identity / agent operating state
  / per-project user-private state ŰŐö user-home, Ű░śŰę┤ team-shareable rules /
  skills / ÝöäŰíťýáŁÝŐŞŰ│ä scheduler / reports ŰŐö project-local. Hermes/OpenClaw
  ýŁś user-home-only ÝîĘÝä┤ýŁÇ multi-platform messaging context ÝĽťýáĽýť╝Űíť ýáĽŰő╣ÝÖö
  ŰÉśŰę░, GEODE ŰŐö workspace-bound runtime ýŁ┤ŰŁ╝ Claude Code ýŁś hybrid ŕ░Ç ŰŹö
  ýáüÝĽę. ÝŤäýćŹ PR ýŁś TODO ý║ÉŰŽČýśĄŰ▓ä: vestigial constants 3ŕ░ť
  (`PROJECT_EMBEDDING_CACHE`, `PROJECT_TOOL_OFFLOAD`, `PROJECT_VECTORS_DIR`
  ÔÇö writer ýŚćýŁî, `cmd_lifecycle.py` ýŁś `/clean` ý╗ĘýŐłŰĘŞýŚÉŰžî Űô▒ŰíŁ) ýŁś ýáĽŰŽČ +
  `~/.geode/runs/` ýŁś `<YYYY-MM>/` bucket + vault TTL ýáĽý▒ů.

### Infrastructure

- **Changelog viewer: entry-level KO / EN toggle plus fallback banner.**
  The previous side-by-side dual-column layout was hard to scan on narrow
  screens and forced readers to read both halves for every entry. Replaced
  with a Tailwind-style dual-list toggle (research notes:
  Stripe / MDN / react.dev / Tailwind / Cloudflare).
  - Per-entry pill at top right: `KO` / `EN`. Defaults to page locale.
    Only rendered when both languages are detected in the entry.
  - Mono-lingual entries: no toggle. When the requested language is
    absent, a small amber Stripe-style banner notes the absence
    ("ýŁ┤ entryŰŐö ýśüýľ┤ ýŤÉŰČŞŰžî ý×Ĺýä▒ŰÉÉýŐÁŰőłŰőĄ" or the EN equivalent).
  - Top sticky nav gains a ÝĽťŕÁşýľ┤ / English page-locale switch on the
    right end. Switching globally changes the default for every entry.
  - Detection logic unchanged (Hangul / Latin ratio per markdown block).
    Code fences and short / symbol-only blocks live in both halves.


### Infrastructure

- **AGENTS.md at repo root.** LLM agent navigation map covering core /
  agent, core / llm, core / tools, core / mcp, core / memory, core / hooks,
  core / wiring, core / server (incl. client_capability handshake),
  core / audit, core / automation, core / skills, core / verification,
  plus the two plugins. Pattern adopted from OpenClaw's AGENTS.md
  convention. Sits next to CLAUDE.md (dev scaffold) and GEODE.md
  (runtime identity).
- **llms.txt and llms-full.txt** (Hermes pattern). Generated by
  `site/scripts/sync-stats.mjs` from the docs sitemap on every sync.
  Plain-text indexes at `mangowhoiscloud.github.io/geode/llms.txt`
  and `/llms-full.txt`. Lists every public page with summary, both
  Korean and English titles, plus pointers to CHANGELOG / CLAUDE / AGENTS.
- **Five outdated pages patched.**
  - `architecture/system-index` switched from hardcoded counts (v0.64,
    223 core, 13 plugins) to live `GEODE_SOT` imports.
  - `harness/lifecycle` gained a "Client capability handshake (v0.84+)"
    section documenting `client_capability` IPC handshake
    (`is_tty`, `width`).
  - `runtime/automation` expanded with the 7-module composition table
    (model_registry / feedback_loop / drift / correlation /
    expert_panel / outcome_tracking / snapshot).
  - `petri/scenarios` got a "Default 173 Petri seeds" category
    breakdown table (refusal_calibration / honesty_deception /
    power_seeking / harm_uplift / sandbox_awareness / manipulation /
    privacy / meta).
  - `runtime/skills` body bulked out: 5-tier discovery detail,
    `core/skills/reports/` v0.71 package layout (8 modules),
    SKILL.md frontmatter convention, three-step authoring guide,
    hook integration.


### Added

- **`~/.geode/` ŰööŰáëÝä░ŰŽČ layout migration ýŁŞÝöäŰŁ╝.** Hermes Agent (NousResearch)
  ýŁś `SessionDB._init_schema` ÝîĘÝä┤ + OpenClaw `autoMigrateLegacyStateDir`
  + GEODE ŕŞ░ýí┤ `_resolve_with_fallback` ýůő ýóůÝĽę. ýőáŕĚť `core/wiring/
  layout_migrator.py` ÔÇö `GEODE_LAYOUT_VERSION` (Ýśäý×Č 1), `~/.geode/
  .layout-version` dotfile marker (Hermes ýŁś `.managed` / `active_profile`
  dotfile ýáäŰíÇ), module-level once-flag Űíť idempotent (OpenClaw
  `autoMigrateStateDirChecked` + Hermes `_bootstrap_applied` ÝĆëÝľë),
  `GEODE_DISABLE_LAYOUT_MIGRATION` env escape hatch.
  - **v0Ôćĺv1 ŰžłýŁ┤ŕĚŞŰáłýŁ┤ýůś**: ýäŞ path ýśĄŰąś ýáĽýáĽ ÔÇö (1) `serve.log` ŕ░Ç
    `~/.geode/` ŰúĘÝŐŞýŚÉýäť `~/.geode/logs/serve.log` Űíť (paths.py ýŁś
    `SERVE_LOG_PATH` ŕ░Ç ýŁ┤Ű»Ş ŕ░ÇŰŽČÝéĄŰŹś ŕ││), (2) `approve_history.json`
    (paths.py ýśĄÝâÇ) Ôćĺ `approval_history.jsonl` (ýőĄýáť writer ýŁ┤ŰŽä),
    (3) `mcp-registry-cache.json` Ôćĺ `mcp/registry-cache.json` (ŰőĄŰąŞ
    MCP state ýÖÇ ÝĽĘŕ╗ś ŰČÂýŁî). `shutil.move` Űíť atomic, ŰĆÖýŁ╝ ÝîîýŁ╝ destination
    ýŁ┤Ű»Ş ýí┤ý×Č ýőť ýćÉŰîÇýžÇ ýĽŐŕ│á warning surface (never overwrite user data).
  - **ÝśŞýÂť ýőťýáÉ**: `core.paths.ensure_directories()` ŰüŁ ÔÇö bootstrap ýŁś
    ŰžĄ ÝśŞýÂťŰžłŰőĄ (idempotent). `uv tool install` / `uv tool update` ŰŐö ýÜ░ŰŽČ ýŻöŰôťŰą╝
    ýőĄÝľëÝĽśýžÇ ýĽŐýť╝Ű»ÇŰíť ýéČýőĄýâü install/update ýžüÝŤä ý▓ź `geode` Ű¬ůŰá╣ýŚÉýäť ÝŐŞŰŽČŕ▒░ŰÉĘ.
  - **ÝÜîŕĚÇ**: `tests/test_layout_migrator.py` 12 cases ÔÇö version marker
    round-trip / corrupt marker / disable env / idempotency / v0Ôćĺv1 ýŁś
    ýäŞ path Ű│ä + conflict-keep-both + missing-source-skip.

### Added

- **Wanted.co.kr ŕŞ░Ű░ś ÝĽťŕÁş job ŕ▓Çýâë ŰĆäŕÁČ (`wanted_jobs_search`).** LinkedIn
  ýŁś PerimeterX/Cloudflare bot detection ýť╝Űíť `search_jobs` MCP ŕ░Ç ŰžĄŰ▓ł
  403 + empty body Űíť ý░ĘŰőĘŰÉśŰŐö ýâüÝÖęýŚÉ ŰîÇÝĽť ŰîÇý▓┤ ŕ▓ŻŰíť. Wanted ýŁś ŕ│Áŕ░ť REST
  endpoint (`/api/v4/jobs`) Űą╝ httpx Űíť ýžüýáĹ ÝśŞýÂťÝĽ┤ OAuth/proxy/scraper
  Ű»ŞŰööýľ┤ ýŁśýí┤ýä▒ ýŚćýŁ┤ ÝĽťŕÁş tech job ýŁä ŕ▓Çýâë. ŕ▓░ŕ│╝ŰŐö ÝĆëÝâäÝĽť dict ŰŽČýŐĄÝŐŞ
  `{job_id, position, company, location, url, posted_at}`. MCP server ŕ░Ç
  ýĽäŰőłŰŁ╝ GEODE Űé┤ý×ą ŰĆäŕÁČ ÔÇö Ű│äŰĆä subprocess ýŚćýŁî. `SAFE_TOOLS` ýŚÉ Űô▒ŰíŁŰÉśýľ┤
  sub-agent / read-only ýáĽý▒ů path ýŚÉýäť auto-approve. tool count 24Ôćĺ25.
  ŰáłÝŹ╝Űč░ýŐĄ: Manus / Devin ýŁś paid scraping provider fallback ÝîĘÝä┤ŕ│╝ŰŐö
  Ű░śŰîÇŰíť ÔÇö ý░ĘŰőĘŰÉśŰŐö source Űą╝ Ű░öŕżŞŰŐö lightweight Ű░ęÝľą.
- **`run_bash` ýŁś read-only pipeline auto-approve.** ŕŞ░ýí┤ `is_bash_auto_approved`
  ŕ░Ç pipe (`|`) ý×Éý▓┤Űą╝ ŰČ┤ýí░ŕ▒┤ unsafe Űíť ÝîÉýáĽÝĽ┤ `find ~/x -type f | sed 's/ÔÇŽ/ÔÇŽ/'
  | head -200` ŕ░ÖýŁÇ ÝĹťýĄÇ read-only ý▓┤ýŁŞýŁ┤ ŰžĄŰ▓ł HITL approval ýÜöŕÁČ. ýŁ┤ýáť
  `SAFE_BASH_PIPELINE_STAGES` (head/tail/wc/sort/uniq/cut/tr/grep/rg/cat/
  less/more/sed/awk/jq/yq/column/fold/nl) Űą╝ ýÂöŕ░ÇÝĽ┤ ÔÇö ý▓ź stage ŕ░Ç ŕŞ░ýí┤
  `SAFE_BASH_PREFIXES` ŰžĄý╣ś + ýŁ┤ÝŤä stage ŰôĄýŁ┤ Ű¬ĘŰĹÉ pipeline-safe Űę┤ ÝćÁŕ│╝.
  `tee` ŰŐö by-design write ŰŁ╝ Ű¬ůýőťýáü ýáťýÖŞ. `sed -i` / `--in-place` ŰĆä Ű│äŰĆä
  reject. ýťä ýÖŞ ÔÇö `>`, `>>`, `;`, `&`, backtick, `$(...)`, `<(...)`, `>(...)`
  ŰŐö ýŚČýáäÝ×ł hard reject. ýáĽýáü helper `core.agent.safety.is_bash_command_read_only`
  Űíť ýÂöýÂť ÔÇö `ApprovalController` ýÖÇ ÝůîýŐĄÝŐŞŕ░Ç ŕ░ÖýŁÇ ÝĽĘýłś ÝśŞýÂťÝĽ┤ drift Ű░ęýžÇ.
  ŰáłÝŹ╝Űč░ýŐĄ: `claude-code` settings.json ýŁś `permissions.allow:
  ["Bash(find:*)", ÔÇŽ]` per-command ŕŞÇŰíťŰŞî + Codex CLI sandbox ýŁś read-only
  stream filter ýáĽý▒ů. ÝÜîŕĚÇ ÔÇö `tests/test_bash_safe_prefix.py` 35 cases (12
  ýőáŕĚť pipeline + sed -i / process subst / background / empty stage).

### Infrastructure

- **Docs CHANGELOG full sync plus three outdated patches.**
  - `site/scripts/sync-stats.mjs` now parses CHANGELOG.md and emits
    `site/src/data/geode/changelog.ts` with every version entry
    (134 releases plus the current Unreleased). The Changelog page reads
    that file and renders every entry, replacing the prior selected-
    highlights manual list.
  - New minimal markdown renderer at
    `site/src/components/geode-docs/markdown-lite.tsx`. Handles the
    subset CHANGELOG entries use: h3 headings, nested bullets, bold,
    inline code, links, fenced code blocks.
  - `/docs/reference/changelog` page rebuilt to render the full list with
    an in-page version index (grid of anchored versions), one section per
    entry, and a footer note pointing back to the repo CHANGELOG.md as
    the authoritative source.
  - Three outdated pages patched.
    - `/docs/runtime/llm/prompt-system`: summary now mentions the v0.93
      XML envelope (16 markers across 9 files).
    - `/docs/runtime/llm/prompt-caching`: boundary-marker section adds
      the `<dynamic_context>` XML wrapper and the audit-mode strip link.
    - `/docs/ops/cost`: full usage-ledger schema (CallDiagnostic-aligned
      fields), v0.90 token-tracker dual-record fix, three jq one-liners,
      provider downgrade chains updated to GLM 5.x and OpenAI 5.x.


### Fixed

- **OAuth Press-[Enter] prompt ÔÇö direct-render wiring parity.** PR #1077
  added the Press-[Enter] watcher only to the IPC render path
  (`EventRenderer._handle_oauth_login_started`). The thin-CLI direct path
  in `core/ui/agentic_ui/events.py:emit_oauth_login_started` (used by
  `RunLocation.THIN` `/login oauth openai`) still rendered the legacy
  layout without the prompt, so users on the THIN path didn't see
  "Press [Enter]" and the browser-watcher never spawned. Extracted the
  watcher to `core/ui/oauth_browser.start_oauth_browser_watcher`; both
  the IPC handler and the direct fallback now call it. Regression:
  `tests/test_oauth_browser.py::TestEmitOauthLoginStartedFallback` (2
  cases ÔÇö fallback prints prompt + spawns watcher; IPC path skips
  fallback) + `TestStartOauthBrowserWatcher` (2 cases ÔÇö TTY gating).



- **Sandbox tilde expansion (`~` / `~/`) ÔÇö silent-fail bug.**
  `core/tools/sandbox.py:validate_path()` advertised that bare `~` and `~/`
  were "expanded by Python, not shell" (`check_shell_expansion` allowed
  them through), but the actual `os.path.expanduser()` call was missing.
  Result: paths like `~/workspace/resume/common` were joined against the
  project root verbatim, producing `/<project>/~/workspace/...` and a
  misleading `Not a directory` error instead of the proper
  `expanded-path-outside-sandbox` permission error. Surfaced by `geode
  serve` when an agent attempted `glob_files(path="~/workspace/...")`.
  Fix: call `os.path.expanduser(path_str)` immediately before `Path()`
  construction in `validate_path()`, and `path.expanduser()` before
  `.resolve()` in `add_working_directory` / `remove_working_directory`.
  Regression: `tests/test_sandbox.py::TestTildeExpansion` (4 cases ÔÇö
  bare `~`, `~/`, sandbox-expanded resolution, working-dir registration).
- **OpenAI OAuth plan tier auto-reconcile.** `chatgpt_plan_type` was
  extracted from the JWT once during `/login` and frozen into
  `Plan.subscription_tier`. If the user upgraded their ChatGPT plan
  (Plus Ôćĺ Pro/Max/etc.) between logins, the stored tier kept showing
  the old value while the JWT itself had the new claim. `load_auth_toml`
  now calls `reconcile_plan_tier_from_stored_jwt()` after hydration,
  which re-decodes the access_token's claims and updates
  `Plan.subscription_tier` + profile metadata in place when a drift is
  found. Drift events log at INFO so the change is visible without
  forcing a re-login (a fresh `/login` still produces a brand-new JWT
  with the current tier ÔÇö this is the in-between fix).
- **OpenAI OAuth ÔÇö JWT decode DRY.** Extracted the inline base64+JSON
  decode block in `login_openai()` into a reusable `_decode_jwt_claims`
  helper; `_plan_type_from_token` builds on top so the reconciliation
  path and login path can't drift apart.

### Added

- **OAuth login UX ÔÇö press Enter to open the verification URL.**
  `EventRenderer._handle_oauth_login_started` now prints
  `Press [Enter] to open the URL in your browser` and spawns a daemon
  thread that calls `webbrowser.open(verification_uri)` on the first
  stdin line. Skipped automatically when stdin is not a TTY (piped
  invocations stay quiet). Pattern grounded from Claude Code's OAuth
  start prompt (`claude-code/src/auth/providers.ts`).

### Infrastructure

- **Docs restructure (F-series) plus Context system page.**
  - **Chapter rename**. "LLM ÝîîýŁ┤ÝöäŰŁ╝ýŁŞ" to "Runtime". "ýÖť ýŁ┤Űáçŕ▓î" to "Why".
  - **Petri folded into Verification**. The five Petri pages (overview /
    scenarios / run / judge-dimensions / bundle) now live under chapter
    06 Verification next to guardrails, biasbuster, and observability.
  - **LangSmith page deleted**. The deprecation notice page is now gone
    entirely. References to LangSmith were already replaced by the
    native four-lens stack in v0.89.0.
  - **Observability moved and expanded**. From `/docs/ops/observability`
    to `/docs/verification/observability`. Body expanded with per-lens
    structure: hook 14-category table, RunLog JSONL schema, audit
    diagnostics CallDiagnostic dataclass, Petri integration, usage
    ledger, OpenTelemetry / inspect viewer adapters, why-LangSmith-was-
    removed rationale.
  - **Context System page new**. `/docs/runtime/context`. Single
    reference for the four mechanisms: 5-tier memory hierarchy (integer
    tiers 0 to 4, no fractional tiers), 5-layer prompt assembly with
    `AssembledPrompt` dataclass, 200K absolute token guard plus 80 / 95
    percent phases plus 25K MCP cap, Clean Context anchoring prevention,
    cache interface, comparison with Claude Code / LangChain / LlamaIndex.
  - **Fractional tier numbers retired**. `Automation (L4.5)` renamed to
    `Automation` (or `Automation Sidecar`); body now describes it as a
    sidecar between L2 Runtime and L4 Agent rather than as a tier.
    `4.5-Tier`, `Memory(4.5T)`, and a `Tier 0.5` reference in portfolio
    components likewise integerized.
  - **Changelog page synced**. Highlight reel now spans v0.65 through
    v0.95 (Anthropic agentic_call streaming, GLM 202_752 correction,
    OpenAI HTML guard, GEODE_PERSONA, XML sandwich, 4-layer observability,
    Petri scenarios, auto-escalation removal, LangSmith removal,
    cold-start arc, lifecycle to wiring rename, geode audit CLI).
  - Net: 50 pages, 11 chapters (down from 12). All bilingual.

### Infrastructure

- **Docs restructure (v0.95.0+) ÔÇö 12 chapters, 49 pages, References chapter

  - **03 Build chapter removed.** The 4 pages (`build/add-tool`,

    (2) ┬Ě 08 Plugins (2).

- **Docs site rebuild ÔÇö Di├ítaxis 8 chapters, 53 bilingual pages.**

- **Public site at `site/` ÔÇö GEODE Pages deploy.** Next.js 16 static
  export integrated into the repo at `site/`. Builds and deploys to
  GitHub Pages at `https://mangowhoiscloud.github.io/geode/` on push to
  `main`.
  - Routes: `/portfolio` (case-study narrative), `/docs` (28 reference

    verification, plugins, reference), `/about`, `/works/reode`.
  - Single source of truth: `site/src/data/geode/sot.ts` ÔÇö auto-synced
    from this repo's `pyproject.toml` + `core/` + `plugins/` filesystem
    counts + `CHANGELOG.md` release count via
    `site/scripts/sync-stats.mjs`. The site cannot advertise a version
    different from what `pyproject.toml` claims.

    artifact Ôćĺ official `actions/deploy-pages@v4`). Path-filtered to
    `site/**`, so Python source changes do not redeploy. One-time
    activation: repo Settings Ôćĺ Pages Ôćĺ Source = "GitHub Actions".
  - Sourced from the standalone `portfolio` repo; route-restructured to
    place GEODE's public surfaces under one tree (basePath `/portfolio`
    Ôćĺ `/geode`, `/geode` page Ôćĺ `/portfolio`, `/geode/docs` Ôćĺ `/docs`,
    root landing Ôćĺ `/about`, `/reode` Ôćĺ `/works/reode`).
  - Master neologism locked: **self-hosting agent harness** (analogue
    of self-hosting compiler ÔÇö the runtime and the build line share the
    same primitives). Defined once at `/geode/portfolio` after the
    hero; every chapter below refers without redefining.

## [0.95.0] ÔÇö 2026-05-12

### Fixed

- **GLM context window precision ÔÇö GAP-X1.** `MODEL_CONTEXT_WINDOW`
  rounded all five registered GLM models (`glm-5.1`, `glm-5`,
  `glm-5-turbo`, `glm-4.7`, `glm-4.7-flash`) to a flat `200_000`-token
  guard. Re-verification against z.ai docs + openrouter listings (2026-05-12)
  yields the precise value `202_752` ÔÇö a +2_752-token delta that the
  post-call 200K guard was tripping early. Cloudflare / LM Studio
  deployments expose smaller windows (`131_072` / `128k`) but GEODE calls
  z.ai directly so the upstream contract applies. Regression test:
  `tests/test_glm_context_window.py` (6 cases ÔÇö per-model assertion +
  family-shared-window invariant). `tests/test_context_monitor.py` fixture
  for the "200K models skip ceiling" case switched from `glm-5` to
  `claude-opus-4-5` (exact 200_000) ÔÇö `glm-5` is no longer exactly 200K.

### Changed

- **Anthropic agentic_call streaming ÔÇö GAP-S1.**
  `ClaudeAgenticAdapter.agentic_call._do_call` now wraps the request in
  `async with self._client.messages.stream(**create_kwargs) as s:` and
  returns `await s.get_final_message()` instead of the previous
  non-streaming `await self._client.messages.create(**create_kwargs)`.
  The final message is the same `anthropic.types.Message` schema, so
  `normalize_anthropic` and the token-tracker path are unchanged ÔÇö the
  benefit is chunk-level network delivery and an SDK-level surface for
  partial state (not yet wired into the agentic loop's UI). OpenAI / GLM
  streaming is deferred (separate PR ÔÇö Responses API has a stricter
  stream contract with reasoning replay). Regression test:
  `tests/test_anthropic_agentic_stream.py` (2 cases ÔÇö stream-vs-create,
  kwargs passthrough). `tests/test_anthropic_sampling_params.py` helper
  updated to mock both transports.

## [0.94.0] ÔÇö 2026-05-12

### Added

- **OpenAI HTML data-URL guard ÔÇö GAP-17.** OpenAI/Codex models, when
  asked to author HTML, frequently emit the entire document as a single
  `data:text/html(;base64)?,...` URL meant to be pasted into a browser's
  address bar ÔÇö a shape that silently breaks GEODE's downstream
  consumers (slide build, report PDF, artifact archiving) and inflates
  `output_tokens` 30ÔÇô50% from base64 overhead.
  - Primary guard: `core/agent/system_prompt._build_model_card` now
    injects a provider-gated instruction for `openai` / `openai-codex`
    forbidding the address-bar shape and demanding raw `<!DOCTYPE html>`
    source. Anthropic / GLM cards are unchanged ÔÇö they do not exhibit
    this drift.
  - Safety net: new `core/llm/postprocess/html_output.py` exposes
    `detect_data_url` / `decode_html` / `extract_artifact_to` so callers
    can recover the HTML when a model emits the shape anyway.
    Idempotent (hash-derived filename), handles base64 + percent-encoded
    payloads + malformed-base64 fallback.
  - 18 regression tests: `tests/test_html_output_guard.py` covering
    5 detection shapes, 3 decode round-trips, 2 disk extraction cases,
    OpenAI/Codex guard presence (3 models), Anthropic/GLM guard absence
    (4 models).
- **GLM thinking effort gate ÔÇö GAP-R1.** `GlmAgenticAdapter.agentic_call`
  now honours `effort in ("off", "none")` by sending
  `{"type": "disabled", "clear_thinking": False}` via `extra_body`.
  GLM-5.x / 4.7 ignore the `disabled` value (thinking is compulsory per
  the upstream contract ÔÇö harmless) but GLM-4.5 / 4.6 hybrid models
  honour it and recover the (typically large) reasoning-token cost when
  the caller asks for cheap, non-thinking output. Any non-off effort
  keeps the v0.58.0 enabled-with-context-preserve shape. Test:
  `tests/test_glm_thinking_control.py` (9 cases ÔÇö 3 hybrid models ├Ś off,
  none alias, 4 non-off efforts, pre-4.5 omission).
- **OpenAI prompt_cache_key ÔÇö GAP-A2.** OpenAI's Responses API
  auto-caches matching prefixes; an optional `prompt_cache_key` routes
  similar requests to the same cache pool, lifting hit-rate when
  `(system + tools)` is stable while the user / conversation differs.
  `OpenAIAgenticAdapter.agentic_call` now derives a 32-hex-char SHA-256
  key over `(system, sort_keys(tools))` with a `\x00` separator and
  injects it into `responses.create` kwargs. Token tracking + cost
  attribution were already wired (`agentic_response.py:251` reads
  `prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens`; `token_tracker.py:175` carries
  per-model `cache_read` pricing), so this PR completes the path.
  Test: `tests/test_openai_prompt_cache.py` (6 derivation contracts +
  1 adapter-wiring stub = 7 cases).
- **Cross-provider tool_choice normalization ÔÇö GAP-T1.** New
  `core/llm/tool_choice.py` centralizes the conversion of a canonical
  `tool_choice` (string / dict / named-tool / `None`) into each
  provider's native shape ÔÇö Anthropic dict, OpenAI Responses string-or-flat,
  GLM Chat Completions nested-function. Replaces 3├Ś inlined conversions
  in `anthropic.py:482-484`, `openai.py:507`, `glm.py:190` and adds first-class
  support for named-tool forcing (`{"name": "X"}` Ôćĺ provider-specific shape)
  and the `required` Ôćö `any` keyword alias. Test:
  `tests/test_tool_choice_normalize.py` (33 cases ├Ś 3 providers + edge cases).

## [0.93.1] ÔÇö 2026-05-12

### Fixed

- **LLM retry policy SOT ÔÇö GAP-E1.** `OpenAIAdapter._retry_with_backoff`
  pinned `max_retries=3`, `retry_base_delay=1.0`, `retry_max_delay=30.0`
  via module-local `_MAX_RETRIES` / `_RETRY_BASE_DELAY` / `_RETRY_MAX_DELAY`
  constants, ignoring `settings.llm_max_retries` /
  `settings.llm_retry_base_delay` / `settings.llm_retry_max_delay`.
  GLM (via `OpenAIAgenticAdapter` inheritance) inherited the same drift.
  Adapter now leaves these arguments unset so `retry_with_backoff_generic`
  resolves them lazily from settings ÔÇö restoring the single source of
  truth shared with Anthropic. Regression test:
  `tests/test_retry_policy_sot.py`.

- **Petri seeds flat-layout (G-A1).** Discovery (post-merge of PR #1044):
  `inspect_petri/_seeds/_markdown.py:read_seed_directory` uses
  `directory.glob("*.md")` ÔÇö **non-recursive**. The 13 curated GEODE
  seeds were nested under `plugins/petri_audit/seeds/<category>/<seed>.md`,
  so `read_seed_directory(plugins/petri_audit/seeds)` returned 0 samples.
  Audits passing `--seed-select id:<csv>` fell back to inspect_petri's
  173 built-in seed lookup, hit a `ValueError("Unknown built-in seed
  id(s): ...")`, and inspect_ai's dispatch layer silently fell back to
  raw-string samples (`Sample(input='id:unrestricted_shell')`). The
  PR #1044 audit's seed-specific claims (e.g. `unrestricted_shell`
  input_hallucination=5, `long_running_loop` admirable=2) are
  **invalidated** ÔÇö the auditor never saw the .md scenario prose,
  only the seed-id name string. Broad alignment claims
  (`broken_tool_use` -1.08, overall \|╬ö\| < 0.5) remain valid as a
  generic-pressure measurement. Fix:
  - 13 .md files flattened from `seeds/<category>/<seed>.md` to
    `seeds/<category>_<seed>.md` so `read_seed_directory` actually sees
    them. Category survives in the filename prefix.
  - `cli_audit.audit` `--seed-select` default changed from `None` to
    `"plugins/petri_audit/seeds"` (the directory). The `id:<csv>` path
    is now documented as broken-by-design (inspect_petri 173-seed scope)
    in the option's help text.
  - Retraction note added to `docs/audits/2026-05-12-petri-geode-audit.md`
    distinguishing valid (broad) from invalidated (seed-specific) claims.
  - 16 new regression guards in `tests/plugins/petri_audit/test_seeds_
    flat.py` pinning: no sub-dirs, exactly 13 .md, `<category>_<seed>.md`
    convention, `read_seed_directory` returns 13 samples with prose-length
    inputs (>100 chars, not 22-char `id:<name>` strings).

## [0.93.0] ÔÇö 2026-05-12

### Changed

- **System-prompt audit + cleanup (2026-05-12).** 12 ÝĽşŰ¬ę GAP audit
  (G1-G12) ýŁś ÝćÁÝĽę ýáĽŰŽČ. **Default behaviour ŕ░Ç Ű░öŰÇîýŚłýŐÁŰőłŰőĄ ÔÇö GEODE
  identity ŕ░Ç ŰžĄ ÝśŞýÂťýŚÉ default Űíť inject ŰÉśýžÇ ýĽŐýŐÁŰőłŰőĄ.**
  - **G1 ÔÇö XML sandwich (`<key>...</key>`)**: `core/llm/prompts/*.md`
    9 ÝîîýŁ╝ýŁś 16 marker (`=== SYSTEM ===` / `=== USER ===` / `=== RESCORE
    ===` / `=== DUAL_VERIFY ===` / `=== AGENTIC_SUFFIX ===` / `===
    ANALYST_TOOLS ===` / `=== SYNTHESIZER_TOOLS ===`) Űą╝ XML tag Űíť

    section ýÂöýÂť. Anthropic / Petri auditor / Claude Code-ref ýŁś
    frontier ÝîĘÝä┤ŕ│╝ ýŁ╝ý╣ś.

## [0.92.0] ÔÇö 2026-05-12

### Added

- **`core.audit.diagnostics` ÔÇö file-based diagnostics log surviving
  `inspect eval` subprocess boundaries.** PR E/F (v0.90.0) ýŁś ad-hoc
  `core/_fa4_debug.py` ÝîĘÝä┤ýŁś ýáĽýőŁ ýŁŞÝöäŰŁ╝ÝÖö. `inspect eval` ýŁś child
  process ŕ░Ç `subprocess.run(capture_output=True)` Űíť stdout/stderr
  ŕ▓ęŰŽČ + inspect_ai ýŁś `init_logger` ŕ░Ç root LogHandler ý×ČýäĄýáĽ Ôćĺ
  GEODE plugin ýŁś INFO/DEBUG ŕ░Ç parent Űíť propagate ýĽł ŰÉĘ. file-based
  append-only log ŕ░Ç ýŁ┤ ŰĹÉ boundary ýÖÇ ŰČ┤ŕ┤ÇÝĽśŕ▓î evidence Ű│┤ýí┤.
  - **API** ÔÇö `from core.audit import diag, diagnostics_path`.
    `diag("petri.anthropic", f"BadRequest: {msg[:200]}")` ÝĽť ýĄäŰíť ÝśŞýÂť
  - **Location** ÔÇö `~/.geode/diagnostics/<YYYY-MM>.log` (ýŤö rotation).
    `GEODE_DIAGNOSTICS_LOG=<path>` ÝÖśŕ▓Ż Ű│Çýłś override (test/CI fixture
    ýÜęŰĆä)
  - **Line format** ÔÇö `<unix_ts:%.3f> <pid> <component> <msg>`. grep/jq
    ý╣ťÝÖö. `component` ŰŐö dotted namespace (`petri.runner`,
    `petri.anthropic`, `petri.lifecycle`)
  - **Best-effort** ÔÇö Ű¬ĘŰôá `OSError` swallow. diagnostics ŕ░Ç audit ŕ╣ĘŰę┤
    ýĽł ŰÉĘ (disk full / permission denied)
  - **GEODE convention ýŁ╝ŕ┤Çýä▒** ÔÇö `~/.geode/usage/`, `~/.geode/petri/
    logs/`, `~/.geode/journal/`, `~/.geode/runs/` ýÖÇ ŕ░ÖýŁÇ ýťäý╣ś. `/tmp/`
    ŕ░ÖýŁÇ OS-level temp ýĽäŰőś (PR E/F ýŁś ýéČýÜęý×É Ű╣äÝîÉ Ű░śýśü)
  - **ÝÜîŕĚÇ ŕ░ÇŰôť 10 ýőáŕĚť** ÔÇö env override / user expansion / month
    rotation / DEFAULT_DIAGNOSTICS_DIR ý╗ĘŰ▓Ąýůś / write format / append /
    OSError ýÜ░ÝÜî / ŰĆÖýőť thread write / package re-export / signature
  - `docs/architecture/petri-observability.md` ýŁś 3-layer Ôćĺ **4-layer**
    ÝÖĽý×ą (Raw + JSONL ledger + MANIFEST + Diagnostics). Layer 4 ýŁś
    `When to reach for` + `Discovery` (grep/awk ÝîĘÝä┤) Ű¬ůýőť. 4573
    passed.

  **fa4 Ôćĺ LoggerEvent ýáäýŁ┤**: PR E/F ýŁś file-based fa4 evidence ŕ░Ç
  PR #1034 ýŁś namespace setLevel(INFO) fix ÝŤä ýáĽýőŁ `.eval`
  LoggerEvent Űíť ý×ÉŰĆÖ ýŐ╣ŕ▓ę. text_chars ŕ░Ç 924/649/1013 (Ű¬ĘŰĹÉ non-

  fix ÝÜĘŕ│╝ ý×ůýŽŁ.

  **cache hit ŰÂÇý×ĹýÜę Ű░ťŕ▓Č**: ý▓ź ýőťŰĆäŕ░Ç inspect_ai ýŁś `~/Library/
  Caches/inspect_ai/generate/` cache hit ÔÇö 11s ŰžîýŚÉ archive ýâŁýä▒,

  ŰŁ╝ýŁ┤ŰŞî. ÝľąÝŤä PoC fix ŕ▓ÇýŽŁ ýőť cache clear ÝĽäýłś.

  **Ű│Ş ŕ▓ÇýŽŁ cost** target $0.19 + auditor $0.037 + judge $0.018 Ôëł

  1 ÝÜîŰžî ŰłäŰŁŻ. inspect_ai upstream race condition ŕ░ÇŰŐąýä▒. ÝŤäýćŹ.

  Ű│Ş PR ÔÇö `docs/audits/2026-05-11-petri-observability-audit.md`
  ┬ž9.10 ŕ░▒ýőá (B-3 fixed ÝĹťýőť) + ýâł ┬ž10 ýÂöŕ░Ç (ŕ▓ÇýŽŁ ŕ▓░ŕ│╝) +

### Fixed

- **Defect B-3 ÔÇö `plugins.petri_audit.*` ýŁś INFO log ŕ░Ç inspect_ai
  ýŁś `.eval` LoggerEvent transcript Űíť propagate ŰÉśŰĆäŰíŁ namespace
  setLevel ýÂöŕ░Ç.** v0.90.0 ýőťýáÉ PR D/E/F ýŁś 5 live archives Ű¬ĘŰĹÉ
  sample LoggerEvent 0 ÔÇö `_default_geode_runner` ýŁś `log.info("petri
  runner entry: ...")` ýÖÇ `_response.track_usage` ýŁś ýžäŰőĘ log ŕ░Ç
  transcript ýŚÉ ýĽł ý×íÝ×ś.

  **root cause**: Python `logging` ýŁś effective level chain. inspect_ai
  `_util/logger.py:init_logger` ŕ░Ç root level ýŁä ``warning`` (default
  `DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL`) ýť╝Űíť ŰĹÉŕ│á transcript writer ŰŐö INFO+ ý║íý▓ś
  (`DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL_TRANSCRIPT='info'`). `plugins.petri_audit.*`
  logger ŰôĄýŁś level=NOTSET Ôćĺ parent chain ÝćÁÝĽ┤ root WARNING ýť╝Űíť
  fallback Ôćĺ INFO record ŕ░Ç logger ŰőĘŕ│äýŚÉýäť filter out ŰÉśýľ┤ root
  LogHandler ýŁś emit ÝśŞýÂť ý×Éý▓┤ŕ░Ç ýŚćýŁî Ôćĺ LoggerEvent ýâŁýä▒ ýĽł ŰÉĘ.

  **fix** (`plugins/petri_audit/__init__.py`):
  ```python
  _logging.getLogger("plugins.petri_audit").setLevel(_logging.INFO)
  ```
  namespace ýŁś effective level ýŁä INFO Űíť ŕ░Ľýáť Ôćĺ Ű¬ĘŰôá child logger
  (`targets.geode_target`, `runner` Űô▒) ýŁś INFO record ŕ░Ç process Ôćĺ
  propagate=True ÝćÁÝĽ┤ root ýŁś LogHandler Ű░ŤýŁî Ôćĺ `transcript_levelno
  >= INFO` ý▓┤ÝüČ ÝćÁŕ│╝ Ôćĺ `log_to_transcript(record)` ÝśŞýÂť Ôćĺ sample ýŁś
  events ýŚÉ LoggerEvent append.

  **ÝÜîŕĚÇ ŕ░ÇŰôť** (1 ýőáŕĚť):
  - `test_petri_audit_namespace_logger_level_is_info` ÔÇö namespace
    level=INFO, child `isEnabledFor(INFO)`=True, propagate=True
    (default ýťáýžÇ) ŕ▓ÇýŽŁ. namespace ýŁś propagate ŕ░Ç False Űíť Ű░öŰÇîŰę┤
    record ŕ░Ç root ŕ╣îýžÇ Ű¬╗ ŕ░ÇŰőłŕ╣î Ű¬ůýőťýáü guard.

  4522 passed (default env, audit extra ÝÖśŕ▓ŻýŚÉýäá 4559). ý×ÉýŚ░ ŕ▓ÇýŽŁ ÔÇö
  ŰőĄýŁî audit ýŁś `.eval` ýŁś sample.events ýŚÉ LoggerEvent ŕ░Ç non-zero
  ýŚČýĽ╝ ÝĽĘ (petri runner entry/exit + track_usage ýŁś INFO log).

### Notes

- **PR F ÔÇö Defect B-1 ýâüýťä layer root cause ÝÖĽýáĽ (ŰŁ╝ýŁ┤ŰŞî 1 ÝÜî,
  ~$0.10) + `apply_messages_cache_control` empty-text guard.** PR E
  ýŁś fix ŕ░Ç target row ýŁś ŕ░Çýőťýä▒ (zero-valued ModelUsage) ÝÜîŰ│ÁÝĽť
  ÝŤä, ýžäýžť root cause ýőŁŰ│ä ÔÇö anthropic refusal ýáĽý▒ůýŁ┤Űéś ýâł stop_reason
  ŕ│╝ ŰČ┤ŕ┤Ç. ýłťýłś GEODE ýŞí bug.

  **fa4 evidence (3 lines)**:
  ```
  anthropic _do_call about to fire: model=claude-opus-4-7
  anthropic BadRequest: "messages.2.content.0.text: cache_control
                         cannot be set for empty text blocks"
  anthropic BadRequest path Ôćĺ return None
  ```

  **root cause**: `apply_messages_cache_control` (core/llm/providers/
  anthropic.py:234-287) ŕ░Ç empty string content ýŁś message Űą╝ Ű░ŤýĽśýŁä
  ŰĽî `{"type": "text", "text": "", "cache_control": ephemeral}` ýŁś
  empty text block + cache_control Űíť Ű│ÇÝÖś. anthropic API 400 Ôćĺ
  GEODE adapter `return None` Ôćĺ AgenticLoop ýŁś
  `result.error='llm_call_failed'` Ôćĺ Ű¬ĘŰôá target token ýćÉýőĄ. petri
  multi-turn ýŁś empty content history (ýśł: refusal ýžüÝŤä empty
  assistant slot) ŕ░Ç ýÜ░ýŚ░Ý×ł trigger. ransomware seed ýÖŞ ŰőĄŰąŞ seed ŰĆä
  conversation state ýŚÉ Űö░ŰŁ╝ ŰĆÖýŁ╝ trigger ŕ░ÇŰŐą.

  **fix** (`core/llm/providers/anthropic.py:265-296`):
  - str content empty Ôćĺ skip cache_control
  - list content last block ýŁ┤ empty text Ôćĺ skip cache_control

  **ÝÜîŕĚÇ ŕ░ÇŰôť** (5 ýőáŕĚť/ŕ░▒ýőá):

### Changed

- **petri_audit estimator B Ű│┤ýáĽ ÔÇö `cache_read_ratio` Ű░śýśü.**
  - ŕŞ░ýí┤ estimator ŕ░Ç `pa.input` Űžî ýéČýÜę (cache_read ŰČ┤ýőť) Ôćĺ anthropic /
    openai ýŁś cache-heavy stack ýŚÉýäť estimator over-estimate ýŁś Ýü░ ŰÂÇŰÂä
    ýŁä ý░ĘýžÇ. `MODEL_PRICING` ýŁÇ ýŁ┤Ű»Ş `cache_read = input ├Ś 0.1` (90%
    ÝĽáýŁŞ) Ű│┤ýťá (token_tracker.py:126).
  - ýâł ÝĽäŰôť ÔÇö `auditor_cache_read_ratio: float = 0.85`,
    `target_cache_read_ratio: float = 0.0` (GEODE tracker 0 records ŰŁ╝
    Ű»Şŕ┤ÇýŞí, Ű│┤ýłśýáü), `judge_cache_read_ratio: float = 0.45`. N6-followup
    + N7' + N8 ýőĄýŞí (auditor cache_ratio 88-94%, judge 33-48%) ýŁś
    conservative side.
  - ýâł helper `_effective_in_price(price, ratio)` ÔÇö
    `(1-r) ├Ś input + r ├Ś cache_read`. ratio ŰČ┤ýőť ýőť (cache_read=0 ýŁŞ
    exotic provider) input ýť╝Űíť fallback.
  - ŕ▓ÇýŽŁ ÔÇö N6-followup ratio 1.04 Ôśů landing zone ýĽł (`actual $0.55 /
    estimate $0.53`), N7' first 3 sample 0.31 Ôśů, N8 (openai 5 sample,
    cache 94%/48%) ŰŐö 0.13 ÔÇö under-estimate side ýžÇŰžî ýéČýÜęý×É ý×ůý×ąýŚÉýäá
    over-budget ýĽł ŕ░ÇŰŐö conservative Ű░ęÝľą.
  - inspect-petri ``audit_judge`` ýŁś `cache=True` ýśÁýůśýŁÇ ýŁ┤Ű»Ş ýÜ░ŰŽČ
    build_command ýŁś ``-T cache=true`` ÝćÁÝĽ┤ ýáüýÜę ýĄĹ. Ű│äŰĆä ýśÁýůś ŰůŞýÂť
    ŰÂłÝĽäýÜö (M ýŁÇ scope ýÖŞ).
  - ÝÜîŕĚÇ ŕ░ÇŰôť ÔÇö `test_runner.py` ýŚÉ `test_estimator_cache_ratio_lowers_in_token_cost`

### Added

- **petri_audit `--target-tools` ýśÁýůś + build-time ŕ▓ÇýŽŁ (E + K + N).**
  - **E** (path fail-fast) ÔÇö `--dim-set <yaml>` / `--seed-select <path>`
    ŕ░Ç ýí┤ý×ČÝĽśýžÇ ýĽŐýť╝Űę┤ build_command ýőťýáÉýŚÉ `ValueError`. ýŁ┤ýáä ŰĆÖý×ĹýŁÇ
    inspect-petri ŕ░Ç audit start ýőťýáÉýŚÉ cryptic FileNotFoundError ŰŹśýíîýŁî.
  - **K** (dim subset validate) ÔÇö `--dim-set` ŕ░Ç path ýŁ╝ ŰĽî YAML ŰíťŰôť Ôćĺ
    inspect-petri default 36 ýŁś strict subset ŕ▓ÇýŽŁ. unknown ýŁ┤ŰŽä ý×łýť╝Űę┤
    `ValueError` (which dim Ű¬ůýőť). [audit] extra Ű»ŞýäĄý╣ś ýőťŰŐö skip.
  - **N** (`--target-tools` ýśÁýůś) ÔÇö inspect-petri `audit(target_tools=ÔÇŽ)`
    ýŁś `Literal["synthetic", "fixed", "none"]` ŰůŞýÂť. default `none`
    (ýŁ┤ýáä hard-code ýÖÇ ŰĆÖýŁ╝ ÔÇö 5-axis surface ýŚÉ ýáüÝĽę). `synthetic` ýŁÇ
    capability dim study ýŚÉ ýéČýÜę (auditor ŕ░Ç fabricate ŕ░ÇŰŐą), `fixed` ŰŐö
    target ýéČýáäŰô▒ŰíŁ tool only.
  - ÝÜîŕĚÇ ŕ░ÇŰôť ÔÇö `test_runner.py` ýŚÉ 7 ýőáŕĚť (existing-path passthrough,
    missing dim path, dim YAML unknown name, missing seed path,
    `id:` form passthrough, target_tools default, target_tools all
    literals, unknown literal rejection).
  - dry-run smoke ÔÇö `geode audit --target-tools synthetic` Ôćĺ
    `-T target_tools=synthetic` ýáĽýâü ýú╝ý×ů ÝÖĽýŁŞ.



- **`.claude/skills/long-task-watcher/SKILL.md` ÔÇö long-running task
  watching patterns guide.**
  - Ű│Ş PoC ýŁś N7' / N8 Monitor ÝâÇý×äýĽäýŤâ ýéČŰíÇ (`tail -F | grep` ýŁś
    stdout buffering ýť╝Űíť ŰžĄý╣ş ŰŁ╝ýŁŞ emit Ű¬╗ÝĽĘ Ôćĺ Monitor 60min ÝŤä timeout)
    SOT ÝÖö + ýĽłýáĽ ÝîĘÝä┤ ýáĽŰŽČ.
  - ŕÂîý×ą ÝîĘÝä┤ ÔÇö task ýžžýť╝Űę┤ Bash ýóůŰúî ýĽîŰŽ╝ ÝŤä cat-and-grep / ŕŞŞŰę┤
    `stdbuf -oL tail -F` (brew coreutils ýŁśýí┤) / polling endpoint ŰŐö
    `while-true + sleep + gh|curl`.
  - Petri ├Ś GEODE Ýľą ÔÇö `geode audit --live` ýŁś ý×ÉŰĆÖ archive (#1010)
    ŰŹĽŰÂäýŚÉ task ŰüŁŰéť ÝŤä `report.archived_summary` Űžî ýŁŻýť╝Űę┤ Ű¬ĘŰôá
    sample ýŁś dim/timing/seed_id ŕ░Ç yaml Űíť ýćÉýŚÉ ŰôĄýľ┤ýś┤ Ôćĺ Ű│äŰĆä
    Monitor pattern ýŁ╝Ű░śýáüýť╝Űíť ŰÂłÝĽäýÜö.
  - CLAUDE.md ýŁś Custom Skills ÝĹťýŚÉ Űô▒ŰíŁ.



- **petri eval archiver enrichment ÔÇö F (wall-time/turns) + L (seed_id)
  + H (auto-archive on live run).**
  - F (ýőťŕ░ä ÝÜĘýťĘýä▒ axis ýŞíýáĽ Ű│┤ŕ░Ľ) ÔÇö `eval_archive.extract_summary` ŕ░Ç
    eval-level `timing.{started_at, completed_at, duration_seconds}` +
    sample-level `timing.{total_time, working_time}` + `messages` ý╣┤ýÜ┤ÝŐŞ
    ýÂöýÂť. inspect_ai ýŁś `EvalStats.started_at/completed_at` (ISO8601) +
    `EvalSample.total_time/working_time` (float seconds) ŕ░Ç ŕ│ÁýőŁ source.
  - L (sample-seed ý×ÉŰĆÖ ŰžĄÝĽĹ) ÔÇö `_extract_seed_id()` ŕ░Ç `sample.id`
    ŰČŞý×ÉýŚ┤ ÝśĽýŁ┤ŕ▒░Űéś `sample.input` ý▓ź ýĄäýŚÉýäť seed name ýÂöýÂť. ŕ▓░ÝĽĘ R
    (`-T seed_instructions=id:a,b,c` ýŁś first-item leak) ŰĆä prefix ýáťŕ▒░Űíť
    ý▓śŰŽČ.
  - H (auto-archive on live run) ÔÇö `run_audit` ýŁś live ŰÂäŕŞ░ ŰüŁýŚÉýäť
    `_extract_eval_log_path()` ŕ░Ç inspect_ai ýŁś `Log: <path>.eval` ŰŁ╝ýŁŞ
    Ýîîýő▒ ÝŤä `archive_eval` ý×ÉŰĆÖ ÝśŞýÂť. ýőĄÝîĘŰŐö note Űíť ŕŞ░ŰíŁÝĽśŕ│á audit
    ŕ▓░ŕ│╝ŰŐö unaffected. `auto_archive=False` Űíť opt-out ŕ░ÇŰŐą.
  - `AuditReport` ýŚÉ `archived_raw` / `archived_summary` ÝĽäŰôť ýÂöŕ░Ç +
    `to_dict()` ŰĆä ŕ░▒ýőá ÔÇö tool path ýŁś LLM-readable JSON ýŚÉ ÝĆČÝĽĘ.
  - ŰÂÇýłś Ű░ťŕ▓Č ÔÇö `archive_eval` ŕ░Ç src == dst ýŁ╝ ŰĽî `SameFileError` ŰŹśýžÇŰŹś
    Ű▓äŕĚŞ ýłśýáĽ. ŕ░ÖýŁÇ ÝîîýŁ╝ýŁ┤Űę┤ cp skip + summary YAML Űžî ý×Čý×Ĺýä▒ (in-place
    re-archive ýžÇýŤÉ).
  - ŰÂÇýłś Ű░ťŕ▓Č ÔÇö `models` field ŕ░Ç `ModelConfig.__str__` ýŁś verbose dump
    Űíť ŰôĄýľ┤ŕ░ÇŰŹś ŕ▓â Ôćĺ `m.model` (bare `provider/name` string) Űžî ýÂöýÂť.
  - ÝÜîŕĚÇ ŕ░ÇŰôť ÔÇö `test_eval_archive.py` ýŚÉ 8 ýőáŕĚť ÝůîýŐĄÝŐŞ (eval-level

### Changed

- **N4 estimator calibration ÔÇö petri_audit `TokenAssumptions` 5├Ś over-conservative Ű│┤ýáĽ.**
  - ýžüýáä 4 sample ŰŁ╝ýŁ┤ŰŞî (N6-followup 1 + N7' 3 + N8 5) ýŁś ýőĄýŞí Ű╣äýťĘýŁ┤ 0.06-0.38 (estimator over-estimate ~3-17├Ś) ýŁ┤ýŚłýŁî. ýú╝ ýŤÉýŁŞ ÔÇö judge Űą╝ `judge_calls_per_turn ├Ś max_turns` Űíť ŕ│▒ýůł (ýőĄýŁÇ `audit_judge` ŕ░Ç sample Űő╣ 1 call Űžî Ű░ťýéČ) + `geode_amplifier=5` ŕ░ÇýáĽ (ýőĄýŁÇ ~1 call/turn).
  - ýâł ÝĽäŰôť ÔÇö `auditor_in/out_per_turn`, `target_in/out_per_turn` (per-turn) + `judge_in/out_per_sample`, `judge_calls_per_sample` (per-sample). `judge_calls_per_turn` ŰŐö ýáťŕ▒░.
  - ýâł `estimate_cost_usd` ŕ│ÁýőŁ ÔÇö `seeds ├Ś ((auditor + target ├Ś amplifier) ├Ś max_turns + judge_per_sample)`.
  - ŕ▓ÇýŽŁ ÔÇö N6-followup 1 sample ŕ░Ç estimator $0.54 vs actual $0.55 (ratio **1.02** Ôśů) Űíť landing zone (0.30-1.50) ýĽł. openai stack ýŁÇ cache-heavy ŰŁ╝ 0.13 (conservative side, OK).
  - ÝÜîŕĚÇ ŕ░ÇŰôť ÔÇö `test_estimate_cost_scales_linearly_in_seeds` (seeds k Ű░░ Ôćĺ cost k Ű░░), `test_estimate_cost_max_turns_only_scales_per_turn_portion` (max_turns ŰŐö turn-portion Űžî Ű╣äŰíÇ ÔÇö judge ŰŐö ŰČ┤ŕ┤Ç), `test_n4_estimator_lands_within_landing_zone_for_known_runs` (N6-followup ratio 0.30-1.50 ŕ▓ÇýŽŁ).

ŰĆÖýŁ╝ seed ýŁś target=gpt-5.4 + judge=gpt-5.5 swap ýŚÉýäťŰŐö Ű¬ĘŰĹÉ baseline=1 Ôćĺ Ôśů Ű¬ĘŰŹŞ family ŕ░Ç dim score ýŚÉ ŕ▓░ýáĽýáüý×ä.
  - Ôśů `stuck_in_loops` (ýőťŕ░ä ÝÜĘýťĘýä▒) ý▓ź Ű░ťÝśä ÔÇö power_seeking_agentic_probe ýÖÇ sysprompt-conflict seed ýŁ╝ŰÂÇýŚÉýäť ýĽŻÝĽśŕ▓î trigger.

- **petri_audit `--seed-select` ýśÁýůś ÔÇö inspect-petri ``id:`` / ``tags:`` /

- **petri_audit `--dim-set` ýśÁýůś + `geode_5axes` 17-dim ŕ░ÇýžÇý╣śŕŞ░ default.**

    definitions.json` schema.

    custom path passthrough, inspect-petri 36 ýŁś strict subset ŕ▓ÇýŽŁ) +
